questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 17) ما حكم تقبيل اليد في الإسلام؟
الجواب: إن تقبيلَ يدِ العلماءِ والصالحينَ والأبوينِ جائزٌ شرعاً، بل هو مظهرٌ من مظاهرِ الآدابِ الإسلاميةِ في احترامِ أهلِ الفضلِ والتقى، وإليكَ بعضُ النصوصِ الصريحةِ في ذلك:
1- أما ما ورد من الأحاديث:

فعَن "صفوان بن عسال" قال: (قال يهودِي لصاحبِه: قُم بنا إلى هذا النَّبي، فأتَيا رسولَ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، فسألا عن تِسع آياتٍ بيّنات، فذكرَ الحَديث... إلى قولِه: قبَّلا يدهِ ورجلهِ وقالا: نشهدُ أنكَ نبيُّ الله). ⁽¹⁾

وروى "أبو داود" عن "أمِّ أبان بنت الوازع بن زارع" عن جدها "زارع" وكان في وفدِ "عبد القيس"، قال: (فجعَلنا نتبادرُ من رواحلِنا فنقبِّل يدَ رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه ورجلِه). وكذلك في " السيرة الشامية" للبيهقي: (ثم جاءَ "الأشج" حتى أخذَ بيدِ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه فقبَّلها، وهو سيد الوفد). وفي "شرح البخاري" لـ "الحافظ ابن حجر العسقلاني" (أن "أبا لبابة" و "كعب بن مالك" وصاحِبيه، قبَّلوا يدَ النبِي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه حين تابَ اللهُ عليهِم).
2- وأما ما ورد من الآثار:

فقد أخرجَ "الطبراني" و "البيهقي" و "الحاكم" عن "الشعبي": (أن "زيد بن ثابت" صلَّى على جنازةٍ فَقُرِّبتْ إليهِ بغلتهُ ليركبَها، فجاءَ "عبد الله بن عباس" رضوان الله عليهما، فأخذَ بركابهِ، فقال "زيد بن ثابت": خلِّ عَنها يا ابنَ عمِّ رسولِ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه. فقال "ابن عباس": هكذا أُمِرْنا أن نفعلَ بالعلماءِ والكبَراء، فقبَّل "زيد بن ثابت" يدَ "عبد الله" وقال: هكذا أمِرنا أن نفعلَ بأهلِ بيتِ رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه.

وأخرجَ "البخاري" في "الأدبِ المفرَد" من رواية "عبد الرحمن بن رزين" قال: (أخرجَ لنا "سلمة بن الأكوع" كفاً له ضخمةً كأنَّها كفُّ بعيرٍ فقُمنا إِليها فقبَّلناها). وعن "ثابت" (أنه قبَّل يد "أنس"). وأخرجَ أيضاً: (أن "علياً" قبَّل يدَ "العباس" ورِجله). وأخرجَ من طريقِ "أبي مالك الأشجعي" (قلت لـ "ابن أبي أوفى": ناوِلني يدكَ التي بايعتَ بها رسولَ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، فناولَنيها، فقبَّلتُها).⁽²⁾

وقال "ابن كثير" في تاريخهِ "البداية والنهاية" في فتحِ بيتِ المقدسِ على يدِ "عمرَ بن الخطّاب" رضوان الله عليه بعدَ كلام... (فلما وصلَ "عمرُ بن الخطابِ" إلى الشّام تلقَّاه "أبو عبيدةَ" ورؤوس الأمَراء، كـ "خالدِ بن الوليد"ِ و "يزيد بن أبي سُفيان"، فترجَّل "أبو عبيدةَ" وترجَّل "عُمر"، فأشارَ "أبو عبيدة" ليقبِّل يدَ "عُمر"، فهمَّ "عُمر" بتقبيلِ رِجل "أبي عُبيدة" فكفَّ "أبو عبيدة"، فكفَّ "عُمر".
وفي “غذاءِ الألبابِ لـ "الحنبلي": (وفي الآدابِ الكبرى: وتُباح المعانقةُ وتقبيلُ اليدِ والرأسِ تديّناً وتكرّماً واحتراماً، مع أمنِ الشّهوة).

وقال "الحافظ ابن الجوزي" في "مناقب أصحابِ الحديث": (ينبَغي للطالبِ أن يبالغَ في التواضعِ للعالمِ ويذَلُّ لَه، قال: ومِن التواضعِ تقبيلِ يده. وَقبَّل "سفيانُ بن عيينة" و "الفضيلُ بن عياض" أحدُهما يدَ "الحسينِ بن علي الجعفي" والآخرُ رِجْلَه).⁽³⁾

وقال "أبو المَعالي" في "شرح الهداية": (أما تقبيلُ يدِ العالمِ والكريمِ لفضلهِ فجائِز، وأما أن تُقَبَّلَ يدُه لِغناه، فقد روي: (من تواضعَ لغنيٍّ لِغناه فقد ذهبَ ثُلثا دينِه). وقد علمتُ أن الصحابةَ قَبَّلوا يدَ المُصطفى صلوات الله وسلاه عليه، كما في حديثِ "ابن عمر" رضوان الله عليهما عندَ قدومهم من غزوةِ مؤتَة.
أقوال الأئمة الأربعة في مسألة تقبيل اليد:
الحنفية:

قال العلاَّمة "ابن عابدين" في حاشيَته، عندَ كلامِ صاحب "الدُّر المُختار": (ولا بأسَ بتقبيلِ يدِ الرجلِ العالمِ والمتورِّع على سبيلِ التبرُّك)، وقيل: (سنَّة). قال "الشرنبلالي": (وعلمتُ أن مفادَ الأحاديث سنيَّته أو ندبه كما أشار إليهِ "العيني"). وفي حاشِية "الطحطاوي على مراقي الفلاح"، قال: (وفي غايةِ البيانِ عن الواقعات: تقبيلُ يدِ العالمِ أو السلطانِ العادلِ جائِز. ووردَ في أحاديثَ ذكرَها البدرُ العيني... ثم قال: فعُلِمَ مِن مجموعِ ما ذكَرنا إباحةُ تقبيلِ اليد...).
المالكية:
قال الإمام "مالك": (إن كانَت - قُبلة اليدِ - على وجهِ التكبُّر والتعظيمِ فمكروهةَ، وإن كانَت على وجهِ القربةِ إلى اللهِ لدينهِ أو لعلمهِ أو لشرفهِ فإن ذلك جائِز).⁽⁴⁾
الشافعية:

قال الإمام "النووي": (تقبيُل يدِ الرجلِ لزهدهِ وصلاحهِ وعلمهِ وشرفهِ، أو نحوَ ذلكَ من الأمورِ الدينيّة، لا يُكره بل يُستحب، فإن كانَ لغناهُ أو شوكتهِ أو جاههِ عند أهلِ الدنيا فمكروهٌ شديُد الكراهِة).⁽⁵⁾
الحنبلية:

وفي "غذاءِ الألباب” شرح منظومة الآداب" للعلامة "السفاريني الحنبلي" قال: (قال المرزوي: سألتُ :"أبا عبد الله - الإمام أحمد بن حنبل" - رحمه الله تَعالى عن قُبلةِ اليد، فقال: إن كانَ على طريقِ التديُّن فلا بأس، قَبَّلَ "أبو عبيدة" يدَ "عُمر بن الخطاب" رضوان الله عليهما) وإن كانَ على طريقِ الدنيا فَلا).
وأحسن ما قيل في تقبيل اليد قول "ابن شرف الحكيم":
كأنيَ إذ أوالي لثم راحتِه   عجزتُ عن شكرهِ حتى سددتُ فَمي
وقال آخر في ذلك:
قبل يد الخِيَرَةِ أهلُ التقى  ولا تخَف طعنَ أعادِيهم
ريحانةُ الرحمن عُبَّاده  وشـــمــــها لــــثــــم أيــــــاديـهم
أ- نصوص السادة الشافعية:
نقلَ العَلامة الفَقيه "محمد الشربيني" في "المغني المحتاج": (ويُسَنُّ القيامُ لأهلِ الفضلِ من علمٍ وصلاحٍ أو شرفٍ أو نحو ذلك لا رياءً وتفخيماً. قال في الروضة: وقد ثبت فيه أحاديثَ صحيحة).
وللإمام "النووي" رسالةٌ خاصةٌ سمَّاها "رسالة الترخيص بالقيام لذوي الفضل" في جوازِ القيامِ للقادِم، واستدلَّ على ذلكَ بأحاديث كَثيرة مِنها:

1- أخرجَ "أبو داود" في سننِه: (أن النبيَّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه كانَ جالساً يوماً، فأقبل أبوهُ من الرضاعةِ فوضعَ له بعضَ ثوبهِ فجلسَ عَليه، ثم أقبَلت أمُّه من الرضاعةِ فوضعَ لها شقَّ ثوبهِ منَ الجانبِ الآخر، ثم أقبلَ أخوهُ من الرضاعةِ فقامَ فأجلسهُ بينَ يديهِ).

2- وأخرجَ الإمام "مالك" في قصةِ "عكرمة بن أبي جهل" رضوان الله تعالى عليه، لمَّا فرَّ إلى اليمنِ يوم الفتحِ ورحلَت امرأتهُ إليهِ حتى أعادتهُ إلى مكةَ مسلماً: (فلما رآهُ النبيُّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه وثبَ إليه فرِحاً ورمى عليهِ رداءَه.
3- وقام النبيُّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه لما قدمَ "جعفر" من الحبَشة فقال: "ما أدري بأيِّهما أنا أُسَرُّ بقدومِ جعفرَ أو بفتحِ خَيبر".
4- وجاءَ بحديثِ "عائشة" رضوان الله عليها: (قدِم "زيد بن حارثة" المدينةَ والنبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه في بَيتي، فقرعَ البابَ فقامَ إليه فاعتنقهُ وقبَّله.
5- وأخرجَ "أبو داود" عن "أبي هريرة" رضوان الله عليهم قال: (كان النبيّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه حدِّثنا فإذا قامَ قمنا إليهِ حتى نراهُ قد دخَل.
ب- نصوص السادة الحنفية:

نقلَ العلامةُ الفقيهُ المحقق "ابن عابدين" عند قولِ "صاحب الدُّر": وفي الوهبانيةِ يجوز، بل يندبُ القيامَ تعظيماً للقادِم، كما يجوزُ القيامُ ولو للقارئ بين يَدي العالِم: (قال في "النية": قيامُ الجالسِ في المسجدِ لمَن دخلَ عليه تعظيماً وقيامُ قارئِ القرآنِ لمن يجيءُ تعظيماً لا يُكره، إذا كانَ ممَّن يستحقُ التَّعظيم. وفي "مشكل الآثار": القيامُ لغيرهِ ليسَ بمكروهٍ لعينِه، إنما المكروهُ محبةَ القيامِ لمن يُقام لَه، فإن قامَ لِمن لا يقامُ له لا يُكره. قال "ابن وهبان" أقول: وفي عصرِنا يَنبغي أن يُستحبَّ ذلك - أي القِيام- لما يورث تركهُ من الحقدِ والبغضاءِ والعداوة، ولاسيما إذا كانَ في مكانٍ اعتقد فيهِ القيام. وما ورد من التوعُّد عليه، في حقِّ من يحبُّ القيامَ بين يديهِ كما يفعلهُ التُّرك والأعاجِم.
ج- نصوص شراح الحديث:

قال "أبو سليمان الخطابي الشافعي" شارحاً الحديث الذي رواهُ "أبو داود" عن "أبي سعيد الخدري" رضي الله عنه: (أن أهل قُرَيْظَةَ لمَّا نزلوا على حُكم "سعد" أرسل إليهِ النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، فجاءَ على حمارٍ أقرم، فقال النبيّ صلوات الله و سلامه عليه: "قوموا إلى سيِّدكم أو إلى خيرِكم"، فجاءَ حتى قعدَ إلى رسول صلوات الله وسلامه عليه.

قال "الخطابي": (فيهِ من العلمِ أن قولَ الرجل لصاحبِه: يا سيدي، غيرُ محظورٍ إذا كان صاحبهُ خيِّراً فاضلاً، وإنما جاءتِ الكراهةُ في تسويدِ الرجلِ الفاجِر، وفيهِ أن قيامَ المرؤوسِ للرئيسِ الفاضلِ وللوليِ العادِل، وقيامُ المتعلمِ للعالمِ مستحبٌ غيرُ مَكروه، وإنما جاءتِ الكراهةُ فيمَن كانَ بخلافِ أهلِ هذه الصِّفات.

وقال العلامة "السفاريني": (وفي مسندِ الإمام أحمد رضوان الله عليه "قوموا إلى سيِّدكم فأنزِلوه" لكن يَنْصُرُ كونَ الأمرِ بالقيام له آخرُ الخبَرِ: وكان رجالٌ من بني الأشهلِ يقولون: قُمنا لهُ عَلى أرجُلنا صفَّين يحييهِ كلُّ رجلٍ منا حتى انتَهى إلى رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه... كما في "السيرة الشامية". وقد أوردَ هذا الخبرُ العلامة "علي بن برهان الدين الحلبي" في كتابه "السيرة الحلبية"، ج2/ص339. في بحث غزوة بني قريظة.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 17: What is the ruling in Islam for kissing hands and standing up for righteous people?

Answer: It is permissible in the Sacred Law (Shariah) to kiss the hands of scholars, righteous individuals, and one’s parents. It is in fact one of the many manifestations of Islamic manners with respect to the people of virtue and piety. Consider these explicit texts regarding the matter.
Hadith Reports

Safwan b. ‘Assal reported, “A Jew once said to his companion, ‘Come, let us go to the Prophet!’ They went to the Messenger of Allah  and asked him about the nine clear signs (given to Prophet Musa). …” At the end of this hadith it states, “[S]o they both kissed the Prophet’s hand and foot and said, ‘We bear witness that you are the Prophet of Allah!’” (Narrated by Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi (who authenticated it), and al-Nasa’i and others.)

Abu Dawud narrated from Umm Aban b. al-Wazi‘ b. Zari‘, from her grandfather Zari‘ who was present in the delegation of ‘Abd al-Qays. He said, “We hastened off of our riding mounts and kissed the hand and foot of the Messenger of Allah .” al-Bayhaqi also narrated this report, as found in al-Sira al-Shamiyya, and in it there contains the addition: “…then Mundhir al-Ash‘ajj, the leader of the delegation, came and took the hand of the Messenger of Allah  and kissed it…”

In his commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari, al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar al-‘Asqalani states, “Abu Lubaba, Ka‘b b. Malik, and his two companions kissed the hand of the Prophet  when Allah turned to them in repentance.”
Narratives

Al-Tabarani, al-Bayhaqi, and al-Hakim narrated from al-Sha‘bi, “Once, Zayd b. Thabit prayed a funeral prayer and soon thereafter his mule was brought for him to mount. At that moment, ‘Abdullah b. ‘Abbas k came to him and grabbed his mule’s stirrups. Zayd b. Thabit said, ‘Leave it, O cousin of the Messenger of Allah !’ Ibn ‘Abbas said, ‘This is how we were ordered to treat our scholars and elders!’ whereupon Zayd kissed his hand and said, ‘And this is how we were ordered to behave with the Family of the Messenger of Allah !’”

Al-Bukhari narrated in al-Adab al-Mufrad from the report of ‘Abd al-Rah’man b. Razin who said, “Salama b. al-Akwa‘  extended his hand to us and it was a large-palmed hand like the hand of a camel, and we kissed it.”

It was reported from Thabit that he kissed the hand of Anas. Al-Bukhari also reported that ‘Ali kissed the hand and foot of al-‘Abbas. He also recorded from the route of Abu Malik al-Ashja‘i who said, “I said to Ibn Abi Awfa, ‘Give me your hand with which you pledged fealty to the Messenger of Allah !’ He gave me his hand and I kissed it.” (Ibn Hajar records the same in Fath al-Bari.)

Ibn Kathir said in his book of history, al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya (7:55), concerning ‘Umar b. al-Khattab’s t conquest of Jerusalem: “When ‘Umar b. al-Khattab arrived in the Levant, he was met by Abu ‘Ubayda and other leaders such as Khalid b. al-Walid and Yazid b. Abi Sufyan. Both ‘Umar and Abu ‘Ubayda dismounted, and Abu ‘Ubayda made a gesture to kiss ‘Umar’s hand. ‘Umar thought to kiss Abu ‘Ubayda’s foot, so both ‘Umar and Abu ‘Ubayda held back.”

In Ghidha’ al-Albab Sharh Manzumat al-Adab (Sustenance for the Intellects: A Commentary on the Rhyming Poem on Etiquette), the erudite Muhammad al-Safarini al-Hanbali said, “And in al-Adab al-Kubra it states, ‘It is permissible to hug and to kiss a person’s hand out of a show honor and respect, provided one is safe from base desires.’”

Al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi said in Manaqib Ashab al-Hadith (The Merits of the People of Hadith), “The student must see to it that he is extremely humble and subservient towards the scholar… [F]rom this humility is kissing his hand. Sufyan b. ‘Uyayna and al-Fudayl b. ‘Iyad kissed the hand and foot of al-Husayn b. ‘Ali al-Ja‘fi respectively [one kissed his hand and the other kissed his foot].” (Ghidha al-Albab: 1:287)

In Sharh al-Hidaya, Abu al-Ma‘ali said, “As for kissing the hand of a scholar or noble person, it is permissible. As for kissing someone’s hand due to his wealth, it has been narrated, ‘Whoso humbles himself to a wealthy person on account of his wealth has lost two thirds of his religion.’ You have come to know that the Companions kissed the hand of the Chosen One (al-Mustafa) , as is narrated in the Hadith of Ibn ‘Umar k, when they returned from the battle of al-Mu’ta.” (Ghidha al-Albab: 1:287)
The Positions of the Legal Schools on Kissing the Hand
The Hanafi Position

The erudite Ibn ‘Abidin commented in his Marginalia on al-Durr al-Mukhtar, “There is no problem with kissing the hand of a scholar or scrupulous person for the sake of seeking blessings. It has been said that it is a Sunna. Al-Shurunbali said, ‘You should know—as was stated by al-‘Ayni—that the hadith reports on this matter all indicate that it is either a Sunna or it is recommended.’”

In al-Tahtawi’s Marginalia on Maraqi al-Falah he said, “In Ghayat al-Bayan fi al-Waqi‘at it states, ‘Kissing the hand of a scholar or a just ruler is permissible.’ There are hadith mentioning this, cited by al-Badr al-‘Ayni… [S]o from all that we have mentioned, it is known that it is permissible to kiss the hand…”
The Maliki Position

Imam Malik said, “If kissing a man’s hand is done out of pride and arrogance then it is detestable. If, however, it is done due to his closeness to Allah or his religiosity, knowledge, or pure pedigree, then it is permissible.” (Ibn Hajar, Fath al-Bari)
The Shafi’i Position

Imam al-Nawawi said, “Kissing a man’s hand due to his asceticism, piety, knowledge, or pure pedigree, or any other religious reason, is not detestable; rather, it is recommended. But if it is due to his wealth, authority, or prestige with the people of the worldly-life, it is severely detestable.” (Ibn Hajar, Fath al-Bari:)
The Hanbali Position

In Ghidha al-Albab Sharh Manzumat al-Adab, the erudite al-Safarini al-Hanbali said, “al-Marrudhi said: ‘I asked Abu ‘Abdullah—Imam Ahmad, Allah have mercy upon him—about kissing hands. He said: ‘If it is for the sake of someone’s religiosity, there is no problem with it. Abu ‘Ubayda kissed the hand of ‘Umar b. al-Khattab. But if it is for the sake of the world, then it should not be done.’”
The best thing said about kissing hands was said by Ibn Sharaf al-Hakim:

When I turn to kiss his hand
It’s as if I’m unable to thank him until I close my lips together
Another poet said:

Kiss the hands of the elect people of piety Do not fear the insults of their enemies
The aromatic sweet basil of the Ever Merciful is found with His servants And to smell it one must kiss their hands!
The Legal Ruling on Standing for Scholars, Righteous Individuals, and One’s Parents
The Shafi’i School

The erudite jurist, Muhammad al-Shirbini mentioned in his work Mughni al-Muhtaj (3:135), “It is a Sunna to stand for the people of virtue who possess knowledge, piety, pure lineage, and the like; not out of ostentation or vainglorious pride. In al-Rawda, he [Imam al-Nawawi] states, ‘There are many authentic Hadith affirming this.”’

Imam al-Nawawi has a specific treatise on this topic that he called Treatise Concerning the Dispensation to Stand for People of Virtue, wherein he explained that it is permissible to stand for someone entering; and he proved it from many Hadith, including:

• In the Sunan of Abu Dawud it is recorded: “The Messenger of Allah  was sitting one day when his father from suckling (rada’a) came to him. The Messenger of Allah  put out part of his garment for him and he sat upon it. Then came his mother from suckling, and he put out part of his garment on the other side for her and she sat upon it. Then came his brother from suckling, whereupon the Messenger of Allah  got up and had him sit in front of him.”

• In the Muwatta’, Imam Malik recorded the story of ‘Ikrima b. Abi Jahl , who had fled to Yemen after the conquest of Mecca. His wife went after him and brought him back to Mecca as a Muslim. In the narration it states: “… When the Prophet  saw him, he jumped out of delight and tossed him his upper garment.”
• The Prophet  stood up when Ja’far arrived from Abyssinia. He  said, “I do not know which of the two makes me more joyful, Ja’far’s arrival or the conquest of Khaybar!”

• In a hadith from ‘A’isha  it states, “Zayd b. al-Haritha entered Medina whilst the Prophet  was in my home. He [Ja’far] tapped on the door, whereupon the Prophet stood up, embraced him and kissed him.”

• Abu Dawud recorded a narration from Abu Hurayra  who said, “The Prophet  would converse with us, and when he would stand up we would stand as well and remain standing until we saw him enter his home.”
The Hanafi School

The erudite jurist and hadith scholar Ibn ‘Abidin quoted the author of al-Durr al-Mukhtar who said “In the Wahbiyya it is said that it is permissible, nay encouraged, to stand out of respect for one who enters; and it is likewise permissible to stand up even for a reciter in the presence of a scholar.” After this quote, Ibn ‘Abidin added, “The author of al-Niyya stated, ‘It is not offensive for a person sitting in the mosque to stand out of reverence for one who enters, or for a reciter [of the Quran] to stand out of reverence for one who comes, provided it is for one who merits reverence. In Mushkil al-Athar [of Imam al-Tahawi] it states, “Standing for someone else is not offensive in and of itself; rather, what is offensive is for one to love that others stand for him. If someone stands for someone who is not [customarily] stood for it is not offensive.”’ Ibn Wahban stated, ‘I maintain that in our time it is fitting that it should be recommended to stand, since not standing engenders rancor, hatred, enmity—especially if standing is not done in a location where it is seen in a positive light. The divine threats that have been narrated about standing apply to one who loves that others should stand in his presence—as was the practice of the Turks and Byzantines.’”
Statements from the Commentators on Prophetic Hadith

In a hadith recorded by Abu Dawud on the authority of Abu Sa’id al-Khudri  it states, “When the people of Banu Qurayza settled on the judgment of Sa’d [ibn Mu’adh], the Prophet  sent for him. He came whilst riding on the back of a donkey stud, whereupon the Prophet  said, ‘Stand for your master’—or he said, ‘Stand for the elect among you’ and he approached until he sat before the Messenger of Allah .” Concerning this narration, Abu Sulayman al-Khattabi al-Shafi’i explained, “From the knowledge that is deduced from this is that there is no harm in one saying to his companion ‘my master’ (ya Sayyidi), provided his companion is good and virtuous. The offensiveness of saying ‘my master’ is only when it is said to a corrupt individual. From this hadith it is also deduced that it is recommended and not offensive for subjects to stand for a virtuous leader and just ruler, and for a learner to stand for a scholar. The offensiveness of such an act has only come with regards to one who is unlike those of the aforementioned qualities.”

The erudite al-Safarini stated, “In the Musnad of Imam Ahmad  it is recorded, ‘Stand for your master,’ whereupon they went and lifted him down off his donkey…’ However, the end of the narration lends strength to the command to stand. In al-Sira al-Shamiyya it is recorded, ‘The men of Banu al-Ash-hal said, “We stood for him on our feet, arranged in two rows; each of us greeted him [as he walked by] until he reached the Messenger of Allah …”’ This narration was cited by the erudite ‘Ali b. Burhan al-Din Halabi in the second volume of his al-Sira al-Halabiyya, in the section on the Battle of Banu Qurayza.”

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 18) ما موقف السادة الصوفية من العلم؟
الجواب: إن المريدَ في ابتداء سيرهِ لا بد لهُ من علمِ العقائدِ وتصحيحِ العِبادات واستقامةِ المعاملات.. وفي أثناءِ سلوكهِ لا يستغنِي عن علمِ أحوالِ القلب وحسنُ الأخلاقِ وتزكيةِ النفسِ.
ولهذا اعتُبِرَ اكتسابُ العلمِ الضروري مِن أهم النِّقاط الأساسيةِ في المنهجِ العمليّ للتصوُّف، إذ ليس التصوّف إلا التّطبيق العمليّ للإسلامِ كاملاً غيرَ منقوصٍ في جميعِ جوانبهِ الظاهرةِ والباطِنة.
ينقسم العلم من حيث حكمه الشرعي إلى ثلاثة أقسام:
1- مأمور به.
2- منهي عنه.
3- مندوب إليه.
أ- العلوم المأمور بها، وهي صنفان:

الصنف الأول: فرضٌ عَين، وهوَ ما لا يسقطُ عن المكلَّف إلا بقيامهِ بهِ بِنفسه. ولا بدَّ قبلَ تعدادِ العلومِ المفروضةِ على المكلَّف فرض عينٍ من أنْ نثبتَ بعضَ القواعدِ الأساسيةِ في هذا الموضوعِ منها قاعدة: (ما لا يتمّ الواجبُ إلا بهِ فهوَ واجب). ومنها قاعدة: (العلمُ تابعٌ للمَعلوم). فالعلمُ الذي يُتوصل بهِ إلى إقامةِ الفرضِ يكون فرضاً، والعلمُ الذي يتوصلُ به إلى إقامةِ الواجبِ يكونُ واجباً، والعلمُ الذي يُتوصلُ به إلى إقامةِ السنَّة يكونُ سنّة.
وبناء على هذه القواعد نعدد بعض العلوم المفروضة فرض عين على كل مكلف:
1- علمُ عقيدةِ أهلُ السنةِ والجماعةِ مع الاستدلالِ الإجماليّ على كلِّ مسألةٍ من مسائلِ الإيمانيَّات، للخروجِ من ربقةِ التَّقليد، وللحفاظِ على إيمانهِ أمامَ تشكيكِ الملحدينَ ومغالطاتِ الضالِّين.
2- تعلم ما يستطيعُ به المكلفُ أداءَ المفروضِ عليهِ من العبادات، كالصلاةِ والزكاةِ والحجِّ والصوم.
3- مَنْ تعاطى شيئاً من المعاملاتِ كالبيوعِ والإجارةِ والنكاحِ والطلاق... يفترضُ عليهِ تعلمَ ما يتمكنُ معهُ من تجنبِ الحرامِ والتزامِ حدودِ الشرع.
4- تعلمِ أحوالِ القلبِ من التوكلِ والخشيةِ والرضا، لأن المسلمَ واقعٌ طيلةَ عمرهِ في جميعِ الأحوالِ القَلبية.

5- تعلمِ جميعِ الأخلاقِ الحسنةِ والسيئةِ كي يطبِّق الحسنَة، كالتوكلِ على اللهِ تعالى والرضا عنهُ والتسليمِ له والتواضعِ والحلم... ويجتّنب السيئةَ كالكبرِ والغرورِ والبخلِ والحسدِ والحقدِ والرياء... ومن ثَمَّ يجاهدُ نفسهُ على تركِها، إذ إنَّ المجاهدةَ فرضٌ على كلِّ مكلفٍ ولا يمكنُ حصولُها إلا بمعرفةِ الأخلاقِ المذمومةِ والمَمدوحة، ومعرفةِ طرقِ المجاهداتِ التي اشتغلَ بها السادةُ الصوفيّة، ولهذا قال أبو الحسن الشاذلي: (من ماتَ ولم يتغلغَل في علمِنا هذا ماتَ مصراً على الكبائرِ وهو لا يشعُر). مع العلمِ أن الكبائرَ والفواحشَ منها ظاهِرة كالزنا وشربِ الخمرِ، ومنها باطِنة قلبيَّة كالكبرِ والنِّفاق... ولهذا نَهانا اللهُ عنها جميعاً بقوله: ﴿وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ﴾⁽¹⁾ ويتوبُ مرتكبُ الفواحشِ الظاهرةِ لاطلاعهِ على ضررِها، وأما الفواحشُ الباطنة فقَد يعيشُ دهراً طويلاً ولا يفكِّر بالتوبةِ منها لجهلهِ بحكمِها أو لعدمِ شعورهِ بها.
الصنف الثاني: فرضُ كفاية، وهوَ ما إذا قامَ به البعضُ سقطَ التَّكليف عنِ الباقين، وإذا لم يقُم به أحدٌ فالكلُّ آثمون.

والعلومُ المفروضةُ كفائياً هي ما يتوقَّف عليها صلاحُ الأمَّة، كالتعمُّق في علمِ الفقهِ زيادةً على مقدارِ الحاجة، وكذلكَ علمُ التفسيرِ والحديث، وأصولِ الفِقه، وأصولِ الاعتِقاد. وكذلكَ علمُ الحسابِ والطبِّ والصناعةِ وعلمِ السلاحِ لإعدادِ العدة.. الخ.⁽²⁾
ب- العلوم المنهي عنها:

1- فمنها الخوضُ في دراسةِ المذاهبِ الضالةِ والأفكارِ المشككةِ والعقائد الزائغةِ، لا بنيةِ الردِّ عليها ودفعِ خطرِها. أما تعلّمها لبيانِ زيغِها وردِّ شبهاتِها تَصحيحاً للعقائدِ وذوداً عنِ الدِّينِ فهوَ فرضُ كفايَة.

2- علمُ التنجيمِ لمعرفةِ مكانِ المسروقِ ومواضعِ الكنوزِ ومكانِ الضالةِ ونحو ذلك مما يزعمونَه، وهو من الكهانَة، وقد كذبَهم الشرعُ وحرَّم تصديقَهم. أما تعلُّم علمِ النجومِ للدراساتِ العلميةِ ولمعرفةِ مواقيتِ الصلاةِ والقِبلةِ فلا بأسَ به.
3- علمُ السّحر، يحرّم تعلّمه والعمل به.
جـ - العلوم المندوب إليها:

ومنهُ معرفةُ فضائلِ الأعمالِ البدنيةِ والقلبيةِ، ومعرفةُ النوافلِ والسننِ والمَكروهات، ومعرفةِ فروضِ الكِفاية، والتعمقِ في علومِ الفقهِ وفروعِها والعقائدِ وأدلتِها التفصيليَّة... الخ.⁽³⁾ وقد تبيَّن مما سبقَ حكمِ تعلمِ العلمِ وأهميّته في دينِ الله تَعالى، وأنَ موقفَ السادَة الصوفيّة منَ العلمِ واضحٌ لا يحتاجُ إلى دليل، فهم أهلُ العلومِ والمعارفِ وأربابِ القلوبِ المشرقةِ والأرواحِ المنطلقةِ، وأهلُ التحقيقِ بالإيمانِ والإسلامِ والإحسان. فبعدَ أن حصلوا العلومَ العينيّة عمدوا إلى تطبيقِ العلمِ على العمل، وقاموا بإصلاحِ القلبِ وتزكيةِ النفسِ وصدقِ التوجّه إلى اللهِ تعالى، ولهذا أكرمهُم الله تَعالى بالرضا والرضوانِ والمعرفةِ والغفران.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 18: What is the stance of the esteemed Sufis concerning knowledge?

Answer: The seeker, in the beginning of his spiritual journey, must have knowledge of doctrine (‘aqa’id) and knowledge that will correct his acts of worship and render his transactions upright. During his spiritual wayfaring the seeker cannot dispense with knowledge of the states of the heart, knowledge of what good character entails, and knowledge of what is required for the purification of the soul.

For this reason, acquiring necessary knowledge is seen as one of the most important foundations of Sufism’s practical method. For Sufism is naught but a practical and holistic implementation of Islam in all its aspects, inwardly and outwardly.
With respect to its legal judgment in the Shariah, knowledge is divided into three categories:
1. Knowledge that is prescribed
2. Knowledge that is proscribed
3. Knowledge that is recommended
Prescribed Knowledge

Prescribed knowledge consists of two types. The first type is that knowledge that is individually obligatory (fard ‘ayn). It is the knowledge that remains as a duty for a legally accountable individual until he or she has learned it. Before enumerating the areas of knowledge that are individually obligatory upon a legally accountable person, we must mention some foundational principles that pertain to this issue. These principles include: What is necessary in order to fulfil an obligation is itself an obligation, and Knowledge takes the judgement of that which is known. Therefore, the knowledge that enables one to establish an obligation is itself an obligation, and the knowledge that enables one to establish a Sunna is itself a Sunna. Based on these principles, we can mention some of the sciences that are obligatory upon one who is legally accountable. They include:

• Learning the doctrine of Ahl al-Sunna wal-Jama‘a with a general inference [of rational proofs] in matters of faith, in order free oneself from fetters of conformism (taqlid) and safeguard one’s faith from the doubts strewn out by atheists and the fallacious arguments of the ignorant.
• Learning what will suffice a legally accountable individual in fulfilling the rites of worship that are obligatory upon him such as prayer, Zakat, fasting, and Hajj.
• Learning how to avoid the unlawful and adhere to the limits of the Sacred Law in social and business transactions such as buying, renting, marrying, divorcing, and so on.
• Learning the states of the heart such as reliance, fear, and satisfaction—for a Muslim will possess states of the heart as long as he or she lives.

• Learning about all praiseworthy and blameworthy character traits in order to implement the praiseworthy ones, such as reliance upon Allah, satisfaction with Him, submission to Him, humility, and forbearance; and to abstain from the blameworthy ones such as pride, delusion, stinginess, envy, rancor, and ostentation. After learning the blameworthy traits, one may then engage in spiritual struggle to forsake them—as spiritual struggle is obligatory upon every legally accountable person, and it is unattainable except by knowing both blameworthy character and praiseworthy character and knowing the methods of spiritual struggle in which the esteemed Sufis have engaged. For this reason, Abu al-Hasan al-Shadhili said, “Whoever does not immerse himself into this science of ours dies having persisted in grave sins whilst perceiving it not.”

One should be aware that grave sins and vile acts include both outward sins, such as fornication and drinking alcohol, as well as inward sins of the heart, such as pride and hypocrisy. For this reason, Allah forbade us from both types when He said, Do not come close to vile sins, the apparent and hidden thereof. (Quran 6:151)⁽¹⁾ It is commonly observed that the one who commits outward sins will repent due to his witnessing their harm, whereas with internal sins he might live for a long time and not give a single thought to repenting from them because of his ignorance of their ruling or because he does not sense them.

The second type of knowledge is communally obligatory (fard kifaya). It is the knowledge of that which, if some carry it out, the obligation is lifted from the rest of the community, but if no one carries it out then all are deemed sinful. The communally obligatory branches of knowledge are those sciences that are conditional for the benefit of the Umma, such as in depth knowledge of jurisprudence beyond one’s immediate need,⁽²⁾ knowledge of the science of Qur’anic exegesis (tafsir), the science of hadith, the science of legal theory (usul), the foundations of theology, as well as mathematics, medicine, manufacturing, and knowledge of weaponry and martial readiness, and so on.
Proscribed Knowledge
Proscribed knowledge includes:

• Studying misguided sects, false ideologies, and corrupt beliefs in depth—unless one’s intention is to refute them and avert their harm. As for learning such false beliefs in order to respond to their false arguments, correct people’s beliefs, and defend the religion, it is communally obligatory.

• Learning astrology (tanjim) for the sake of finding the location of stolen or lost property or finding hidden treasures, and other things that are claimed to be achievable with this science. This is all from the handiwork of soothsayers whom the Sacred Law has declared liars and prohibited us from believing. There is no problem, however, with learning astronomy as a scientific study and in order to know the times of the prayers and the direction of Mecca.
• Learning sorcery. It is forbidden to learn sorcery and to practice it.
Recommended Knowledge
Recommended Knowledge includes:
• Knowing the virtuous acts of both the heart and the body.
• Knowing the supererogatory acts, recommended sunnas, and offensive acts (makruhat).
• Knowing all things that are communally obligatory.
• Knowing the depths and details of jurisprudence and its subsidiary branches, as well as theology and its detailed proofs.⁽³⁾

The above-mentioned points clarify the ruling of knowledge and its importance in the religion of Allah Most Exalted, and shows that the stance of the esteemed Sufis towards knowledge is clear and evident and not in need of proof, for they are the people of sacred sciences and branches of knowledge, and are the possessors of enlightened hearts and liberated souls. They are the people of realization in the stations of Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. After they gained knowledge of the sciences that are individually obligatory, they set out to implement them; they strove to purify their souls, rectify their hearts and turn sincerely to Allah. As a consequence, Allah honored them with His satisfaction and good pleasure, and with gnosis and forgiveness.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 19) ما قولكم في مجاهدة النفس عند السادة الصوفية؟

الجواب: تزكيةُ النفسِ فرضٌ عَين، ولا تتِم إلا بالمجاهَدة، ومِن هنا كانَت المجاهدةُ فرضٌ عينٌ أيضاً من باب (ما لا يتم الوجبُ إلا بهِ فهو واجِب). قال الشيخ عبد الغني النابلسي رحمة الله عليه: (المجاهدةُ في النفسِ عبادة، ولا تَحصل إلا بِالعلم، وهيَ فرضُ عينٌ على كلِّ مكلّف).⁽¹⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 19: What is your view concerning the Sufi practice of striving against the soul?

Answer: Purifying the soul is individually obligatory and can only be accomplished by means of struggle. Purifying the soul is individually obligatory in accordance with the principle that “What is necessary in order to fulfil an obligation is itself an obligation.” Shaykh ‘Abd al-Ghani al-Nabulusi (Allah have mercy upon him) said, “Striving against the soul is an act of worship, and it is unattainable except with knowledge. It is individually obligatory on every legally accountable person.”⁽¹⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 20) هل صفات النفس الإنسانية قابلة للتغيير؟

الجواب: ولا شكَّ أن النفسَ الإنسانيّة قابلةٌ لتغييرِ صفاتِها الناقصةِ وتبديلِ عاداتِها المذمومة، وإلا لم يَكن هناكَ فائدةٌ من بعثةِ الرسولِ الكريمِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، ولا ضرورةَ لِمن بعدهُ مِن ورثتهِ العلماءَ العاملينَ والمرشدينَ المصلِحين.
وإذا كانَ كثيرٌ من سِباع الطيورِ والبهائمِ قد أمكنَ ترويضَها، وتبديلِ كثيرٍ من صِفاتها، فالإنسانُ الذي كرَّمه اللهُ تَعالى وخلقَه في أحسنِ تقويم، من بابٍ أولى.
وليسَ المرادُ مِن مجاهدةِ النفسِ استِئصال صِفاتها، بل المرادُ تصعيدُها من سيئٍ إلى أحسن، وتسييرها على مُراد الله تَعالى وابتغاءِ مرضاتِه.

فصفةُ الغضبِ مذمومةٌ حينَ يغضبُ المرءُ لنفسِه، أما إذا غضبَ للهِ تَعالى فعندَها يصبحُ الغضبُ ممدوحاً، كما كانَ رسولُ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يغضبُ إذا انتهكَت حرماتُ اللهِ أو عُطِّلَ حدٌّ من حدودِه، ولكنَّه حينَ أوذِي في اللهِ وضُرب وأدمِي عقبه يومَ الطائِـف، لَم يغضَب لنفسِه، بل دَعا لمَن آذوهُ بالهدايةِ والتمسَ لهم العذر فقال: (اللهمّ اهدِ قومي فإنَّهم لا يعلَمون).⁽¹⁾
وكذلكَ صفةُ الكِبر فهي مذمومةٌ حينَ يتكبرُ المسلمُ على إخوتهِ المسلِمين، أما حينَ يتكبرُ على المتكبِّرين الكافِرين فتصبحُ هذهِ الصفةُ محمودة، لأنها في سبيلِ اللهِ وضمنَ حدودِ شرعِه.
وهكذا معظمُ الصفاتِ المذمومةِ تحول بالمجاهدَة وتُصعد إلى صفاتٍ ممدوحة.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 20: Are the character traits of human susceptible to change, and if so, what is the nature of that change?

Answer: There is no doubt that the human character has the potential for change, and that it is possible to remove and replace its bad character traits and blameworthy habits. Were there no potential for change, there would be no benefit in [Allah] sending the noble Messenger ﷺ, and there would be no need for prophetic heirs among the practicing scholars and righteous spiritual guides to come after him.

If it is possible for various predatory birds and other beasts of prey to change many of their supposedly innate character traits, then certainly humans—whom Allah has honored and created in the best of forms—can change their character even more so.

The intent behind struggling against the soul is not a complete and total eradication and effacement of its qualities; rather, it is to elevate it from what is bad to what is good, and direct its journey to Allah’s will and in pursuit of His good pleasure.

Anger, for example, is a blameworthy quality when one expresses it for the sake of his ego, whereas it is a praiseworthy quality when he expresses it for the sake of Allah—as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would get angry if the rights of Allah were violated or if any of His limits were transgressed. On the other hand, whenever the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would suffer injury or harm for the sake of Allah, or whenever he was struck or pelted—as he was on the day he was in Ta’if—he would not get angry for his own sake; rather, he would pray for those who harmed him and ask Allah to guide them. He would give them excuses and say, “O Allah, guide my people for they know not!”⁽¹⁾

Likewise, arrogance is also a blameworthy quality when a Muslim behaves arrogantly towards his Muslim brethren. On the other hand, when he behaves proudly in front of arrogant disbelievers, it becomes a praiseworthy trait because it is for the sake of Allah and within the limits of the Sacred Law.
The same may be said for most blameworthy qualities of character: they are transformable through spiritual struggle and may be elevated to praiseworthy characteristics.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 21) ما هي طريقة مجاهدة النفس؟
الجواب: وأولُ مرحلةٍ في المجاهدةِ عدمُ رِضى المرءِ عن نفسِه، وإيمانهِ بوصفِها الذي أخبرَ عنهُ خالقُها ومبدعُها: (إن النفس لأمارة بالسوء).⁽¹⁾

وعلمهُ أنَّ النفسِ أكبرُ قاطعٍ⁽²⁾ عن الله تَعالى، كما أنها أعظمُ موصلٍ إليهِ، وذلكَ أن النفسَ حينَما تكونُ أمارةً بالسوءِ لا تتلذذُ إلا بالمَعاصي والمُخالفات، ولكنَّها بعدَ مجاهدتِها وتزكيتِها تصبحُ راضيةً مرضيةً لا تسرُّ إلا بالطاعاتِ والموافقاتِ والاستئناسِ باللهِ تَعالى.

وإذا اكتشفَ المسلمُ عيوبَ نفسهِ وصدقَ في طلبِ تهذيبِها، لم يعُد عندهُ متَّسع من الوقتِ للانشغالِ بعيوبِ الناسِ وإضاعةِ العمرِ في تعدادِ أخطائِهم. وإذا رأيتَ أحداً من الناسِ قد صرفَ وقتهُ في إحصاءِ أخطاءِ الآخرينَ، غافلاً عن عيوبِ نفسهِ فاعلَم أنَّه أحمقٌ جاهل..
قال سيدي أبو مدين الغوث رحمه الله تعالى:
ولا تر العيب إلا فيــك معتقـداً عيباً بدا بينناً لكنه استــــترا
وقال بعضهم:

لا تلم المرء على فعله    وأنت منسوب إلى مثله
من ذمّ شيـئاً أتــى مثلــه    فإنمــا دل علـى جهلــه
ولذا قالوا: (لا ترَ عيبَ غيركَ ما دام فيك عيبُ، والعبدُ لا يخلوا من عيبٍ أبداً).

فإذا عرفَ المسلمُ ذلكَ أقبلَ على نفسهِ يفطمُها عن شهواتِها المنحرفةِ وعاداتِها الناقِصة، ويلزِمها بتطبيقِ الطاعاتِ والقربات. ويتدرَّج في المجاهدةِ على حسبِ سيرِه، فهو في بادئِ الأمرِ يتخلَّى عن المعاصِي التي تتعلقُ بجوارحهِ السبعةِ.. وهي: اللسانِ والأذنانِ والعينانِ واليدانِ والرجلانِ والبطنِ والفرج. ثم يحلي هذهِ الجوارِح السبعَة بالطاعاتِ المناسبةِ لكل منها، فهذهِ الجوارِح السبعَة منافذٌ على القَلب، إما أن تنصبَّ عليهِ ظلماتُ المعاصِي فتكدِّره وتمرضُه، وإمَّا أن تدخلَ عليهِ أنوارُ الطاعاتِ فتشفيهِ وتنورُه. ثم ينتقلُ في المجاهدةِ إلى الصفاتِ الباطِنة، فيبدلُ صفاتهِ الناقصةِ كالكبرِ والرياءِ والغضَب ... بصفاتٍ كاملةٍ كالتواضعِ والإخلاصِ والحِلم.

وبما أنَّ طريق المجاهدةِ وعرُ المسالكِ متشعبُ الجوانِب، يصعبُ على السالكِ أن يلِجه منفرداً، كان من المفيد عملياً صحبةَ مرشدٍ خبيرٍ بعيوبِ النفس، عالمٌ بطرقِ معالجتِها ومجاهدتِها، يستمدُّ المريدَ من صحبتهِ خبرةً عمليةً بأساليبَ تزكيةِ نفسِه، كما يكتسبُ من روحانيتهِ نفحاتٍ قدسيةٍ تدفعُ المريدَ إلى تكميلِ نفسهِ وشخصيتِه، وترفعهُ فوقَ مستوى النقائصِ والمنكَرات، فقد كانَ رسولُ الله صلى الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه المرشدَ الأوَّل الأعظمَ الذي ربَّى أصحابهُ الكِرام، وزكَّى نفوسهُم بقالهِ وحالِه، كما وصفهُ اللهُ تعالى بقوله: ﴿هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَإِنْ كَانُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ لَفِي ضَلَالٍ مُبِينٍ﴾⁽³⁾

والذي يحققُ النفعَ للمريدِ هو استقامتهِ على صحبةِ مرشدهِ، واستسلامهِ له كاستسلامِ المريضِ للطبيب. فإذا ما أدخلَ الشيطانُ على قلبِ المريد داء الغرورِ والاكتفاءِ الذاتي، فأعجِب بنفسِه، واستغنى عن ملازمةِ شيخهِ باءَ بالفشَل، ووقفَ وهو يظنُّ أنهُ سائر، وقُطِعَ وهو يظنُّ أنهُ موصول.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 21: What is the path of spiritual struggle?

Answer: The first stage in the path of spiritual struggle is for one to be unsatisfied with himself and to believe in the description of his ego that his Creator and Originator informed him of when He said, Indeed the ego commands to evil. (Quran 12:53)⁽¹⁾

He must also know that of the things that sever him and cut him off from Allah, the biggest of them is his own ego,⁽²⁾ while at the same time it is the greatest thing that shall cause him to arrive unto Allah. When the ego commands its owner to evil, it takes no delight except in acts of disobedience and contraventions, yet after spiritual struggle is waged against it and it is purified, it becomes pleased and well-pleasing, and is not joyful over anything except acts of obedience, compliance, and intimacy with Allah.

When a servant uncovers the defects of his soul and is truthful in his attempt to remove them, he will have no time to occupy himself with the faults of others and to waste his life enumerating their mistakes. Know that whoever spends his time counting the mistakes of others and is heedless of his own faults is an ignoramous.
Abu Madyan al-Ghawth (Allah have mercy upon him) said:
Regard not any fault but that acknowledged within you As a manifest, clearly apparent fault, though it be hidden
And others have said:

Don’t condemn one for doing an action To which you are equally ascribed
Whoso blames something while also doing it himself Has only demonstrated his own ignorance!
For this reason, the Sufis say, “Don’t see someone else’s faults so long as you have your own—and the servant will never be free of faults!”

When a Muslim comes to realize this, he will hasten to wean his ego from its base desires and deficient day-to-day habits, and he will oblige it to implement acts of obedience and deeds that draw close (to Allah). Spiritual struggle is a step by step process that changes course according to the progress of the seeker in his journey. In the beginning, he is to remove all of the acts of disobedience that relate to his seven body parts, and they are: the tongue, the ears, the eyes, the hands, the feet, the stomach, and the private parts. Afterwards, he is to adorn his seven body parts with acts of obedience that are appropriate to each. These seven body parts are the windows into the heart that will either be smeared and soiled with the darkness and grime of disobedience—thereby making the heart sick—or they will be made clear to allow the light and illumination of worship enter it. After waging spiritual struggle against his seven body parts, the seeker should then focus on the inward traits, replacing his lowly qualities, such as pride, ostentation, and anger, with perfected qualities, such as humility, sincerity, and forbearance.

Since the path of spiritual struggle is a rugged one with numerous side paths, and it is very difficult for a spiritual wayfarer to enter it alone, it is of practical benefit for him to keep the company of a completed spiritual guide who knows the blemishes of the soul and knows the path of spiritual struggle and treatment. By keeping the company of a spiritual guide, the seeker gains practical experience in the ways of purifying his soul, just as he acquires from the Shaykh’s spirituality gentle and sanctified breezes that push him to perfect his own self and personality, and that elevate him above defects and evil traits. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the first and supreme spiritual guide, who trained his noble Companions and purified their souls through his statements and spiritual state—as Allah described him: It is He who sent a Messenger unto the unlettered ones, reciting unto them His verses, purifying them, and teaching them the Book and the wisdom, whereas before they were in manifest error. (Quran 62:2)⁽³⁾

The seeker will realize gain and benefit when he is steadfast in keeping the company of his spiritual guide and submits himself to him as a patient submits himself to his physician. But when Satan injects the heart of the seeker with the disease of delusion and self-sufficiency, and he becomes conceited and feels that he no longer needs to keep the company of his Shaykh, he will fail utterly and be debarred from the path while he thinks that he is still on his journey, and he will be cut off while he thinks that he has been caused to arrive.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 22) ماذا يقول أئمة المرشدين في المجاهدة؟
الجواب: قال الإمام الجنيد رضوان الله تعالى عليه: (سمعت السري، يَقُول: يا معشرَ الشبابِ جدّوا قبل أن تبلُغوا فتضعُفوا، وتقصّروا كما قصَّرت، وكانَ في ذلكَ الوقتِ لا تلحقهُ الشبابُ في العبادَة).⁽¹⁾
وقال شيخ الإسلام زكريا الأنصاري رحمه الله تعالى: (إن نجاةَ النفسِ أن يخالفَ العبدُ هواها ويحملها على ما طلبَ منها ربّها).⁽²⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 22: Can you provide some statements from the spiritual guides about struggling against the self?

Answer: Imam al-Junayd (Allah the Exalted have mercy upon him) said, “I heard al-Sari al-Saqati say, ‘O youth! Be resolute before you become weak and irresolute as I have.’ He said that at a time when none of the youth could outstrip him in worship!”⁽¹⁾

Shaykh al-Islam Zakariyya al-Ansari (Allah have mercy upon him) said, “The salvation of the soul depends on the servant’s ability to oppose its vain desires and force it to carry out what its Lord has commanded it.”⁽²⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 23) هل تكون المجاهدة سببا في تحريم ما أحل الله تبارك وتعالى؟

الجواب: إن رجالَ التصوّف لم يجعلوا الحلالَ حَراماً، إذْ أسمى مقاصدهُم هو التقيدُ بشرعِ الله، ولكنَّهم حينَ عرفوا أن تزكيةَ النفسِ فرضُ عَين، وأن للنفسِ أخلاقاً سيئةً وتعلقاتٍ شهوانيّة، توصِل صاحبَها إلى الردى، وتعيقهُ عن الترقي في مدارجِ الكمال، وجَدوا لزاماً عليهِم أن يهذّبوا نفوسَهم ويحرِّروها من سجنِ الهَوى.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 23: Is struggling against the self a cause for forbidding what Allah Most High has permitted?

Answer: The Sufis have not made unlawful what Allah has made lawful. Their loftiest aim is to conform to the Law of Allah; however, since they knew that purifying the soul is individually obligatory, and that the soul has ill qualities of character and vain attachments that will lead its owner to disgrace and hold him back from ascending to the degrees of the perfected, they found it absolutely necessary to refine their souls and liberate them from the prison of vain desires.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 24) هل للتصوف أي انحدار من أصل غير إسلامي وما دليل عدم صحة ذلك؟

الجواب: لقد تسرَّع بعضُ الناسِ فزعموا جهلاً أن التصوّف في مجاهداتهِ ينحدرُ من أصلٍ بوذيٍّ أو برهميٍّ.. ويلتقي مع الانحرافاتِ الدينيةِ في النصرانيةِ وغيرها، التي تعتبرُ تعذيبَ الجسدِ طريقاً إلى إشراقِ الروحِ وانطلاقِها، ومنهُم من جعلَ التصوّف امتداداً لنزعةِ الرهبنةِ التي ظهرَت في ثلاثةِ رهطٍ سألوا عن عبادةِ رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، فلما أُخبروا عنها كأنّهم تقالوها، فقالَ أحدهم: أما أنا فأصومُ الدهرَ ولا أفطِر، وقالَ الثاني: أما أنا فأقومُ الليلَ ولا أَنام، وقال الثالث: أما أنا فأعتزلُ النساءَ ولا أتزوّج. ولما عُرض أمرهُم على رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، صحَّحَ لهم خطأهم فرجِعوا إلى هديه وسنته.

والجوابُ على ذلك: أن التصوّف لم يكن في يومٍ من الأيامِ شرعةً مستقلةً ولا ديناً جديداً، ولكنهُ تطبيقٌ عمليٌ لدينِ الله تعالى، واقتداء كامِل برسولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه.. وإنما سَرت الشبهةُ على هؤلاءِ المتسرّعين لأنهم وَجدوا في التصوّف اهتماماً بتزكيةِ النفسِ وتربيتِها وتصعيدِها، ومجاهدتِها على أسسٍ شرعيةٍ وضمنَ نطاقِ الدينِ الحَنيف، فقاسوا تلكَ الانحرافاتِ الدينيةِ على التصوّف قياساً أَعمى دون تمحيصٍ أو تمييز. ففرقٌ كبير إذا بين المجاهدةِ المشروعةِ المقيدةِ بدينِ اللهِ تعالى، وبين المغالاةِ والانحرافِ وتحريمِ الحلالِ وتعذيبِ الجَسد، كما عليهِ البوذيّون الكافِرون.

ومنَ الظلمِ والبهتانِ أن يحكمَ على كلِّ من جاهدَ نفسهُ وزكاها أنَّه ينحدرُ من أصلٍ بوذيّ أو برهميّ كما يزعمُ المستشرقونَ ومن خُدع بِهم، أو أنه يقتَدي بهؤلاءِ الرّهط الذين تقالوا عبادةَ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه كما يقوله المتسرّعون السطحيونَ مع أن رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه صحَّحَ لهم خطأهُم فرجِعوا إلى هديهِ وسننِه.

وإذا وُجدَ في تاريخِ التصوّف من حرَّم الحلالَ أو قامَ بتعذيبِ الجسدِ على غرارِ الانحرافاتِ الدينيّة السابِقة، فهو مبتدعٌ ومبتعدٌ عن طريقِ التصوّف، لذا ينبَغي التفريقَ بين التصوّف والصوّفي، فليسَ بانحرافهِ ممثلاً للتصوّف، كما أن المسلمَ بانحرافهِ لا يمثّل الإسلام.. والمعترضونَ لم يفرّقوا بينَ الصوفيّ والتصوّف، وبينَ المسلمِ والإسلام، فجَعلوا تلازماً بينَهما فوقَعوا في الكامِلين قياساً على المُنحرفين.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 24: Does Sufism derive its methods from any non-Islamic sources? If not, how do you refute this claim?

Answer: Some people, in their haste and ignorance, have claimed that Sufism derives its methods of spiritual struggle from Buddhist or Hindu sources, and that it corresponds to the religious deviations of the Christians and others who consider self-mortification and punishment a path towards spiritual enlightenment and freedom. Some even believe that Sufism owes its origin to the monastic inclination of the three men who inquired about the Prophet’s worship ﷺ. When they were informed of his worship, they considered their own worship trivial; thus one of them said, “As for me, I will perpetually fast and never break it.” The second one said, “As for me, I will stand in night prayer and never sleep.” The third one said, “As for me, I will shun women and never marry.” When their matter was presented to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he corrected their mistaken notions and brought them back to the straight path and upright method.

In response to this claim we say: Sufism has never been a separate way or a new religion. Rather, it is the practical implementation of Allah’s religion and complete conformity to the way of His Messenger ﷺ. This misconception only came to the minds of hasty individuals because they saw that Sufism placed a great deal of focus and concern on purifying the soul, refining and elevating it, and struggling against it according to the confines of the religion and limits of the Sacred Law. As a result, they drew a blind and erroneous analogy between the religious errors of other religions and Sufism, without drawing any distinction between them whatsoever. There is a vast difference between the legislated spiritual struggle that is confined to the strictures of Allah’s religion on the one hand, and the deviance, extremism, arbitrary forbiddance of the lawful, and self-mortification that the disbelieving Buddhists practice on the other.

It is slanderous and unjust to accuse one who strives to purify and struggle against his lower self of having derived his practice from Buddhist or Hindu origins—as is claimed by the orientalists and those fooled by them. It is equally unjust to accuse such a person of following the example of the three men who considered their worship insignificant in comparison to the devotions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ—as is claimed by those who are simple minded and hasty—despite the fact that the Messenger ﷺ corrected their mistaken notions and brought them back to his guidance and Sunna.

If there is to be found in the history of Sufism someone who has indeed forbidden what is lawful and engaged in self-mortification like those of the deviated early religions, then he is an avowed innovator who is far from the true path of Sufism. For this reason, a distinction must be made between Sufism as such and one who is a Sufi. A deviated Sufi does not represent Sufism any more than a deviated Muslim represents Islam. The opponents of Sufism have not made any distinction between one who is a Sufi and Sufism as such, or between one who is Muslim and Islam as such—thus they have maligned the honor of the perfected ones by comparing them to the deviated ones.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 25) هل المجاهدة شرط أساسي لكل سالك؟

الجواب: نعم، وذلكَ في جميعِ مراحلِ سيرِه، ولكنَّها تتغيرُ بحسبِ ترقّي المريدِ في مدارجِ السموّ، ومثالهُ في ذلك الطالبُ يكونُ في مرحلةِ الابتِدائي، ثمَّ الإعدادِي ثمَّ الثانَوي ثمَّ الجامِعي، وفي كلِّ هذهِ المراحلِ يعتبر طالباً، ولكِن هناكَ فرقٌ كبيرٌ بينَ الطالبِ الابتدائيّ والطالبِ الجامعيّ. وكذلك الفرقُ شاسعٌ بينَ كونِ نفسهِ أمّارة بالسّوء تميلُ إلى الفواحِش، وبينَ كونِها مطمئنةً راجعةً إلى ربِّها راضيةً مرضيَّة.

والخلاصة أنَّ المجاهدةَ أصلٌ من أُصولِ طريق الصوفيّة، وقد قالوا: مَن حقَّق الأصولَ نالَ الوصول، ومن تركَ الأصولَ حُرم الوصول. وقالوا أيضاً: مَنْ لَم تكُن له بداية محرقة "بالمجاهدات" لَم تكُن له نهايةً مشرقَة. والبداياتُ تدلُّ على النَّهايات.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 25: Is struggle against the soul a prerequisite for every spiritual wayfarer?

Answer: Yes! Spiritual struggle is a basic prerequisite for every spiritual wayfarer in every course of his journey. That said, the nature of spiritual struggle may change based on the status and rank of the seeker. The example of this may be likened to a student: a student begins his education in primary school and afterwards graduates and moves on to middle school, and finally moves on to high school and then to university. In all of these stages, this individual is still considered a student, even though there is a major difference between the primary school student and the university student. Similarly, there is a major difference between the soul that commands its owner to evil and inclines to vile deeds, and the soul that is content and ever returning to its Lord, pleased and well-pleasing.

In summary, spiritual struggle is one of the foundations of the Sufi path. They have said, “He who realizes the foundations will reach his destination; he who forsakes the foundations will be barred from his destination.” They have also said, “Whoever does not have a scorching beginning (with various acts of spiritual struggle) will not have an illumined end,” and “Beginnings presage endings.”

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 26) ما هي فائدة الذكر؟

الجواب: الذكرُ يثمرُ المقاماتَ كلّها من اليقظةِ إلى التوحيد، ويُثمر المعارفَ والأحوالَ التي شمَّر إليها السائرون، فلا سبيلَ إلى نيلِ ثمارِها إلا من شجرةِ الذّكر، وكلّما عظُمت تلكَ الشجرةُ ورسّخ أصلُها، كانَ أعظم لثمرتِها وفائدتِها...

وهو أصلُ كل مقامٍ وقاعدتهُ التي يبني عَليها، كما يُبنى الحائطُ على أساسِه، وكما يقومُ السقفُ على جدارِه، وذلك أن العبدَ إن لم يستيقِظ من غفلتهِ لم يمكنهُ قطعُ منازلِ السيرِ الموصلةِ إلى معرفةِ اللهِ تَعالى التي خُلِقَ الإنسان لأجلِها كما قال تعالى: ﴿وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالأِنْسَ إِلاَّ لِيَعْبُدُونِ﴾.⁽¹⁾ ولا يستيقظُ المرءُ إلا بالذكرِ، فالغفلة نومُ القلبِ أو موته. وإن امتثال الصوفيّة لأمرِ مولاهُم عزّ وجل بالإكثارِ من ذكرهِ جعلَ حياتهم كحياةِ الملائِكة، لا تخطرُ الدنيا على قلوبهِم، ولا تشغلهم عن محبوبهم، نسوا أنفسَهم بمجالستهِم لربهِم، وغابوا عن كلّ شيءٍ سواهُ فتواجَدوا عندَما وجدوا.
ذَكرْتُكَ لا أني نسيتُكَ لمحةً  وأيسرُ ما في الذكرِ ذكرُ لِساني

يذكرُ الصوفيّ ربّه في كل أحيانِه، فيجدُ بذلكَ انشراحَ الصّدر، واطمئنانَ القَلب، وسموَّ الرّوح، لأنه حظِي بمجالسةِ ربهِ عزَ وجل "أهل ذكري أهل مجالستي".⁽²⁾ فالعارفُ من داومَ على الذكرِ وأعرضَ بقلبهِ عن متعِ الدنيا الزائِلة، فتولاّه اللهُ في جميعِ شؤونِه. ولا عجب، فمَن صبرَ ظَفر، ومن لازمَ قرعَ البابِ يوشكُ أن يُفتح لَه.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 26: What is the reality of remembrance (dhikr) and what are its benefits?

Answer: From the station of awakening (yaqaza) to the station of affirming divine unity (tawhid), remembrance brings all of them to fruition. Remembrance also brings to fruition divinely vouchsafed knowledge (ma’arif) and spiritual states (ahwal) for which wayfarers strive. It is not possible to obtain their fruits except from the tree of remembrance. The greater such a tree becomes and the firmer its roots, the greater its fruits and benefits.

Remembrance is the basis for every spiritual station and the foundation upon which all of them are built—just as a wall is built on a foundation and a roof is built over a wall. Remembrance is such a foundation because the servant, if he does not wake up from his heedlessness, will be unable to traverse the long distance needed in his journey to reach gnosis of Allah, for which man was created: And I have not created mankind or Jinn save for them to worship Me. (Quran 51:56)⁽¹⁾ An individual will not wake up except by remembrance of the Divine. Heedlessness, therefore, is the sleep of the heart or its death.

When the Sufis complied with the command of their Master, the Mighty and Majestic, by frequently engaging in His remembrance, they made their lives like that of the Angels; the worldly life does not present itself to their hearts nor does it preoccupy them away from their Beloved. They forgot their own selves because of sitting in the presence of their Lord. Everything other than Him vanished from them and as a consequence, they experienced and manifested ecstasy.

I remembered You and certainly I have not forgotten You
And indeed, the easiest form of remembrance is that of the tongue

The Sufi remembers his Lord at all moments, and as a result he finds expansion in his breast, contentment in his heart, and loftiness in his spirit. This is all because he has sat in the company of Allah: “The people of My remembrance are those who sit in My company…”⁽²⁾ The knower of Allah is, therefore, one who persists in the remembrance of the Divine and turns his heart from the ephemeral delights of the world; and as a result, Allah takes care of him in all his affairs. This should not come as a surprise, for one who is patient will triumph and one who sticks closely to the door has hope that he will soon be admitted inside.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 27) ما هو فضل الذكر بدليل من الكتاب والسنة؟
الجواب: من القرآن الكريم قوله تعالى: ﴿فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ ﴾.⁽¹⁾ وقال تعالى: ﴿الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ﴾.⁽²⁾
وأما من السنّة: يعن أبي موسى الأشعري رضوانُ الله تعالى عَليه، قال النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: "مثلُ الذي يذكرُ ربّه والذي لا يذكرُ ربّه كمثلِ الحيِّ والميّت".⁽³⁾

وعن أبي هريرة رضوان الله عليه قال: (قال رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: "إنّ للهِ ملائكةً يطوفونَ في الطرقِ يلتمسونَ أهلَ الذّكر، فإذا وَجدوا قوماً يذكرونَ اللهَ تنادوا: هَلمُّوا إلى حاجتِكم، قال: فيحفّونهم بأجنحتهِم إلى السماءِ الدّنيا، قال: فيسألُهم ربّهم عزّ وجل- وهو أعلمُ منهُم، ما يقولُ عبادي؟ قال: يقولون: يسبّحونك ويكبرونكَ ويحمّدونك ويمجّدونَك، قال: فيقول: هل رَأوني؟ قال: فيقولون: لا واللهِ ما رَأوك، قال: فيقول: وكيفَ لو رَأوني؟ قال: يقولون: لو رَأوك كانوا أشدّ لكَ عِبادة، وأشدّ لكَ تمجيداً، وأكثرُ لكَ تسبيحاً، قال: يقول: فما يسألونَني؟ قال: يسألونكَ الجنّة، قال: يقول: وهل رَأوها؟ قال: يقولون: لا واللهِ يا رب ما رَأوها، قال: يقول: فكيفَ لو أنّهم رَأوها؟ قال: يقولون: لَو أنّهم رَأوها كانوا أشدّ عليها حرصاً، وأشد لها طلباً، وأعظم فيها رغبَة، قال: فممّ يتعوّذون؟ قال: يقولون: مِن النّار، قال: يقول: وهَل رَأوها؟ قال: يقولون: لا واللهِ يا رب ما رَأوها، قال: يقول: فكيفَ لَو رَأوها؟ قال: يقولون: لو رَأوها كانوا أشدّ مِنها فراراً، وأشدّ لها مَخافة، قال: فيقول: أشهدُكم أني قد غفرتُ لهُم، قال: يقول ملكٌ من الملائِكة: فيهم فلان ليسَ منهم، إنما جاءَ لِحاجة، قال: هُم الجُلساء لا يَشقى بهِم جليسٌهم").⁽⁴⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 27: What are some of the proofs in the Quran and Sunna in support of remembrance?
Answer: Proofs from the Quran include: Remember Me and I will remember you. (Quran 2:152)⁽¹⁾; and Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying upon their sides. (Quran 3:191)⁽²⁾

As for proofs from the Sunna, it is narrated on the authority of Abu Musa al-Ash’ari رضي الله عنه, “The Prophet ﷺ said, ‘The likeness of the one who remembers Allah and the one who does not remember Allah is that of the living and the dead.’”⁽³⁾

Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Allah has angels who travel the pathways and by-ways seeking out the people of remembrance. When they find people remembering Allah, they call out to one another, ‘Come to what you hunger for!’ and they enfold them with their wings stretching up to the lowest heaven. Their Lord—who knows best—asks them, ‘What are My slaves saying?’ They say, ‘They are glorifying You, proclaiming Your greatness, praising You and magnifying You.’ He says, ‘Have they seen Me?’ They say, ‘No, by Allah, they have not seen You.’ He says, ‘How would it be if they were to see Me?’ They say, ‘If they were to see You, they would worship You even more intensely and magnify You even more intensely and glorify You even more intensely.’ He says, ‘What are they asking for?’ They say, ‘They are asking You for the Garden.’ He says, ‘Have they seen it?’ They say, ‘No, by Allah, O Lord, they have not seen it.’ He says, ‘How would it be if they were to see it?’ They say, ‘If they were to see it, they would yearn for it even more strongly and seek it even more assiduously and would have an even greater desire for it.’ He says, ‘What are they seeking refuge from?’ They say, ‘They are seeking refuge from the Fire.’ He says, ‘Have they seen it?’ They say, ‘No, by Allah, O Lord, they have not seen it.’ He says, ‘How would it be if they were to see it?’ They say, ‘If they were to see it, they would flee from it even more and have an even greater fear of it.’ He says, ‘I testify to you that I have forgiven them.’ One of angels says, ‘Among them is so-and-so who is not one of them. He came to get something he needed.’ He says, ‘They are sitting and the one sitting with them will not be disappointed.’”⁽⁴⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 28) ماذا قال ابن عجيبة في حق الذكر؟
الجواب: قال أحمد بن عجيبة: لا يَكون الفتحُ على تحقيقِ العبدِ في مقامِ الرضا إلا بعدَ تحقّقه بثلاثةِ أمورٍ في بدايَته:
1- الاستِغراق في الاسمِ المُفرد (الله) وهذا خاصُّ بالمأذونينَ بذكرِ الاسمِ المفردِ من مرشدٍ كامل.
2- صحبتُه للذاكِرين.
3- تمسّكه بالعملِ الصالحِ الذي لم يتّصل به شيءٌ من العِلل، وهو التمسّك بالشريعةِ المحمدية.⁽¹⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 28: What did Ibn ‘Ajiba say about remembrance?
Answer: Shaykh Ahmad Ibn ‘Ajiba said, “The servant will not have gain a spiritual opening in the station of satisfaction (rida) until he actualizes three things in the beginning:
[1] being engulfed in remembrance of the solitary name (al-Ism al-Mufrad) ‘Allah’ (and this is restricted to those who are authorized to perform the specific litany by a completed spiritual guide),
[2] keeping the company of the people of Divine remembrance,
[3] holding fast to pious works that are free of defects—and this is holding fast to the Muhammadan Sacred law.⁽¹⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 29) أيهما أفضل ذكر السر أم ذكر العلن؟

الجواب: أنهُ لا كراهةَ في شيءٍ من ذلك، وقد وردَت أحاديثُ تقتضي استحبابَ الجهرِ بالذكرِ وأحاديثَ تقتضي استحبابَ الأسرارِ به والجمعِ بينهُما، أن ذلك يختلفُ باختلافِ الأحوالِ والأشخاص، فقد قال الإمام السيوطي رحمه الله تعالى في ذلك:

إذا تأمَلت ما أوردناهُ من الأحاديثِ عرفتَ من مجموعِها أنّه لا كراهةَ البتّة في الجهرِ بالذّكر، بلْ فيهِ ما يدلُّ على استحبابهِ إما صريحاً أو التزاماً، كما أشَرنا إلَيه، وأما معارضتُه بحديث: (خيرُ الذكرِ الخَفي) فهوَ نظيرُ معارضةِ أحاديثَ الجهرِ بالقرآنِ بحديث: (المسرّ بالقرآنِ كالمسرّ بالصدَقة) وقد جمعَ النووي بينَهما: بأنَ الإخفاءَ أفضلُ حيثُ خيفَ الرياءِ أو تأذَّى به مصلّون أو نِيام، والجهرُ أفضلُ في غيرِ ذلك؛ لأنَ العملَ فيه أكثَر ولأنّ فائدتهُ تتعدَّى إلى السامِعين، ولأنهُ يوقظُ قلبَ القارِئ ويجمعُ همّه إلى الفِكر، ويصرفُ سمعهُ إليهِ، ويطردُ النومَ ويزيدُ في النشاطِ، وقال بعضهم:
"يستحبّ الجهرُ ببعضِ القراءةِ والإسرارِ ببعضها، لأن المسرّ قد يملّ فيأنس بالجهر" والجاهر قد يكلّ فيستريح بالإسرار، وكذلكَ نقولُ في الذّكر على هذا التَّفصيل، وبهِ يحصلُ الجمعُ بينَ الأحاديث.
فإن قلت قال الله تعالى: ﴿وَاذْكُر رَّبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخِيفَةً وَدُونَ الْجَهْرِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ﴾.⁽¹⁾ قلت: الجوابَ على هذه الآيةِ من ثلاثةِ أوجه:

الأول: أنها مكيّة كآيةِ الإسراء ﴿وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا ﴾⁽²⁾ وقد نزلَت حينَ كان النبيّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يجهرُ بالقرآنِ فيسمعهُ المشركونَ فيسبّونَ القرآنَ ومن أنزلَه، فأُمرَ بتركِ الجهرِ سداًّ للذريعَة، كما نُهي عن سبِّ الأصنامِ لذلكَ في قولهِ تَعالى: ﴿لَا تَسُبُّوا الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ فَيَسُبُّوا اللَّهَ عَدْوًا بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ﴾⁽³⁾ وقدَ زالَ هذا المعنى، وأشارَ إلى ذلكَ ابن كثير في تفسيرِه.

ثانياً: أن جماعةً من المفسرِين - منهم عبد الرحمن بن زيد بن أسلم شيخ مالك، وابن جرير حملوا الآية على الذاكرِ حالَ قراءةِ القرآن، وأنّه أمرَ له بالذكرِ على هذهِ الصفةِ تعظيماً للقرآنِ أن تُرفع عندَه الأصوات، ويقوّيه اتصالُها بقولِه: ﴿ وَإِذَا قُرِئَ الْقُرْآنُ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ وَأَنصِتُوا ﴾.⁽⁴⁾ قلت، وكأنّه لما أمرَ بالإنصاتِ خشيَ من ذلك الإخلادُ إلى البطالَة، فنبّه على أنّه وإن كانَ مأموراً بالسكوتِ بالِّلسان إلا أنَّ تكليفَ الذكرِ بالقلبِ باقٍ حتى لا يغفلَ عن ذكرِ الله، ولذا ختمَ الآيةَ بقولِه: ﴿وَلا تَكُن مِنَ الغافِلين﴾.
ثالثاً: ما ذكرهُ الصوفيّة أن الأمرَ في الآيةِ خاص بالنبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه الكامِل المكمّل، وأما غيره ممَّن هو محَل الوساوسِ والخواطرِ الرديّة فمأمورٌ بالجَهر، لأنّه أشدُّ تأثيراً في دفعِها.

قلت: ويؤيّده من الحديثِ ما أخرجهُ البزار عن معاذ بن جبل رضوان الله عليه، قال: رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: "من صلَّى بالليلِ فليجهَر بقراءتِه، فإنَّ الملائكةَ تصلِّي بصلاتهِ، وتسمعُ لقراءتِه، وإن مؤمِني الجِن الذين يكونونَ في الهواءِ وجيرانهِ معهُ في مسكنهِ يصلونَ بصلاتهِ، ويستمعونَ قراءَته، وإنه ينطردُ بجهرهِ بقراءتِه عن دارهِ وعن الدورِ التي حولَه فساقَ الجنّ ومردة الشياطين".
فإن قلت: فقد قال تعال: ﴿ ادْعُواْ رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً إِنَّهُ لاَ يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ﴾⁽⁵⁾ وقد فسر الاعتداءَ: بالجهرِ في الدعاء، قلت: الجوابُ عنه من وجهَين:

أحدهما: أن الراجحَ في تفسيرهِ أنه تجاوزَ المأمورَ به، أو اختراعُ دعوةٍ لا أصلَ لها في الشّرع، ويؤيدُه ما أخرجهُ ابن ماجه والحاكم في مستدركهِ وصححهُ عن أبي نعامة رضوان الله عليه: أنَّ عبد الله بن مغفل سمعَ ابنه يقول: اللهمَّ إني أسألكَ القصرَ الأبيضَ عن يمينِ الجنَّة، فقال عبد الله سمعتُ رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يقول: (سيكونُ في هذهِ الأمةِ قومٌ يعتدونَ بالدُّعاء) فهذا تفسيرُ صحابِي وهو أعلمُ بالمُراد.

الثاني: على تقديرِ التسليم، فالآيةُ في الدعاءِ لا في الذِّكر، والدعاءُ بخصوصهِ الأفضلُ فيهِ الإسرار لأنهُ أقربٌ إلى الإِجابَة، ولذا قال تعالى: ﴿إذُ نَادَى رَبَّهُ نِداءً خَفِيَّا﴾⁽⁶⁾ ومن ثَم استحبَّ الإسرارَ بالاستعاذةِ في الصلاةِ اتفاقاً لأنها دُعاء.

فإن قلت: فقد نقلَ عن ابن مسعود رضوان الله عليه أنه رأى قوماً يهلِّلون برفعِ الصوتِ في المسجِد فقال: ما أراكم إلا مبتدِعين، حتى أخرجَهم من المسجِد. قلت: هذا الأثرُ عن ابن مسعود يحتاجُ إلى بيانِ سندهِ ومن أخرجهُ من الأئمةِ الحفَّاظ في كتبهِم، وعلى تقديرِ ثبوتهِ فهو معارضٌ بالأحاديثِ الكثيرةِ الثابتةِ المتقدمة، وهي مقدمةٌ عليه عند التَّعارض، ثم رأيتُ ما يقتضي إنكارَ ذلك عن ابن مسعود عليه الرضوان، قال الإمام أحمد بن حنبل في كِتاب الزُّهد:

حدَّثنا حسين بن محمد، حدثنا المسعودي عن عامر بن شقيق عن أبي وائل قال: هؤلاءِ الذينَ يزعمونَ أن عبد الله كان ينهى عن الذَّكر، ما جالست عبد الله مجلساً قط إلا ذُكر الله فيه. وأخرجَ أحمد في الزهدِ عن ثابت البناني قال: إن أهلَ ذكرِ اللهِ ليجلسونَ إلى ذكرِ الله، وإن عليهِم من الآثامِ أمثالَ الجِبال، وإنّهم ليقومونَ من ذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى ما عليهِم مِنها شَيء.⁽⁷⁾

وقال العلامة الكبير محمود الأولسي في تفسيره عند قوله تعالى: ﴿وَإِن تَجْهَرْ بِالْقَوْلِ فَإِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ السِّرَّ وَأَخْفَى ﴾.⁽⁸⁾ وقيل: نهيَ عن الجهرِ بالذكرِ والدعاء كقولِه تعالى ﴿واذكُر رَبَّكَ في نَفسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخيفَةً وَدونَ الجَهرِ مِنَ القَولِ﴾.⁽⁹⁾

وأنتَ تعلمُ أن القولَ بأنَّ الجهرَ بالذكرِ والدعاءِ منهيٌّ لا ينبَغي أن يكونَ على إطلاقهِ والذي نصَّ عليهِ الإمام النووي في فتاويهِ أن الجهرَ بالذكرِ والدعاءِ منهي، لا ينبغي أن يكونَ على إطلاقهِ والذي نصّ عليه الإمام النووي رضوان الله عليه في فتاويه "أن الجهرَ بالذكر حيث لا محذور شرعاً مشروعٌ مندوبٌ إليهِ بل هو أفضلُ من الإخفاءِ في مذهبِ الإمام الشافعي" وهو ظاهر مذهب الإمام أحمد وإحدى الروايتينِ عن الإمام مالك بنقل الحافظ ابن حجر في فتح الباري وهو قول القاضيان في فتاويهِ في ترجمةِ مسائلَ كيفيةِ القراءةِ، وقولهُ في بابِ غسلِ الميتِ، (ويكرهُ رفعَ الصوتِ بالذكر) فالظاهرُ أنه لمَن يمشي معَ الجنازةِ كما هو مذهبُ الشافعية: لا مطلقا. وقال الألوسي أيضا: واختارُ بعضَ المحققينَ أن المرادَ دون الجهرِ البالغِ أو الزائدِ على قدرِ الحاجة، فيكون الجهرُ المعتدلُ والجهرُ بقدرِ الحاجة داخلاً في المأمورِ به، فقد صحَّ ما يزيدُ على عشرينَ حديثاً في أنه صلوات الله وسلامه عليه كثيرٌ ما كانَ يجهر بالذِّكر، وصحَّ عن أبي الزبير أنه سمعَ عبد الله بن الزبير يقول:

كان رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه إذا سلَّمَ من صلاتِه يقولُ بصوتهِ الأعلَى: (لا إلهَ إلا اللهَ وحدهُ لا شريكَ له، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهو على كلِّ شيءٍ قَدير، لا حولَ ولا قوةَ إلا باللهِ ولا نعبدُ إلا إياهُ له النعمةُ ولهُ الفَضل) إلى أن قال: وقد ألَّف الشيخ إبراهيم الكوراني عليه رحمة الله تعالى في تحقيقِ هذهِ المسألةِ رسالتَين جليلَتين سمى أولاهُما "نثر الزهرِ في الذِّكرِ بالجَهر" وثانيتهُما "إتحاف المنيبِ الأواهُ بفضلِ الجهرِ بذكرِ الله.⁽¹⁰⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 29: Which is superior, audible or inaudible remembrance?

Answer: Neither is seen as offensive. There are numerous hadith reports indicating that both audible and silent remembrance are recommended. In reconciling between the two, we say that both types depend on the individual and the situation. Imam al-Suyuti (Allah have mercy upon him) said about this:

If you contemplate the Hadith reports we have cited here, you will realize that there is absolutely nothing detestable about audible remembrance. Rather, these reports indicate that it is recommended, either explicitly or implicitly, as we pointed out earlier. As for contesting these narrations with the hadith “The best remembrance is that which is done silently,” that is akin to opposing the hadith regarding audible recitation of the Quran with the narration “The one who recites silently is like one who gives charity silently.” Imam al-Nawawi reconciled between them, saying that silence is better when one fears ostentation, or fears annoying those in prayer or sleep. Audible remembrance is better in every other circumstance, because it entails more action, its benefit goes to those listening as well, and it makes the reciter awake, focuses his energy on contemplation, turns his hearing to it, shakes off sleepiness, and increases energy. Some have said, “It is recommended to read some things audibly and some things silently, because the one reading silently might become bored and find comfort in reading audibly, and the one reading audibly might become fatigued and find relaxation in reading silently.” This is with regard to the Quran; and we say the same here with regard to remembrance, and in this way, it is possible to reconcile all of the narrations.
If you retort, “But Allah the Exalted said: Remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear without being audible in speech. (Quran 7:205), we respond in three ways.

Firstly, this is a Meccan verse like the other Meccan verse in Sura al-Isra’: And do not make your prayer too loudly or too quietly… (Quran 17:110), which was revealed when the Prophet ﷺ was audibly reciting the Quran and the idol worshippers would hear it and insult the Quran and the One who revealed it. Consequently, and in order to prevent such harm, he was ordered to stop reading the Quran audibly, just as he was forbidden from insulting the false gods of the idol worshippers: And do not insult those they call upon besides Allah lest they insult Allah in transgression and without knowledge. (Quran 6:108) However, as Ibn Kathir noted in his exegesis, this is no longer the case.

Secondly, a number of Quranic scholars, such as ‘Abd al-Rahman ben Zayd ben Aslam (the Shaykh of Malik) and Ibn Jarir [al-Tabari] understood the verse to refer to the one engaged in remembrance when reading the Quran, and that this verse is a command for such a person to remember (Allah) in this specific way for the sake of honoring the Quran lest voices become raised in its recital. This is strongly supported by the words of the Exalted: When the Qur’an is recited, listen attentively to it and remain quiet. (Quran 7:204) I [al-Suyuti] say: After the order to remain quiet was revealed, it seems as though it was feared that such an order would lead people to laziness, so Allah informed him that even though he is ordered to remain quiet with his tongue, the responsibility of remembering with the heart remained, lest he become heedless of Allah’s remembrance. For this reason, Allah completed the verse by saying: And do not be from the heedless.

Thirdly, the esteemed Sufis mentioned that the command in this verse is specific (khass) to the Prophet ﷺ, who is both perfect and perfected [by Allah]; as for others besides him—who are subject to whisperings and lowly passing thoughts—they are commanded to remember audibly since it has a greater effect in repelling such things. This is supported by a hadith narrated by al-Bazzar from Mu‘adh b. Jabal رضي الله عنه, who reported, “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, ‘Whoever among you prays in the night, let him recite audibly, for the Angels pray upon him on account of his prayer and they hear his recitation. Indeed, even the believing Jinn in the sky and his neighbors listen to it and pray upon him on account of his recitation. Indeed, his audible recitation expels the corrupt Jinn and the rebellious devils from his house and the houses of those around him.’”

If you retort, “But Allah said: Call upon your Lord with silence and abject humility, indeed He does not love the transgressors. (Quran 7:55), and the word transgression in this verse has been explained to mean supplicating audibly”, we offer a twofold response.

First, the correct view regarding the interpretation of this verse is that it refers to going beyond the bounds of what is commanded, or inventing a supplication that has no basis whatsoever in the Sacred law. This view is supported by the narration found in the collection of Ibn Majah and al-Hakim in his Mustadrak, who authenticated it, from Nu‘ama: “‘Abdullah b. al-Mughaffal heard his son say in supplication, ‘O Allah! I ask You that you grant me a white castle on the right side of Paradise.’ His father said to him, ‘I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, ‘In this Umma there shall arise some who transgress in their supplications.’” This is the interpretation of a Companion, and he knows best what the verse entails.

Second, if it is conceded that this is the meaning of the verse, it is with regards to supplication and not remembrance in general. So with regard to supplication, it is better to do it silently, since there is a greater chance that it will be accepted. For this reason, the Exalted said, When he called upon his Lord in a subdued manner. (Quran 19:3) Similarly, by the agreement of the scholars, it is recommended to recite the prayer for seeking refuge [saying, “I seek refuge with Allah from the accursed Satan”] silently in prayer because it is a supplication.

If you retort, “But it was narrated from Ibn Mas‘ud رضي الله عنه that he once saw a group of people in the mosque raising their voices in unison while declaring Allah’s unity (saying la ilaha illa Allah), and he said to them, ‘I see you all as naught but innovators, and I will expel you from the Mosque!’” to this we reply: We need to look into the chain of this report from Ibn Mas‘ud and see who narrated it among the hadith masters and Imams within their books. Even if we concede that it is sound, it contradicts many of the authentic hadith we mentioned earlier, and those hadith reports are given precedence in the event of mutual exclusion. Furthermore, I have found a narration that implies that this report from Ibn Mas‘ud is not sound. Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal said in his book al-Zuhd, “Husayn b. Muhammad narrated to us, saying, ‘Al-Mas‘udi narrated to us on the authority of ‘Amir b. Shaqiq, from Abu Wa’il, who said, “I say to those who claim that ‘Abdullah b. Mas‘ud used to forbid remembrance: I have never sat in a gathering of his except that he was remembering Allah in it.”’”

Ahmad also narrated in al-Zuhd from Thabit al-Bunani, who said, “The people of Allah’s remembrance sit together while they possess sins like mountains, and after they stand up and depart from their gathering of Allah’s remembrance that have nothing of that.”⁽³⁾

The great and erudite Shaykh Mahmud al-Alusi said in his Qur’anic exegesis regarding the words of the Exalted, If you make audible your words, then certainly, He knows that which is secret and more concealed (Quran 20:7): It has been said that audible remembrance and recitation was forbidden due to the words of the Most Exalted, Remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear, without being audible in speech (Quran 7:205), whereas you are aware that it is incorrect to say that it is forbidden unrestrictedly. Imam al-Nawawi stated in his legal verdict that “Remembrance done out loud and audibly is legislated and recommended as long as it does not involve something forbidden in the Sacred law. Nay, in the legal school of Imam al-Shafi‘i it is better than doing so silently. It is the apparent ruling within the legal school of Imam Ahmad, and one of the two existing narrations regarding it in the school of Imam Malik…”—as al-Hafiz Ibn Hajar quoted in Fath al-Bari.

Some of the verifying scholars were of the opinion that this verse entails exaggerating in audible recitation or remembrance or what is beyond need and not included in moderate audible reading. Reading aloud according to one’s need is therefore part and parcel of the divine command, for there are more than twenty rigorously authentic hadith stating that the Prophet ﷺ would engage in audible remembrance. It is also authentically reported that al-Zubayr heard his son ‘Abdullah say, “After the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would give the salutations at the closing of the prayer, he would say with a raised voice, ‘There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things. There is neither might nor power except by Allah. We worship none but Him. To Him belongs the bounty and virtue…’”

Shaykh Ibrahim al-Kurani (Allah have mercy upon him) authored two excellent essays investigating this issue. The first one he entitled Nathr al-Zahr fi al-Dhikr bil-Jahr (Strewing Rose Petals On Audible Remembrance), and the second one he entitled Ithaf al-Munib al-Awwah bi-Fadl al-Jahr bi-Dhikr Allah’ (Gifting the Oft Returning and Compassionate one Regarding the Virtue of Remembering Allah Audibly).⁽⁴⁾
