questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 30) وماذا قال العلامة الطحطاوي في الجهر بالذكر؟

الجواب: قال العلامة الطحطاوي في حاشية على مراقي الفلاح: (اختُلِفَ هل الإسرارُ بالذِّكرِ أفضل فقيل: نَعم، لأحاديثَ كثيرةٍ تدلُّ منها: (خيرُ الذكرِ الخفيِّ وخيرُ الرزقِ ما يَكفي).. لأن الإسرارَ أبلغُ في الإخلاصِ وأقربُ إلى الإِجابة، وقيلَ: الجهرُ أفضلٌ لأحاديثَ كثيرة:

منها ما رواهُ ابن الزبير رضوان الله تعالى عليهما: كان رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه إذا سلَّمَ من صلاتهِ قال بصوتهِ الأَعلى (لا إلهَ إلا اللهَ وحدهُ لا شريكَ له، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهو على كلِّ شيءٍ قَدير، ولا حولَ ولا قوةَ إلا بالله).⁽¹⁾

وقد كان صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يأمرُ من يقرأَ القرآنَ في المسجدِ أن يُسمِع قراءَته. وكان ابن عمر يأمرُ من يقرأ عليه وعلى أصحابهِ وهم يستمِعون، لأنهُ أكثرُ عملاً، وأبلغُ في التدَبُّر، ونفهُ متعدٍ لإيقاظِ قلوبِ الغافلين.

وجُمع بين الأحاديثِ الواردةِ بأنَّ ذلكَ يختلفُ بحسبِ الأشخاصِ والأحوال، فمن خافَ الرِّياء، أو تأذَّى بِه أحد، كان الإسرارُ أفضَل، ومتى فُقد ما ذكِر كانَ الجهرُ أفضل. قال في الفتاوي: لا يُمنعُ من الجهرِ بالذكرِ في المساجد، احترازاً عن الدخولِ تحت قولِه تعالى: )ومَنْ أظلَمُ مِمَّنْ مَنَعَ مساجدَ اللهِ أنْ يُذكَرَ فيها اسمُه).⁽²⁾ كذا في البزازية.

ونص الشعراني في ذكر الذاكر للمذكور والشاكر للمشكور ما لفظُه: "وأجمعَ العلماءَ سلفاً وخلفاً على استحبابِ ذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى جماعة في المساجدِ وغيرها من غيرِ نكير، إلا أَن يشوّش جهرهُم بالذكرِ على نائمٍ أو مصلٌّ أو قارئِ قرآن، كما هو مقررٌ في كتبِ الفقه.⁽³⁾"

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 30: What did Shaykh al-Tahtawi say regarding audible and inaudible remembrance?
Answer: The erudite scholar al-Tahtawi said in his marginalia on Maraqi al-Falah:

There is a difference of opinion over silent remembrance and whether it is superior [to audible remembrance]. It has been said that silent remembrance is superior and that there are many hadith proving it. Among them: “The best remembrance is that which is done silently, and the best provision is that which suffices.” It has been said that silent remembrance is more conducive to sincerity and more apt to be answered. It has also been said that engaging in remembrance audibly is better due to many hadith reports, such as the narration from Ibn al-Zubayr, who said, “After the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would give the salutations at the closing of the prayer, he would say with a raised voice, ‘There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things. There is neither might nor power except by Allah. We worship none but Him. To Him belongs the bounty and virtue…’”⁽¹⁾ The Prophet ﷺ would order those who recited the Quran in the Mosque to make their recital heard. Ibn ‘Umar used to order the same to his companions who would recite the Quran to him because it entailed more action and was more favorable for contemplation, and its benefit reached others and was a means of awakening the hearts of the heedless.

The hadith reports cited in favor of both silent and audible remembrance have been reconciled, and the conclusion is that both methods depend on the person and the situation. Silence in remembrance is preferred for one who fears showing off or annoying someone, and when that is not feared, audible remembrance is preferred. In the legal verdicts (al-Bazaziyya), it states, “Audible remembrance should not be prevented in the Mosques, for fear of one being included in the verse And who is more wrong than he who prevents others from the mosques of Allah and remembering His name therein?” (Quran 2:114)

[Imam] al-Sha‘rani stated in his work Dhikr al-Dhakir lil-Madhkur wal-Shakir lil-Mashkur (The Remembrance of the One who Remembers the Remembered and the Gratitude of the Grateful to the One Thanked), “The scholars among the early forebears (Salaf) and those after them concurred in that it is recommended to remember Allah Most High within the mosques in congregation and alone—with no censure at all, unless their audible remembrance confuses someone engaged in prayer or someone reciting the Quran or (annoys) a sleeper—as is explained in the books of jurisprudence.”⁽²⁾

سؤال 31) أيهما أفضل: ذكر القلب أم ذكر اللسان بحسب الإمام الغزالي؟

الجواب: قال الإمام الغزالي رحمه الله تعالى: (اعلَم- يرحمكَ الله- أنه قَد انكشفَ لأربابِ البصائرِ أن الذِّكرَ أفضلُ الأعمال، ولكن لهُ أيضاً قشورٌ ثلاثةٌ بعضها أقربُ للُّب من بعضٍ، ولهُ لبُّ وراءَ القشورِ الثلاثة، وإنما فُضل القشورَ لكونِها طريقاً إليه، فالقشرُ الأعلى مِنه: ذكرُ اللسانِ فقط، والثاني: ذكرُ القلبِ إذا كانَ القلبُ يحتاجُ إلى موافقتهِ حتى يحضرَ مع الذّكر، ولو تُركَ وطبعهُ لاسترسلَ في أوديةِ الأفكار.
والثالث: أن يستمكنَ الذِّكرَ من القلبِ، ويستوليَ عليهِ بحيثُ يحتاجُ إلى تكلُّفٍ في صرفِه عنهُ إلى غيرِه، كما احتيجَ في الثاني إلى تكلُّف في قرارهِ معهُ ودوامهِ عَليه.

والرابع: وهو الّلباب، أن يستمكنَ المذكورُ من القلبِ، وينمَحي الذِّكر ويَخفى، وهو الّلباب المَطلوب، وذلكَ بأن لا يلتفِت إلى الذّكر ولا إلى القَلبِ بل يستغرقُ المذكورُ جُملتَه، ومَهما ظهرَ لهُ في أثناءِ ذلكَ التفاتٌ إلى الذكرِ فذلك حجابٌ شاغل. وهذه الحالةُ التي يعبِّر عنها العارفونَ بالفَناء... ثم قال رحمه الله تعالى: فهذهِ ثمرةُ لبابِ الذكر، وإنما مَبدؤها ذكرُ اللِّسان، ثم ذِكر القلبِ تكلفاً، ثم ذكرُ القلبِ طبعاً، ثم استيلاءُ المذكورِ وانمحاءُ الذِّكر.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 31: Which is superior, remembrance of the heart or remembrance of the tongue? And what has Imam al-Ghazali said about this question?
Answer: Imam al-Ghazali (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

Know—may Allah have mercy upon you—that it is has been unveiled to those with inner vision that remembrance is the best of deeds; however, it also has to it three husks, one of which is closer to the kernel than the others. Beyond these three husks there lies a kernel, and as such the husks are only considered virtuous on account of them being a means to arrive at the kernel. The first husk is remembrance with the tongue only. The second husk is remembrance of the heart when the heart is forced by its owner to engage in remembrance (which otherwise, if left on its own, would saunter in the valleys of idle thoughts. The third husk is that the remembrance takes root in the heart and overcomes it to the point where it takes effort to disengage from remembrance, just as it previously took effort to engage and persist in it. Then there is the kernel. This is when the One remembered is firmly in the heart and the remembrance disappears and vanishes; this is the kernel of what is sought, and it is accomplished by not giving notice to the remembrance or the heart, rather being completely absorbed in the One remembered. Whenever someone takes notice of his heart or the remembrance while in pursuit of this kernel, he should know that this is a veil that will preoccupy him (away from the Remembered). This state of (complete absorption in the One remembered) is referred to by the Gnostics as annihilation (fana’)… […]These are the fruits of the kernel of remembrance. The beginning stage is remembrance of the tongue, followed by the heart being forced to attentiveness, then remembrance of the heart by its own (newly subdued) nature, and then finally, the dominance of the One remembered and the effacement of the remembrance.⁽¹⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 32) أيهما أفضل، ذكر الجماعة أم الذكر المنفرد؟

الجواب: اﻟﻌِﺒﺎﺩﺍﺕ ﻣﻊ ﺍﻟﺠَﻤﺎﻋﺔ - ﻭﻓﻴﻬﺎ ﺫﻛﺮ ﺍﻟﻠﻪِ- ﺗﺰيدُ ﻓﻲ ﺍﻟﻔﻀﻞِ ﻋﻠَﻰ ﺍﻟﻌﺒﺎﺩﺓِ ﻓﻲ ﺣﺎﻟﺔِ ﺍﻻﻧﻔﺮﺍﺩ، ﻓَﻔﻲ ﺍﻟﺠﻤﺎﻋﺔِ ﺗﻠﺘَﻘﻲ ﺍﻟﻘﻠﻮﺏ، ﻭﻳﻜﻮﻥُ ﺍﻟﺘﻌﺎﻭﻥُ ﻭﺍﻟﺘﺠﺎﻭُﺏ، ﻭﻳﺴﺘَﻘﻲ ﺍﻟﻀّﻌﻴﻒ ﻣﻦ ﺍﻟﻘَﻮﻱ، والمظلمُ منَ النّور، والكثيفُ من اللطيف، ﻭﺍﻟﺠﺎﻫﻞُ ﻣﻦ ﺍﻟﻌﺎلِم... وهكذا.

عن سيدِنا أنسٍ رضيَ اللهُ عليهِ أن رسولَ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه قال: (ﺇﺫﺍ ﻣﺮَﺭﺗﻢ ﺑﺮﻳﺎﺽِ ﺍﻟﺠﻨّﺔ ﻓﺎﺭﺗَﻌﻮﺍ. ﻗﺎﻟﻮﺍ: ﻭَﻣﺎ ﺭﻳﺎﺽُ ﺍﻟﺠﻨﺔ؟ ﻗﺎﻝ: ﺣِﻠَﻖ ﺍﻟذِّكر).⁽¹⁾ وعن أبي هُريرَة رضوان الله عليه قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (قال رسولُ ﺍﻟﻠﻪِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: ﻣﺎ ﻣِن ﻗومٍ يذكُرونَ ﺍﻟﻠﻪَ إلا حفّتهم الملائِكَة، وغَشِيتهم الرَّحمة، ونزلَت عليهم السَّكينَة، وذَكرهُم اللهُ فيمَن عندَه).

وعَن مُعاوية رضوانُ اللهِ عليه أن النبيَّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه خرجَ على حلقةٍ مِن أصحابِه فقال: ( ما يُجلسكم؟ قالوا جَلسنا نذكرُ اللهَ تعالى ونحمدُه. فقالَ: أنه أتاني جِبريل فأخبَرني أنَّ اللهَ تَعالي يُباهى بكُم الملائكة).⁽²⁾ وعَن أنَس عليهِ الرّضوان قالَ: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلواتُ اللهِ وسلامُه عليه: (إن للهِ تعالى سيَّارة من الملائكَة يطلُبون حلق الذِّكر فإذا أَتوا عليهِم حفُّوا بهِم).⁽³⁾ وعَن أَنس عليهِ الرّضوان قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (إذا مَررتم برياضِ الجنَّةِ فارتَعوا، قالوا: وما رياضُ الجنَّةِ يا رسولَ الله؟ قال: حلق الذكر).⁽⁴⁾

قال العلاَّمة ابن علان شارحِ الأذكارِ في مَعنى هذا الحَديث والمعنى: إذا مَررتم بجماعةٍ يذكرونَ الله تَعالى فاذكُروا موافقة لَهم، أو اسمعوا أذكارَهم، فإنهم في رياضِ الجنةِ حالاً ومالاً.⁽⁵⁾ قال تعالى: ﴿وَلِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَام رَبّه جَنَّتَانِ﴾.⁽⁶⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 32: Which is superior, congregational or individual remembrance?

Answer: Acts of worship done congregationally—including the remembrance of Allah—are more virtuous than acts of worship done individually. In congregations, the hearts meet and there is mutual cooperation and harmony. In congregations, the weak seeks help from the strong, the unenlightened seeks help from the enlightened, the one of dense (spiritual veils) seeks help from the one who is light, and the ignorant seeks help from the knowledgeable, and so on.

Anas  related, “The Messenger of Allah  said, ‘When you pass by the gardens of Paradise, graze therein.’ The Companions asked, ‘O Messenger of Allah, what are the gardens of Paradise?’ He replied, ‘They are the circles of remembrance.’”⁽¹⁾

Mu’awiya  reported, “The Prophet  came across a circle of his Companions and inquired, ‘What causes you all to sit?’ They responded, ‘We have sat in order to remember and praise Allah.”’ He said, ‘Jibril came to me and informed me that Allah mentioned you with pride among His Angels!’”⁽²⁾
Anas  related, “The Messenger of Allah  said, ‘Indeed to Allah belongs Angels that travel in pursuit of the circles of remembrance. When they find people in them, they surround them.’”⁽³⁾

Concerning the hadith “When you pass by the gardens of Paradise, graze therein….” the erudite Ibn ‘Allan, author of a commentary on [Imam al-Nawawi’s] al-Adhkar, said about said about its meaning: “This means: when you pass by a group engaged in Allah’s remembrance, you should also engage in remembrance so as to be in conformity with them, or at least listen to their remembrance, for they are in a garden of Paradise either in their current state, or in their end state (i.e. after death). The Most Exalted said And he who fears my station shall have two gardens.” (Quran 55:46)⁽⁴⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 33) ما الفرق بين الذكر المقيد والذكر المطلق؟

الجواب: الذكر المقيد: هوَ الذي ندبَنا إليهِ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه مقيَّداً بزمانٍ أو مكانٍ خاص، كالذّكر بعدَ أداءِ كلِّ صَلاة، مِن تَسبيحٍ وتحميدٍ وتَكبير، وأذكارِ المُسافر والآكلِ والشارِب، وأذكارِ النِّكاح، وأذكارٍ تُقال عندَ الشدَّة ودفعِ الآفاتِ والمصائِب، وعندَ المرضِ والموتِ وما يتعلقُ بهما، وبعدَ صلاةِ الجمعَة وليلتِها، وعندَ رؤيةِ الهلال، وإِفطار الصائِم، وأذكارٌ تُقال في الصباحِ والمَساء، وعندَ النومِ والاستيقاظ، وأذكارُ الجهادِ في سبيلِ الله، وأذكارٌ متفرِّقة: عندَ صياحِ الدِّيك، ونَهيق الحِمار، وأذكارٍ عندَ رؤيةِ مُبتلى بمرضٍ وغيرِه. وهذه نبذٌ قليلةٌ من الأذكارِ المقيدَة، وإن أردتَ استيعابَها فارجِع إلى كتبِ الأَذكار.

وأمَّا الذكرُ المُطلق: فهوَ ما لم يُقيد بزمانٍ ولا مَكان، ولا وقتٍ ولا حال، ولا قيامٍ ولا قُعود، فالمطلوبُ من المؤمنِ أن يذكرَ ربَّه في كلِّ حالٍ حتى لا يَزال لسانهُ رطباً بذكرِ الله، والآيات في ذلكَ كَثيرة منها قوله تعالى: ﴿فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ﴾⁽¹⁾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿يُسَبِّحونَ الَّليلَ والنَّهارَ لا يَفْتُرُونَ﴾⁽²⁾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿يا أيُّها الَّذينَ آمَنوا اذكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثيراً وسَبِّحُوه بُكرَةً وأَصيلاً﴾ ⁽³⁾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَالذَّاكِرَاتِ أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾.⁽⁴⁾ وغيرها مِن الآياتِ التي تَدعو إلى الإكثارِ من ذكرِ الله مُطلقاً دون تقييدٍ بزمانٍ ومَكان، كما أن الرسولَ الأعظم صلوات الله وسلامه عليه ندَبنا إلى ذكرِ اللهِ مُطلقاً في جَميع أحوالِنا وأوقاتِنا. فقد روى عبد الله بن بسر رضوان الله تعالى عليه أن رجلاً قال: يا رسولَ الله: إن شرائعَ الإسلامِ قد كثُرت عليَّ، فأخبِرني بشيءٍ أتشبثُ به، قال: (لا يزالُ لسانُك رَطباً من ذكر الله).⁽⁵⁾ وقد وصفتِ السيدة عائشة رضوان الله تعالى عليها رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بقولِها: (كان رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يذكرُ اللهَ في كلِّ أحيانِه).⁽⁶⁾ وقد دَعانا عليه الصَّلاة والسَّلام في أحاديثَ كثيرةٍ إلى أنواعٍ من صيغِ الذكرِ من تسبيحٍ وتهليلٍ وتكبيرٍ واستِغفار، دون أن يحدِّد لها وَقتاً معيناً، أو مناسبةً خاصَّة.

قال ابن عباس رضوان الله عليهما: (لم يفرضِ الله تَعالى على عبادهِ فريضةً إلا جعلَ لها حداً معلوماً، ثم عذر أهلها في حالِ العذر، غَير الذّكر، فإنهُ لم يجعل لَه حداً ينتَهي إِليه، ولَم يعذر أحداً في تركهِ إلا مغلوباً على عقلِه، وأمرَهُم بذكرهِ في الأحوالِ كلِّها، فقالَ عزَّ من قائِل: ﴿فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِكُمْ﴾.⁽⁷⁾ وقال تعالى: ﴿يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا﴾⁽⁸⁾ أي بالليلِ والنَّهار، وفي البرِّ والبَحر، والسفرِ والحضَر، والغِنى والفَقر، وفي الصحَّة والسّقم، والسرِّ والعلانِية، وعلى كل حال⁽⁹⁾
وقد نهجَ السادَة الصوفيَّة على هذا المِنوال، فذكَروا اللهَ في جميعِ أحوالِهم وأطوارِهم.

وكما أنَّ الذكرَ منهُ مقيدٌ بزمَن، ومنه مطلقٌ عن ذلك، فكذلكَ الذكرُ منه مقيدٌ بعدَد، ومنهُ مطلقٌ عن العَدد، أما المقيّد بالعددِ فكالتسبيحِ دبرَ كلِّ صَلاة، وكالتَّحميدِ والتَّكبير: عَن أبي هريرةَ رضوان الله عليه، عن رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (مَنْ سبَّح اللهَ في دبرِ كلِّ صَلاة ثلاثاً وثَلاثين، وحمدَ اللهَ ثلاثاً وثَلاثين، وكبَّر اللهَ ثلاثاً وثَلاثين، فتلكَ تسعةُ وتِسعون، وقال تَمام المائة: لا إلهَ إلا اللهَ وحدهُ لا شريكَ لَه، لهُ الملكُ وله الحمدُ، وهوَ على كلِّ شيءٍ قَدير، غُفرت خَطاياهُ وإن كانَت مثلَ زبدِ البحر).⁽¹⁰⁾

وعن سعد بن أبي وقاص رضوان الله عليه قال: كنَّا عند رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه فقال: ( أيعجزُ أحدكم أن يَكسب في كلِّ يومٍ ألفَ حَسنة؟ فسألهُ سائلٌ من جلسائِه: كيفَ يكسبُ أحدنا ألفَ حَسنة ؟ قال: يُسبِّح مائة تسبيحَة فتُكتَب لهُ ألفُ حَسنة، أو تُحَط عَنه ألفُ خَطيئة). ⁽¹¹⁾
وعن الأغَرِّ بن يسار المُزَني رضوان الله عليه قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (يا أيُّها الناسُ توبوا إلى اللهِ واستَغفروه، فإنِّي أتوبُ في اليومِ مائة مرَّة).⁽¹²⁾

وعن أبي هريرة رضوان الله عليه أن رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه قال: (من قالَ لا إلهَ إلا اللهَ وحدهُ لا شريكَ له، لهُ المُلك ولهُ الحمدُ وهوَ وعلى كُل شيءٍ قَدير في يومٍ مائةَ مرَّة، كانَت له عدلَ عشرِ رِقاب، وكُتبت له مائةَ حَسنة، ومُحيت عنهُ مائةُ سيئة، وكانَت له حِرزاً منَ الشيطانِ يومهُ ذلك حتَّى يُمسي، ولم يأتِ أحدٌ بأفضلَ مما جاءَ به إلا رجلٌ عملَ أكثرَ مِنه). ⁽¹³⁾

يقول ابن علان في شرحهِ لهذا الحديث: (قالَ القاضي عياض: ذِكْرُ هذا العَدد من المِئة، وهذه الحصر لهذهِ الأذكار دليلٌ على أنَّها غاية وحدٍّ لهذهِ الأجور، ثم نبَّه صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بقوله: (ولَم يأتِ أحدٌ بأفضلَ مما جاءَ بهِ إلا رجلٌ عملَ أكثرَ مِنه) إلى أنه يَجوز أن يُزاد على هذا العَدد، فيكونَ لقائلهِ من الفضلِ بحسبِ ذلك لِئلا يُظنّ أنها مِن الحدودِ التي نُهي عَن اعتدائِها، وأنَّه لا فضلَ للزيادةِ عَليها كالزيادةِ على ركعاتِ السننِ المحدودةِ وأعدادِ الطهارة. قال ابن الجوزي: وهذا غلطٌ ظاهِر، وقولٌ لا يُلتفت إِليه، بَل الصوابُ أنه كَما قال الشاعر: ومن زاد زادَ اللهُ في حسناتِه).⁽¹⁴⁾

وأما الذّكر المطلق عَن العدَد: فهوَ الذي وجهَنا الله تَعالى إلى الإكثارِ منهُ في جميعِ أحوالِنا وأوقاتِنا دونَ تقييدِه بعددٍ مخصوص، كما في قولهِ تَعالى: ﴿يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا﴾⁽¹⁵⁾ وكلَّما علَت همةُ المؤمنِ وزادَت محبتهُ للهِ تَعالى أكثرَ من ذِكرِه، لأنَّ من أحبَّ شيئاً أكثرَ من ذِكرِه.
لا بأسَ للمرشدِ الموجهِ أن يُرغِّبَ المريدَ بأعدادٍ معينةٍ من الأذكارِ ليرفعَ من همتهِ ويشُد مِن عزيمَته، ويدفعَ عنهُ الإهمالَ والتَّقاعس، وحتَّى يكونَ من المكثرينَ من ذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 33: What is the difference between limited remembrance and unlimited remembrance?

Answer: Limited remembrance is the remembrance that the Messenger of Allah encouraged us to do, which is limited to particular times or places, such as the remembrances said after offering the prescribed prayers (the glorification (tasbih), praise (tahmid), and declaration of Allah’s greatness (takbir)), and the remembrances said when going on a journey, when eating and drinking, when getting married; the remembrance said at times of difficulty and when seeking to avert calamities and afflictions; remembrance said during times of illness and upon someone’s death and related matters; remembrance said after the Friday prayer and the night before it; remembrance said when seeing the new crescent; remembrance said when one breaks his or her fast; remembrance said in the morning and the evening and when going to sleep and when waking up; remembrance said when fighting in the path of Allah; and miscellaneous forms of remembrance, such as when hearing the crowing of a rooster or the braying of a donkey, when seeing someone tested with an illness and the like—these are but some of the forms of limited remembrance. If you want to gain familiarity with them you may refer to the books on remembrances.

As for the unlimited remembrance, it is the remembrance that is not restricted by a set time, place, or condition, and is not limited to standing or sitting. What is sought from the believer is that he remembers his Lord in every condition such that his tongue never ceases being moist with Allah’s remembrance. There are several Quranic verses that speak to this. The Most High says, So remember Me and I will remember you (Quran 2:152). He also says, The glorify Him by night and day and never slacken… (Quran 21:20) He also says, O you who believe, remember Allah abundantly, and glorify Him by morning and night… (Quran 33:41–42) And the Most High says, And those men and women who remember Allah abundantly—Allah has prepared from them forgiveness and a magnanimous reward! (Quran 33:35)—in addition to many other verses that invite towards abundant remembrance of Allah unrestrictedly and without limitation by time or place.

Like limited remembrance, the Messenger of Allah  encouraged us to do unlimited remembrance in all of our states and moments. ‘Abdullah b. Busr  related, “A man once said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, the laws of Islam have become many for me. Please inform me of something I can stick to.’ The Messenger of Allah  replied, ‘See to it that your tongue remains moist in the remembrance of Allah.’”⁽¹⁾ Lady ‘A’isha  described the Messenger of Allah , saying, “The Messenger of Allah  used to remember Allah in all of his moments.”⁽²⁾ In several hadith reports the Messenger of Allah  invited us to various forms of remembrance—glorification, praise, extolment, seeking forgiveness, etc.— without limiting them to a set time or circumstance.

Ibn ‘Abbas k said, “Allah Most High has not obligated anything upon His servants except that He made for it a set limit and excused those who were in a excusable state—except for remembrance, for He did not give it a limit nor did he excuse anyone in leaving it, except for the one whose intellect is overcome. He ordered them to make remembrance of Him in all states: Remember Allah while standing, sitting, and lying upon your sides. (Quran 4:103) And He said, O you who believe! Remember Allah in abundance. (Quran 33:41)This means to remember Him night and day; on the ocean and upon the land; in travel and in residence; in poverty and affluence; in sickness and in health; in secret and in public; in every state.”⁽³⁾
And thus, the Sufis have proceeded in this manner, remembering Allah in all of their states and situations.

Just as there is remembrance restricted to a specific time and that which is unrestricted, similarly there is remembrance restricted to a specific number and that which is not. That which is restricted to a certain number includes the number of glorifications, praises, and declarations of Allah’s greatness (tasbih, tahmid, and takbir) said at the end of the prescribed prayers.

Abu Hurayra  related that the Messenger of Allah  said, ‘Whosoever, after the prescribed prayer, glorifies Allah thirty-three times, praises Allah thirty-three times, declares Allah’s greatness thirty-three times—and that is ninety-nine times in total—and then completes one hundred by saying There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners, to Him belongs the dominion and all praise, and He has power over all things—his sins will be forgiven even if they were as numerous as the foam of the sea!”⁽⁴⁾

Sa‘d b. Abi Waqqas  reported, “We were with the Messenger of Allah  one day when he asked, ‘Are any of you incapable of acquiring one thousand good deeds each day?’ Someone in the gathering asked him, ‘How can we earn one thousand good deeds?’ He replied, ‘Whosoever says one hundred times ‘I declare Allah exaltedly transcendent’ (subhanallah) shall have either one thousand good deeds written for him, or have one thousand sins removed.’”⁽⁵⁾

Aghar b. Yassar al-Muzani related that the Messenger of Allah  said, “O people! Repent unto Allah and seek His forgiveness, for certainly I turn to Allah in repentance over one hundred times a day.”⁽⁶⁾

Abu Hurayra  related that the Messenger of Allah  said, “Whosoever says one hundred times in a day There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners, to Him belongs the dominion and all praise, and He has power over all things, shall have the reward equivalent to freeing ten slaves, and shall have written for him one hundred good deeds and have removed from him one hundred sins, and it will be a protection for him against Satan for his entire day until evening arrives. No one brings better than what he has done except the one who does more than him.”⁽⁷⁾
Ibn ‘Allan said in explanation of this hadith:

Qadi ‘Iyad said, “This mention and restriction of one hundred in the remembrance is a proof that it is the amount required for attaining this reward. But then he  informed that ‘No one brings better than what he has done save the one who does more than him’—thus showing that it is permissible to add more to this number, with such a person attaining virtue commensurate with that number (lest it be thought that this number represents the uppermost limit that one is forbidden from going past, and that there is no virtue in doing more than it, like adding additional units to the Sunna prayers that have predetermined numbers, or the number of times one washes the limbs in ritual purification). Some have exaggerated and claimed that the reward promised [in this hadith] is preconditioned on the exact number mentioned. Ibn al-Jawzi said, “This is a clear fallacy and a statement that should not be given any consideration whatsoever. The correct view regarding this is, as the poet once said, ‘He who increases, Allah shall increase him in reward.’”⁽⁸⁾

As for the unlimited remembrance that is not restricted to a set number, it is the remembrance that Allah encouraged us to do in all of our conditions and times, without restricting ourselves to a predetermined number. He said, O you who believe, Remember Allah abundantly.)(Quran 33:41) The higher the believer’s aspiration rises and the stronger his love is for Allah, the more he engages in His remembrance, for the one who loves something remembers it often. There is no problem with the spiritual guide instructing a seeker to engage in a predetermined amount of remembrance in order to raise his aspirations, strengthen his resolve, and avert laziness and neglect so he is among those who are frequent in the remembrance of Allah Most High.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 34) ما حكم الذكر بالاسم المفرد: الله؟

الجواب: بدليلِ قولِ الله تعالى: ﴿وَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ وَتَبَتَّلْ إِلَيْهِ تَبْتِيلاً﴾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿وَاذْكُرِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلا﴾ ⁽¹⁾ وقد روى في الحديثِ الشريفِ الذي رواهُ أنس بن مالك عن النبيِّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (لا تقومُ الساعة حتَّى لا يُقال في الأرضِ: الله الله)⁽²⁾ فهذا اسمٌ مفردٌ وردَ ذكرهُ مكرراً في هذا الحَديث... وفي روايةٍ أخرى عن أنس رضوان الله عليه قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (لا تقومُ الساعةُ على أحدٍ يقول: الله الله).

قال العلامة علي القاري في شرح هذا الحديث: (أي: لا يُذكر اللهُ فلا يَبقى حكمةٌ في بقاءِ النَّاس، ومِن هذا يُعرَف أن بقاءَ العالمِ ببركةِ العلماءِ العامِلين، والعُباد الصالِحين وعمومِ المُؤمنين، وهوَ المُرادُ بما قال الطيبي رحمه الله تعالى: معنى حتى لا يقال: (الله الله)، حتى لا يُذكر اسمُ اللهِ ولا يُعبد".
ثمَّ إن الآياتِ الكريمةِ والأحاديثِ الشريفةِ التي رغَّبت في الذكرِ جاءَت عامةً ومطلقةً لم تخصص ذكراً معيناً، ولَم يرِد نص شَرعي يحرِّم الذّكر بالاسمِ المفرَد "الله".

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 34: What is the ruling on invoking with the Solitary Name (al-Ism al-Mufrad), Allah?

Answer: It is permissible to invoke the Solitary Name (Allah), and it is proven from the words of Allah, Remember the name of your Lord and dedicate yourself wholly to Him. (Quran 73:8), and, Remember the name of your Lord in the morning and evening. (Quran 76:25) In the noble hadith narrated by Anas b. Malik , the Prophet  said, “The Last Hour will not be established until there is no longer anyone on the earth who says ‘Allah, Allah.’” Here we see the Solitary Name mentioned in repetition. In another narration from Anas, he reported, “The Messenger of Allah  said, ‘The Last Hour will not be established upon anyone who is saying “Allah, Allah.”’”⁽¹⁾
The erudite ‘Ali al-Qari said in explanation of this hadith:

This means [the Last Hour will not arrive] until Allah is no longer remembered—for there is no wisdom after that for the people to remain. From this we come to know that the world remains because of the blessings of the practicing scholars, the righteous worshippers, and generality of the believers. This is the intent of al-Tibi’s statement (Allah have mercy upon him), when he said, “The meaning of his statement ‘…until it is no longer said “Allah, Allah”’ means until the name of Allah is no longer remembered and He is no longer worshipped.⁽²⁾

Furthermore, the noble Quranic verses and Prophetic hadith reports that encourage remembrance have appeared in a general and unrestricted form, and no specific remembrance was identified, and what’s more, there is no revealed text that forbids invoking the Solitary Name (Allah).

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 35) ما هي مرتبة اسم الجلالة عند جمهرة العلماء؟

الجواب: قال العلامة الخادمي في ذلك (اعلم- يرحمكَ الله- أن اسمَ الجلالة "الله" هوَ الاسمُ الأعظمُ عند أبي حنيفة والكسائي والشعبي وإسماعيل بن إسحاق وأبي حفص وسائرِ جمهورِ العُلماء وهو اعتقادُ جماهيرِ مشايخِ الصوفيّة ومحقِّقي العارِفين، فإنه لا ذكرَ عندَهم لصاحبِ مقامٍ فوقَ مقامِ الذّكرِ باسمِ "الله" مجرداً، قالَ الله لنبيِّه صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (قل الله: ثم ذرهم ).

وقال العارفُ باللهِ سيدي ابن عجمية: فالاسمُ المفرد (الله) هوَ سلطانُ الأسماء، وهوَ اسمُ اللهِ الأعظَم، ولا يزالُ المريد يذكرهُ بلسانهِ ويهتزُّ بهِ حتى يمتزجَ بلحمهِ ودمِه، وتَسري أنوارهُ في كلياتهِ وجزئياتِ، إلى أن قال: " فينتقلُ الذكرَ إلى القلبِ ثم إلى الروحِ ثم إلى السّر، فحينئذٍ يخرس اللسانُ ويصلُ إلى الشهودِ والعيان.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 35: What is the status of the Majestic Name (Allah) according to the majority of scholars?
Answer: The erudite al-Khadimi said:

Know—may Allah have mercy upon you—that according to Abu Hanifa, al-Kasa’i, al-Sha’bi, Isma’il ibn Ishaq, Abu Hafs, and indeed the majority of scholars, the Majestic Name Allah is the Supreme Name. It is the firm conviction of most of the Shaykhs of the Sufis and the realized gnostics. According to them, there is no remembrance for the possessor of spiritual stations beyond the station of remembering the name Allah alone. Allah said to His Prophet , Say “Allah” and leave them… (Quran 6:91) The knower of Allah, Sayyidi Ibn ‘Ajiba said:

The Solitary Name Allah is the sultan of all other names, and it is His Supreme Name. The seeker does not cease remembering It with his tongue and moving with It until It mixes with his flesh and blood and Its lights cause him to ascend to Its universals and particulars…until finally the remembrance moves to the heart, then to the spirit, then to the secret, whereupon the tongue falls silent and he reaches [the rank of] witnessing and intellective vision.⁽¹⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 36) ما مغبة ترك ذكر الله تبارك وتعالى؟
الجواب: لقد حذَّر الله تباركَ وتَعالى مِن تركِ ذكرهِ في كتابهِ الكَريم وعلى لسانِ رسولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، كما حذَّرَ العارفونَ باللهِ من المربينَ المرشدينَ مريديهِم من تركِ الذِّكرِ كذلك.

أما في كتابِ اللهِ الكريم: فقد قال تعالى: ﴿وَمَن يَعْشُ عَن ذِكْرِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ نُقَيِّضْ لَهُ شَيْطَانًا فَهُوَ لَهُ قَرِينٌ ﴿36﴾ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ ﴾⁽¹⁾ وقال تعالى: ﴿اذكُر رَبَّكَ في نَفسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخيفَةً وَدونَ الجَهرِ مِنَ القَولِ بِالغُدُوِّ وَالآصالِ وَلا تَكُن مِنَ الغافِلينَ﴾⁽²⁾ وقالَ في ذمِّ المُنافقين: ﴿وَلَا يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا﴾.⁽³⁾

وأما في سنة رسولِ اللهِ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه: فقد رويَ عن أبي هُريرة رضوان الله عليه قال: قالَ رسول اللهِ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه: (ما منْ قومٍ يقومونَ من مجلسٍ لا يذكرونَ فيهِ الله إلا قاموا عَن مثلِ جيفة حِمار، وكانَ عليهم حسرةً يومَ القِيامة).⁽⁴⁾
وعَنه أيضاً قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه: (من قعدَ مقعداً لَم يذكرِ اللهَ فيهِ كانَ عليهِ من الله تِرةً، وما مَشي أحدٌ ممشىً لا يذكرُ اللهَ فيه إلا كانَ عليهِ من الله ترة).⁽⁵⁾
وعنه أيضاً: قال رسول الله رضوان الله وسلامه عليه: (ما جلسَ قومٌ مجلساً لَم يَذكروا اللهَ فيه، ولم يُصلُّوا على نبيِّهم، إلا كانَ عليهِم تِرةً فإن شاءَ عذَّبهم، وإن شاءَ غفرَ لَهم).⁽⁶⁾
وعَن معاذ بن جبلَ رضوان الله عليه قال: قالَ رسول اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (ليسَ يتحسَّر أهل الجنةِ إلا على ساعةٍ مرَّت بهم لَم يذكرُوا اللهَ فيها).⁽⁷⁾
وأما ما ورد من أقوالِ العارِفين:
فقد قال سهل: (ما أعلمُ معصيةً أقبح من تركِ ذكرِ هذا الرّب).

وقال أبو الحَسن الشاذِلي رضي الله عنه: (من علامةِ النفاقِ ثقل الذكرِ على اللسان، فتُب إلى اللهِ تعالى يخفُّ الذّكر على لِسانك). كأنَّه اقتبِس ذلك من وصفِ الله تَعالى للمنافقين: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يُخَادِعُونَ اللّهَ وَهُوَ خَادِعُهُمْ وَإِذَا قَامُواْ إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ قَامُواْ كُسَالَى يُرَآؤُونَ النَّاسَ وَلاَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللّهَ إِلاَّ قَلِيلاً﴾⁽⁸⁾

وقيل: (لكلِّ شيءٍ عقوبة، وعقوبةُ العارفِ انقطاعهِ عن الذّكر( .. فعَلى العاقلِ أن ينتبَّه مِن غَفلته، وأن يَسعى جاداً في إيقاظِ قلبهِ بذكرِ ربِّه، متصفاً بصفةِ المؤمنينَ الذاكرينَ اللهَ كثيراً، بعيداً عن صفةِ المنافقينَ الذي لا يذكرونَ اللهَ إلا قليلاً.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 36: What is the negative effect of neglecting the remembrance of Allah?

Answer: Allah Most High, in His Noble Book upon the tongue of His Messenger ﷺ, warned His servants from neglecting remembrance. Likewise, the gnostics among the spiritual trainers and guides warned their disciples from leaving it. As for the Quran, Allah Most High said, Whosoever abandons the remembrance of the All-Merciful, We shall appoint for him a Satan that shall serve as his consort. Indeed they prevent others from the path and reckon that they are rightly guided. (Quran 43:36–37)⁽¹⁾ He also said, Remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear, without being audible in speech, and do not be among the heedless. (Quran 7:205)⁽²⁾ And He said, rebuking the hypocrites, They remember Allah naught but a little. (Quran 4:142)⁽³⁾

As for the Sunna, Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “There are no people who get up from a gathering in which they did not engage in Allah’s remembrance, except that it is as if they stood up from the dead carcass of a donkey, and they shall feel immense regret on the Day of Judgment.”⁽⁴⁾

Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه also related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Whosoever sits down in a place without remembering Allah therein, he shall have a misdeed from Allah; whosoever lies down somewhere without remembering Allah, he shall have a misdeed from Allah; and whosoever walks somewhere without remembering Allah in his walk, he shall have a misdeed from Allah.”⁽⁵⁾

Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه also related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “No people sit in a gathering while not remembering Allah or sending prayers upon their Prophet, except they will have a regret and a reckoning—if He (Allah) wills, He may punish them, and if He wills, He may forgive them.”⁽⁶⁾

Mu‘adh b. Jabal رضي الله عنه related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “The inhabitants of Paradise shall not have any regrets save for that hour which passed them by when they were not engaged in Allah’s remembrance.”⁽⁷⁾
As for the statements of the knowers of Allah, Sahl b. ‘Abdullah al-Tustari said, “I know of no other disobedience uglier than forsaking remembrance of the Lord.”

Abu al-Hasan al-Shadhili رضي الله عنه said, “Among the signs of hypocrisy is the heaviness of remembrance upon the tongue; so repent unto Allah Most High and He shall make remembrance light upon your tongue.” It is as if Abu al-Hasan al-Shadhili deduced his saying from Allah’s description of the hypocrites: Indeed, the hypocrites seek to deceive Allah, whereas it is He who deceives them; and when they stand for prayer they stand lazily, seeking to be seen by men, and they do not remember Allah save a little. (Quran 4:142)⁽⁸⁾
It has been said, “Everything has its punishment, and the punishment of the gnostic is being cut off from Allah’s remembrance.”

One who is intelligent must wake up from his heedlessness and strive with seriousness of purpose to revive and awaken his heart to remember his Lord. He must take on the attributes of the believers who remember Allah in abundance. He must be far removed from the character traits of the hypocrites who do not remember Allah but a little.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 37) هل تجوز الحركة في الذكر وما دليل ذلك؟

الجواب: الحركةُ في الذكِر أمرٌ مستحسَن، لأنها تنشِّط الجسمَ لعبادةِ الذكر وهي جائزةٌ شرعاً، بدليل ما أخرجهُ الإمام أحمد في مسندهِ و"الحافظ المقدسي" بـ "رجال الصحيح" من حديث أنس رضوان الله عليه قال: (كانَت الحبشة يرقصونَ بينَ يدي رسولِ اللهِ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه، ويقولونَ بكلامٍ لهم: "محمد" عبدٌ صالح ( فلما رآهم في تلكَ الحالةِ لم ينكِر عليهِم، وأقرَّهم على ذلك، والمَعلوم أن الأحكامَ الشرعيَّة تؤخذُ من قوله رضوان الله وسلامه عليه وفعلهِ وتقريرِه، فلمَّا أقرَّهم على فعلهِم ولم ينكِر عليهِم تبيَّن أن هذا جائِز. وفي الحديثِ دليلٌ على صحةِ الجمعِ بين الاهتزازِ المُباح ومدحِ رسولِ اللهِ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه، وأنَّ الاهتزازَ في الذّكر لا يُسمَّى رَقصاً محرماً، بَل هو جائزٌ لأنه ينشِّط الجسمَ للذّكر، ويساعدُ على حضورِ القلبِ مع اللهِ تَعالى إذا صحَّت النيَّة. فالأمورُ بمقاصدِها "وإنما الأعمالُ بالنيات وإنَّما لكلِّ امرىءٍ ما نَوى".

ولنِستمع إلى الإمامِ عليّ رضوان الله عليه كيفَ يصفُ أصحاب النبيِّ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه: قال أبو أرأكة: (صلّيتُ معَ "علي" صلاةَ الفَجر، فلما انفتلَ عن يمينهِ مكثَ كأنَّ عليه كآبَة، حتى إذا كانتِ الشمسُ على حائطِ المسجدِ قيدَ رُمح صلَّى ركعَتين، ثم قلبَ يَده فقال: (واللهِ لقد رأيتُ أصحابَ محمدٍ رضوان الله وسلامه عليه، فما أَرى اليومَ شيئاً يشبههُم، لقَد كانوا يصبحونَ صفراً شعثاً غُبراً، بين أيديهِم كأمثالِ رُكَب المَعْزى، قد باتوا للهِ سجداً وقياماً، يَتلون كتابَ اللهِ يتراوحونَ بينَ جباههِم وأقدامهِم، فإذا أصبحوا فَذكروا اللهَ مادوا "أي تحركوا" كما يميدُ الشجرُ في يومِ الرّيح، فإنّكَ تجده صريحاً في الاهتزاز ويبطلُ قول من يدعي أنه بدعة محرمة، ويثبت إباحةِ الحركةِ في الذكر مطلقاً. وقد استدلَّ الشيخ عبد الغني النابلسي رحمه الله في هذا الحديثِ في إحدى رسائلهِ على ندبِ الاهتزازِ في الذكر، وقال: هذا صحيحٌ بأن الصحابةَ رضوان الله عليهم كانوا يتحرّكونَ حركةً شديدةً في الذّكرِ، على أن الرجلَ غير مؤاخذٍ حينَ يتحرّك، ويقومُ ويقعُد على أي نوعٍ كان، حيث أنهُ لم يأتِ بمعصيةٍ ولم يقصدها كما ذكرنا.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 37: Is movement permitted during invocation?

Answer: Movement during invocation is a good thing because it energizes the body for that act of worship. Movement during invocation is permissible in the Sacred Law, as is proven by the narration of Ahmad in his Musnad, and al-Maqdisi, with sound narrators from the hadith of Anas رضي الله عنه who reported, “One day the Abyssinians were dancing in front of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, saying in their language, ‘Muhammad is a righteous servant!’ He ﷺ asked, ‘What are they saying?’ It was said, ‘[They are saying], “Muhammad is a righteous servant.”’”

When he saw them in that state he did not censure them. Rather, he approved of their act. It is known that rulings in the Sacred Law are taken from the Prophet’s statements, actions, and tacit approvals ﷺ. Since the Prophet ﷺ approved of their action and did not censure them, it is clear that it is permissible.

This hadith proves that it is permissible to gather between permissible movement and exaltation of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. It also proves that movement during remembrance is not described as forbidden dance; rather, it is permissible because it energizes the body for remembrance and assists one in having presence of heart with Allah—provided one’s intention is sound, for deeds are according to their ends and “Actions are only governed by their intentions, and every person shall have what he intended.”

Let us pay close attention to the statement of Imam ‘Ali رضي الله عنه who described the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ: Abu Araka reported, “On one occasion I prayed the morning prayer with ‘Ali. When he moved from his position, he remained sitting as if he was sad. When the rays of the sun shone upon the wall of the mosque, covering the span of a spear, he [stood and] offered two units of prayer. Afterwards, he wrung his hands and said, ‘I have seen the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. I see no one resembling them today! By Allah, they used to rise in the morning disheveled, dust-covered, pale, with something between their eyes resembling a goat knee [marks of prostration], as they had spent the night reciting Allah’s Book, turning from their feet to their foreheads [in prayer]. If Allah was mentioned, they swayed as trees sway on a windy day, then their eyes would pour out tears until—by Allah!—they soaked their clothes.’”⁽¹⁾

The expression of Imam ‘Ali that concerns us here is his statement, “… they swayed as trees sway on a windy day…” This is explicit in stating that it was movement, and it repudiates the view of those who said it is a forbidden, blameworthy innovation. This statement also establishes the unrestricted permissibility of movement during remembrance.

This hadith was used as evidence by Shaykh ‘Abd al-Ghani al-Nabulusi (Allah have mercy upon him) in one of his essays that he wrote in support of movement during remembrance. He said, “This states explicitly that the Companions used strong movements during remembrance; and this is based upon the fact that one is not taken to account when he moves, stands, and sits down in anyway because he has not committed an act of disobedience or intended one, as we have mentioned.”

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 38) اذكر قصيدة الشيخ أبي مدين رضي الله عنه في الحث على الحركة في الذكر؟
الجواب: قال الشيخ أبو مدين رحمه الله تعالى في قصيدته:
وقـل للـذي يـنهى عـن الـوجـــــــد أهلَه إذا لـم تذقْ معـنى شـرابِ الهـوى دَعْنــــا
إذا اهتزَّت الأرواحِ شـوقًا إلى اللقــــــا نعـم تـرقصُ الأشبـاحُ يـا جـاهل الـمعـنى؟
أمـا تـنظرُ الطـير الـمُقفَّصَ يـــــــا فتى إذا ذكر الأوطـانَ حنَّ إلى الـــــــــمغنى
ويرقصُ فـي الأقفـاص شـوقًا إلى اللقـــــا فتهتزُّ أربـابُ العقــــــــــــول إذا غنَّى
كذلك أرواح الـمحـبـيـن يــــــــــا فتى تُهـزْهِزُهـا الأشـواقُ للعـالــــــم الأسنى
أتلزمُهـا بـالصـبر وهـي مشـــــــــــوقةٌ  فهل يستطـيع الصـبرَ مـن شـاهد الـمعــنى؟
فـيـا حـاديَ العـشـاق قـمْ واحْدُ قــــائمًا وزمزِمْ لنـا بـاسمِ الـحـبـيب وحـيِّنــــــا

وبعد فتحِ خيبر قدِم من الحبشةِ جعفر بن أبي طالب رضوان الله عليه ومن معهُ من المسلِمين وهم ستة عشرَ رجلاً، فتلَقى النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه لـ جعفر وقبَّل جبهتهُ وعانقهُ وقامَ له ـ وقد قامَ لـ صفوان بن أُمية لما قدمَ عليه، ولـ عدي بن حاتم رضوان الله عليهما، ثم قالَ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (ما أدري بأيّهما أفرحُ بفتحِ خيبرَ أم بقدومِ جَعفر) وقال صلوات الله وسلامه عليه لـ جعفر: (أشبهتَ خَلْقي وخُلُقي) فرقصَ رضوان الله عليه من لذةِ هذا الخِطاب، فلَم ينكِر صلوات الله وسلامه عليه رقصَه، وجُعلَ ذلك أصلاً لرقصِ الصوفيَّة عندما يجدونَ من لذّة المواجيدِ في مجالسِ الذكرِ والسّماع).⁽¹⁾

وقال العلامة الألوسي في تفسيرهِ عند قولهِ تعالى: ﴿الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ﴾: وعليهِ يُحمل ما حُكي عن ابن عمر رضوان الله عليهما وعروة بن الزبير وجماعة رضوان الله عليهم أجمعين، من أنَّهم خَرجوا يومَ العيدِ إلى المُصلى، فجعلوا يذكرونَ الله تَعالى، فقالَ بعضهم: أمَا قالَ الله تعالى: ﴿يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا﴾؟ فقاموا يذكرونَ الله تَعالى على أقدامهِم، على أنّ مُرادهم بذلكَ التبرّك بنوعِ موافقةٍ للآيةِ في ضمنِ فردٍ من أفرادِ مدلولِها.⁽²⁾

ويُفهم مما سبقَ أن الحركةَ في الذّكرِ مباحةٌ شرعاً، هذا بالإضافةِ إلى أن الأمرَ بالذكرِ مطلقٌ يشملُ جميعَ الأحوال، فمن ذكرَ اللهَ تَعالى قاعِداً أو قائِماً، جالِساً أو ماشِياً، متحرِّكاً أو ساكِناً... فقد قامَ بالمطلوبِ ونفَّذَ الأمرَ الإلهِي.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 38: Can you quote for us the lines from the ode of Shaykh Abu Madyan رضي الله عنه that encourage movement in invocation?
Answer: Shaykh Abu Madyan (Allah have mercy upon him) said in his ode:

Say to the one who censures the people [the Sufis] on account of ecstasy:
Leave us alone if you haven’t tasted the meaning
Of drinking from the fountain of longing

Yes, O you who is ignorant of its meaning!
When the souls move out of longing for the tryst
The physical bodies dance

O young man, don’t you see the caged bird
That when its home is mentioned, it begins to sing?
By singing, it releases what’s in its heart
And the limbs become shaky, physically and spiritually
That, young man, is also the state of lovers’ souls!

Longing for the higher realms constantly shakes them
Do we force them to be patient while they are in a state of longing?
And is the one who witnesses the spiritual meaning able to be patient?

So stand upright, O driver of the lovers!
And murmur for us in the name of the Beloved and put us at ease!

Consider also the statement of the Mufti of the esteemed Shafi‘is in Mecca the Ennobled, the erudite Ahmad Zayni Dahlan (Allah have mercy upon him), who, in his famous book on the Prophetic biography, cited a scene of the Companions. In the margins, he stated:

After the conquest of Khaybar, Ja‘far b. Abi Talib رضي الله عنه returned from Abyssinia along with some of the Muslims—some sixteen men in total. Ja‘far then met with the Prophet ﷺ, kissed his forehead, and embraced him. The Prophet ﷺ stood for him, just as he had stood for Safwan b. Umayya and ‘Adi b. Hatim when they came to him (from travel), then he said, “I do not know which of the two things I am the most delighted about, the conquest of Khaybar, or the return of Ja‘far!” Then he said to Ja‘far, “You resemble me in both my manners and my physical form.” Upon hearing this, Ja‘far رضي الله عنه danced from the sweetness of what was said, and the Prophet ﷺ did not censure him for his dancing. This has served as the basis for the dancing of the Sufis when they experience ecstasy in the gatherings of invocation and audition.⁽¹⁾
The erudite al-Alusi said in his Quranic exegesis, commenting on the words of the Most High, Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and while upon their sides…) (Quran 3:191):

In light of this, the narration of Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما, ‘Urwa b. al-Zubayr, and a group of Companions is understood, where it is narrated that they went out to the prayer-place on the day of Eid (celebration), and started to remember Allah. Some of them said, “Didn’t Allah say, Those who remember Allah standing and sitting…?” Then they stood up and remembered Allah while on their feet. This narration indicates that by this act, they intended to seek blessings through a way of conforming to the verse by (performing) one aspect that it indicates.⁽²⁾

From what has been mentioned, it is understood that movement during invocation is permissible. This is especially so when considering that the command to invoke [Allah] is unrestricted and encompasses every state. Whoever remembers Allah Most High while sitting or standing, resting or walking, or moving or still, is carrying out what is sought from him, and is complying with the Divine command.

The legal ruling on singing and audition

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 39) ما دليل جواز الإنشاد والسماع في المسجد؟

الجواب: عن أبي بن كعب رضوان اللهِ عليه قال: (إن من الشعر لحكمة)⁽¹⁾ وعن أنَس رضوانُ الله عليه أن رسولَ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه كانَ ينقلُ اللبنَ مع القومِ في بناءِ المسجدِ وهم يرتجِزون ويقولونَ: (اللهمَّ لا عيشٌ إلا عيشُ الآخرة فارحَم الأنصارَ والمهاجرَة ).⁽²⁾

وعن سلمة بن الأكوع رضوان الله عليه قال: خَرجنا مع النبيّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه إلى خيبرَ فسِرنا ليلاً، فقالَ رجلٌ من القومِ لـ عامِر : يا عامر ألا تُسمعنا من هنيهاتِك؟ وكانَ عامِر رجلاً شاعراً ، فنزلَ يحدوا بالقومِ يقول :
اللهم لولا أنت ما اهتدينا   ولا تصدقنا ولا صلينا
فاغفر فداء لك ما أبقينا   وثبت الأقدام إن لاقينا
وألقين سكينة علينا   أنا إذا أصيح بنا أبينا
وبالصياح عولوا علينا
فقالَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (من هذا السَّائق؟ قالوا: عامر بن الأكوع قال: يرحمه الله فقال لرجلٍ من القوم: وجبت يا رسولَ الله، لولا أمتعتَنا به، فَأصيب).⁽³⁾

وعن سعيد بن المسيب قال: (مرَّ عُمرُ في المسجدِ و"حسان" يُنشد، فلحِظَه عُمر "أي نظرَ إليه نظرةَ إِنكار"، فقالَ: كنتُ أُنشد وفيهِ من هوَ خيرٌ مِنك "يريد رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه" ثم التفتَ إلى أبي هُريرة فقال: (أُنشِدُك باللهِ أسمعتَ رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يقول: “أجِب عني، اللهمَّ أيده بروحِ القدُس؟” قال: نعم).⁽⁴⁾

وعن عائشة رضوان الله عليها: كان النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يضعُ لـ حسان منبراً في المسجدِ يقومُ عليهِ قائماً يُفاخر عن رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، ويقولُ الرسولُ عليه السلام: (إن اللهَ يؤيدُ حسانَ برُوح القدُس ما نافحَ أو فاخرَ عن رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه).
يقولُ العلامة السفاريني شارح منظومة الآداب: (وفي روايةِ أبي بَكر بن الأنباري، أنَّ كعب بن زهير لما جاءَ تائباً وقالَ قصيدتهُ المَشهورة:
بانت سعادُ فقلبي اليومَ متْبولُ   مُتَيَّمٌ إثرَها لم يُفْدَ مكبولُ
إلى أن وصل:
إن الرسول لسيفٌ يُستضاء به  مُهنَّدٌ من سيوف الهند مسلولُ

رَمى إليه صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بردةً كانت عَليه، وأنَّ معاوية بذلَ فيها عشرةَ آلاف فقال: ما كنتُ لأوثرَ بثوبِ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه أحداً، فلمَّا ماتَ كعب بعثَ معاوية إلى رثتهِ بعشرينَ ألفاً فأخذَها منهُم... إلى أَن قال: تَحصَّل من إنشادِ قصيدةِ كعب بن زهير رضوان الله عليه بين يدَي رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه وإعطائهِ – عليه السلام- البردةَ عدةُ سُننٍ:
1- إباحةُ إنشادِ الشعر.
2- استماعه في المساجِد.
3- الإعطاءِ عَليه.⁽⁵⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 39: What is the proof that singing odes and listening to them in mosques is permitted?

Answer: Ubayy b. Ka’b رضي الله عنه related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Indeed some poetry contains wisdom.”⁽¹⁾ Anas رضي الله عنه reported that as they were constructing the Mosque (in Medina), the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would carry bricks with the people while they were singing:

O Allah! There is no true life but the life of the hereafter
So give victory to the Helpers and the Immigrants!

Salama b. al-Akwa‘ رضي الله عنه reported, “We went out with the Prophet ﷺ to Khaybar at night when a man, who was a poet from the people of ‘Amir b. al-Akwa‘, said, ‘Will you not listen to us?’ He alighted from his mount and came to the people and started to sing:

O Allah! Were it not for You, we would not have been guided
Nor would we have given charity or prayed

So forgive us, may we be sacrificed for You
And make firm our feet if we meet (the enemy in battle)

And cast tranquility upon us
When the call is made, we shall come
And by the call, they relied upon us

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ inquired, ‘Who was that rider?’ They replied, ‘It was ‘Amir b. al-Akwa‘.’ He ﷺ said ‘May Allah have mercy upon him!’ Someone from the group said, ‘O Messenger of Allah! Would that you let him stay among us a while longer!’ Afterwards (in the ensuing battle), he was struck and killed.”⁽²⁾

Sa‘id b. al-Musayyib رضي الله عنه reported, “Once, ‘Umar passed by the Mosque while Hassan (b. Thabit) was singing. ‘Umar gave him a stern look of rebuke, to which Hassan replied, ‘I used to sing songs while one better than you was present [i.e., the Messenger of Allah ﷺ].’ Then, Hassan turned to Abu Hurayra and said, ‘I adjure you by Allah! Didn’t you hear the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, “Respond on my behalf! O Allah! Aid him with the Sanctified Spirit (al-Ruh al-Qudus)?”’ Abu Hurayra replied, ‘Yes.’”⁽³⁾

‘A’isha رضي الله عنها related, “The Prophet ﷺ used to place Hassan on a pulpit within the Mosque where he could stand upon it and laud the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The Messenger ﷺ would say, ‘Certainly Allah aids Hassan with the Sanctified Spirit whenever he defends or lauds the Messenger of Allah!’”⁽⁴⁾
The erudite scholar al-Safarini, author of a commentary on a rhyming ode about propriety (Manzumat al-Adab), said:
And in the narration of Abu Bakr al-Anbari it mentions, “When Ka‘b b. Zuhayr repented, he sung his now famous ode:

Su‘ad has gone and as a result, my heart has today been rendered sick and weak
Enslaved to her in her wake, shackled
As if in chains, with no escape
Up until:

Indeed the Messenger is a light from whom illumination is sought
An unsheathed Muhannad [Indian] sword from the swords of Allah!

When he reached this line, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ tossed him his outer cloak that he was wearing. Mu‘awiya offered him over ten thousand dirhams for it, to which he replied, ‘I am not about to prefer anyone over the Messenger of Allah ﷺ!’ When Ka‘b died, Mu‘awiya sent twenty thousand (dirhams) to his heirs and acquired it from them…”

[M]any sunnas are derived from this story of Ka‘b singing to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Among them: the permissibility of singing poetry, listening to such poetry within the Mosque, and giving gifts for it.⁽⁵⁾

The legal ruling on singing to drive animals

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 40) ما حكم الحداء في الإسلام؟

الجواب: لَم يزلِ الحداءُ وراءَ الجمالِ من عادةِ العربِ في زمانِ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه وزمانِ الصحابةِ الكرام رضوان الله عليهم، وما هو إلا أشعارٌ تُؤَدّى بأصواتٍ طيبةٍ وألحانٍ موزونَة، ولم يُنْقَل عن أحدٍ من الصحابةِ إنكاره).

وعن أَنس رضيَ الله عنه: (أن النبيَّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه كانَ في سَفر، وكانَ غلامٌ يَحدو بهنّ يقال له "أنجشة" فقال النبيّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: "رويدَك يا "أنجشة" سَوْقك بالقوارير"قال أبو قلابة: يعني ضَعَفَة النساء).⁽¹⁾

وعَن "أنس بن مالك" رضوان الله عليه قال: (كان للنبيِّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه حادٍ يقال له "أنجشة" وكان حسنُ الصَّوت، فقالَ له النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: "يا "أنجشُ" رويدكَ لا تكسرِ القوارير". قال قتادة: يعني ضَعَفَة النساء).

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 40: What is the ruling in Islam on singing to drive camels and other animals?

Answer: [Imam al-Ghazali said], “Melodious singing on the backs of camels was a custom of the Arabs in the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and the Companions—may Allah be pleased with them all. It is nothing more than poetry recited with nice voices and measured rhythms. It is not recorded from any of the Companions that they rebuked it.”⁽¹⁾

Anas b. Malik رضي الله عنه reported, “On one occasion the Prophet ﷺ was on a journey, and there was a young servant named Anjasha who would sing to the camels [to make them ride faster]. The Prophet ﷺ said to him, ‘Take it easy, Anjasha! You are carrying delicate vessels!’” Abu Qilaba said, “He meant the weak ones among the women folk.”⁽²⁾

Anas b. Malik رضي الله عنه also reported, “The Prophet ﷺ had a singer who was called Anjasha, and he had a very beautiful voice. The Prophet ﷺ once said to him, ‘Take it easy, Anjasha! Do not break the delicate vessels!’” Qatada said, “He meant the weak ones among the women folk.”⁽³⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 41) وماذا قال صاحب الحظر والإباحة العلامة النحلاوي في حق السماع والنشيد؟

الجواب: وأما سماعُ السَّادة الصوفيَّة رضوان الله عليهم، فبمعزلٍ عن هذا الخِلاف – يعني اختلاف العلماءِ في مسألة السماع- بَل ومرتفِع عن درجةِ الإباحَة إلى رتبةِ المستحبّ كما صرح به غير واحدٍ من المحققِّين).⁽¹⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 41: What did the scholar Khalil al-Nahlawi say in his book al-Hazr wa al-Ibaha (The Lawful and Unlawful) about singing and audition?

Answer: He said, [quoting al-Fatawa al-Khayriyya (2:167)] “As for the audition of the noble Sufis—may Allah be pleased with all of them!—it is removed from this and has nothing to do with the difference of opinion; rather, it is elevated from the level of mere permissibility to one of recommendation, as was explicitly stated by several verifying scholars.”⁽¹⁾

referenceArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 10px 25px; font-size: 13px;'>
(1) الدرر المباحة في الخطر والإباحة، الشيخ خليل بن عبد القادر الشيباني الشهير بالنحلاوي، ص 93.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 42) ما الغاية من الإنشاد والسماع؟

الجواب: لما كانتِ الغايةُ من الإنشادِ الإرشادُ والمواعظُ والفوائد، حيث أن من طبيعةِ سماعهِ إثارة كوامنِ النفوس، وتَهييج مكنوناتِ القلوب، بما فيها من الأنسِ بالحضرةِ القدسيَّة، والشوقِ إلى الأنوارِ المحمديَّة، مما اتَّصف به ساداتُنا الصوفيَّة الذين لم يُحْجَبُوا بالأصواتِ لهواً، ولا يجتمعونَ عبثاً، وهم في وادٍ والناسُ في وادٍ آخر، والسرّ أنهم سَمعوا ما لم يَسمع الناس، وعَرفوا ما لم يعرف الناس، فسماعهُم يثيرُ أحوالَهم الحسنة، ويظهر وَجْدَهم، ويبعثُ ساكنَ الشوقِ ويحركُ القَلب، ولما كانت قلوبُهم بربِّهم متعلقة، وعليه عاكِفة في حضرةِ قربهِ قائِمة؛ فالسّماع يَسقي أرواحَهم، ويسرّع في سيرهم إلى الله تعالى، خلافاً لسماع الفسقَة اللئام؛ يجتمعونَ على اللهوِ وآلاتِ الطَّرب، فيبعثُ ما في قلوبهِم من الفُحش والفِسق، وينسيهِم واجباتهم تِجاه اللهِ تعالى، وعلى ذلكَ لا يمكنُ قياس الأبرارِ بالفجَّار، ولا الصالحينَ على الطالِحين.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 42: What is the objective behind singing and audition?

Answer: The goal behind singing odes is to deliver guidance, exhortation, and benefits. The nature of listening odes is that they stir what is in the latent depths of the souls, and excite the hearts with their lyrics—speaking of intimacy with the Divine Presence, and longing for the Muhammadan Lights. They [the Sufis] have not been veiled by their voices, using them for idle talk, nor have they gathered for a vain pastime. They are in one valley, and the people are in another. The secret is that they heard what the people did not, and they knew what the people knew not. Their singing stirs their good spiritual states, manifests their ecstasy, stimulates their longing, and moves their hearts. And since their hearts were devoutly attached their Lord and established in His Divine presence, the audition watered their hearts and helped them move quickly in their journey unto Allah Most High. This is quite different from the singing performed by the blameworthy and corrupt people; they gather to enjoy idleness and musical instruments, which brings out the corruption and vileness latent in their souls, and causes them to forget their obligations towards Allah Most High. Given this disparity, it is not possible to make a sound and valid comparison between the pious and the corrupt, and the righteous and the unrighteous.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 43) اذكر بعض الشواهد في فوائد الإنشاد والسماع؟

الجواب: ما قاله "مسلم العباداني" (قدمَ علينا "صالح المُري" و"عتبة الغلام" و"عبد الواحد بن زيد" و"مسلم الأسواري" ونَزلوا على الساحلِ ذات ليلة، فهيأتُ لهم طعاماً، ودعوتُهم إليه، فجاءوا إليَّ، ولما وضعتُ الطعامَ بين أيديهِم قالَ قائِل:
وتُلهيك عن دارِ الخلود مَطاعمٌ   ولَذةُ نفسٍ غيُّها غيرُ نافع
فصاحَ "عتبة الغلام" صيحةً وخرَّ مغشياً عَليه، وبَكى القوم، فرفعتُ الطعامَ من بينِ أيديهِم، وما ذاقوا والله لُقمة مِنه).⁽¹⁾
قال "أبو عثمان النيسابوري": (أنشدَ قَوَّالٌ بين يدي "الحارث المحاسبي" هذه الأبيات:

أنا في الغربةِ أبكي   ما بكتْ عينُ غريبِ
لم أكن يوم خروجي  من بلادي بمصيبِ
عجباً لي ولتركي   وطناً فيه حبيبي)
فَقام يتواجدُ ويبكي، حتى رحمهُ كلّ من حضره).⁽²⁾
لمَّا وردَ "ذو النونِ المصريّ" بغداد، جاءَه قومٌ من الصوفيّة بقوَّالهم، وطَلبوا منهُ أن يأذنَ لهُ بأن يقول، فأذِن لهُ فأنشَد:

صغيرُ هواك عذبني  فكيف به إذا احتنكا
وأنت جمعتَ في قلبي  هوىً قد كان مشتركا
أما ترثي لمكتئبٍ   إذا ضحك الخليُّ بكى)
فَقام "ذو النونِ" وسقطَ على وَجهه.⁽³⁾
رُبَّ ورقاءَ هتوفٍ في الضحى  ذاتِ شجوٍ صدحتْ في فَنَنِ

ذَكرتْ إلفاً ودهراً صالحاً   وبكتْ حُزْناً فهاجتْ حَزني
فبكائي ربما أرَّقها    وبُكاها ربما أرَّقني
ولقد أشكو فما أُفهمها   ولقد تشكو فما تُفهمني
غير أني بالجَوى أعرفها   وهي أيضاً بالجوى تعرفني
قال: فَما بقيَ أحدٌ من القومِ إلا قامَ وتواجَد، ولم يحصُل لهم هذا الوجدُ من العلمِ الذي خاضوا فيه، وإِن كان العلم جِداً وحقاً.⁽⁴⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 43: Can you cite some examples that show the benefits of singing and audition?

Answer: Muslim al-‘Abadali said, “One night, Salih al-Murri, ‘Utba al-Ghulam, ‘Abd al-Wahid b. Zayd, and Muslim al-Aswari came to us and went down to the sea coast. I prepared some food for them. When I was finished, I invited them to partake of the meal. They came over, and when I placed the food in front of them, one of them said:

Foods divert you from the eternal abode
And the pleasure of the ego will not profit!

Upon hearing this, ‘Utba al-Ghulam let out a loud scream and fell unconscious, and the people began to weep. Seeing this, I removed the food from their presence, and by Allah, they didn’t taste a single bite of it.”⁽¹⁾
Abu ‘Uthman al-Naysaburi said, “One time a singer sung the following lines in the presence of al-Harith al-Muhasibi:

I am crying in my state of strangeness
And never has the eye of a stranger cried
On the day I departed from my homeland
I was not correct
How astounding is my affair!
And how astounding is my leaving the abode in which there is my beloved!
Upon hearing this, al-Harith al-Muhasibi stood up and, in a state of spiritual ecstasy, began weeping so much that those attending asked Allah to show him mercy!”⁽²⁾

When Dhu al-Nun al-Misri passed through Baghdad, a group of Sufis, along with their singer, came to see him. They sought Dhu al-Nun’s permission to allow the singer to sing. He gave his permission, and the man sung:

A little of your love has punished me
So what if it enveloped me?
You have gathered in my heart
Love that was once shared with others
Aren’t condolences given to one in sorrow;
For when the lonely one laughs he is really crying?
Upon hearing this, Dhu al-Nun fell upon his face.”⁽³⁾

It is narrated that Abu al-Hasan al-Nuri was once with a group, attending a gathering they were invited to, and a discussion ensued regarding an issue of knowledge (although Abu al-Hasan remained silent). Soon after, he raised his head and said [in poetry]:

It maybe that a chirping dove in the early hours of the morning.
Filled with sadness and sorrow, is singing aloud on a branch;
Remembering a companion and bygone days,
And it cries out of sadness, stirring my own sadness
Maybe my crying is keeping her awake
Maybe her crying is keeping me awake
Perhaps she’s complaining but I can’t understand her
Perhaps I’m complaining but she can’t understand me
Except that—through my intense longing (for my beloved)—
Have I come to know her (and the pain of separation from her beloved)
And she—through her intense longing (for her beloved)—
Has she come to know me (and the pain of separation from my beloved)!

Imam al-Ghazali added, “No one among them remained except they were standing in a state of ecstasy. Such ecstasy was not obtained by the knowledge they were discussing with each other, even if that knowledge was real and true.”⁽⁴⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 44) ما هي فوائد الذكر إجمالا؟

الجواب: عن أَبي هريرةَ رضوان الله عليه، وأبي سعيد الخدري رضوان الله عليه، قالا: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم: (ما مِن قومٍ يذكرونَ الله إلا حفَّتهم الملائِكة، ونزَلت عَليهم السَّكينة، وذكَرهم الله فيمَن عِنده).⁽¹⁾
وعن أبي سعيد الخدري رضوانُ الله عليه قال: قالَ رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: يقول الربُّ تباركَ وتَعالى: (مَن شغله القرآن عن ذكري ومسألتي أعطيته أفضل ما أعطي السائلين).⁽²⁾
وعنهُ رضيَ الله عنهُ أيضاً عن النبي صلواته وسلامه قال: (يقول الرب يوم القيامة: سيعلمُ أهل الجمعِ اليومَ من أهل الكرمِ فقيل: ومَن أهلُ الكرمِ يا رسولَ الله ؟ قال: أهلُ الذكرِ في المَساجد).⁽³⁾

وعن معاوية رضي الله عنه، أن النبيَّ صلوات وسلامه عليه خرجَ على حلقة من أصحابهِ فقال: (ما أجلسَكم؟ فقالوا: جلَسنا نذكرُ اللهَ ونحمَده. فقال: أتاني جِبريل فأخبرَني أنَّ اللهَ تَعالي يُباهي بِكم الملائِكة).⁽⁴⁾ وعن أنس رضوانُ الله عليه قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (ما من قوم اجتمعوا يذكرونَ اللهَ إلا نادى منادٍ منَ السّماء: قوموا مغفوراً لَكم، قَد بدّلت سيئاتِكم حَسنات).⁽⁵⁾

وعَن ثابت قال: كانَ سلمانُ في عصابةٍ يذكرونَ الله، فمرَّ النبيّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه فكفّوا. فقال: (ما كُنتم تقولون؟ قلنا: نَذكر الله. قال: إني رأيتُ الرحمةً تنزلُ عليكُم فأحببتُ أن أشارككُم فيها، ثم قال: الحمدُ للهِ الذي جعلَ في أمّتي من أمرت أن أصبّر نفسي معهُم).⁽⁶⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 44: Can you mention some of the general benefits of remembrance?

Answer: Abu Hurayra and Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri رضي الله عنهما related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “There are no people who remember Allah except that the Angels surround them, and they are enveloped in mercy, tranquility descends upon them, and Allah mentions them with those with Him (the higher gathering of Angels).”⁽¹⁾

Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri رضي الله عنه also related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “The Lord—Blessed and Exalted is He—says, ‘Whosoever is engaged in reading the Quran or engaged in My remembrance and is too busy to supplicate to Me because of that, I shall give him the best of what is given to the supplicants.’”⁽²⁾

Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri رضي الله عنه also related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Allah will say on the Day of Judgment, ‘The people of the grouping shall know who the people of nobility are!’ The Companions inquired, “Who are the ‘people of nobility,’ O Messenger of Allah?” He replied, “They are the people who gather in the mosques for the sake of remembrance.”⁽³⁾

Mu‘awiya رضي الله عنه reported, “The Prophet came across a circle of his Companions and inquired, ‘What causes you all to sit?’ They responded, ‘We have sat down in order to remember and praise Allah.’ He said, ‘Jibril came to me and informed me that Allah mentioned you with pride with His Angels.’”⁽⁴⁾

Anas b. Malik رضي الله عنه related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “There are no group of people who gather to remember Allah—not intending anything other than the pleasure of Allah—except that a caller from the heavens calls out to them, saying, ‘Stand up as people who are forgiven for their sins, for your ill deeds have been replaced with good deeds!’”⁽⁵⁾

Thabit reported, “Salman (al-Farisi) was once in a group remembering Allah. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by they stopped. He ﷺ enquired, ‘What were you all saying?’ They replied, ‘We were remembering Allah.’ He ﷺ said, ‘Indeed, I saw mercy descending and I wished to share with you in it!’ He then said, ‘All praise is due to Allah who has placed in my Umma those with whom I have been ordered to be patient!’”⁽⁶⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 45) ما الفرق بين ذكر العامة وذكر الخاصة؟

الجواب: الذّكر على قِسمين، ذِكر العامّة وذِكر الخاصّة: أما العامّة فهو ذِكر الأجرِ والثواب، وهو أن يذكر العبدُ مَولاه بما شاءَ من ذِكر، معَ بقائهِ في صفاتِه المذمومَة، كالرّياء والكِبر والعجبِ والغُرور.. وغيرِ ذلك.

وأما ذِكر الخاصّة: فهو ذِكر الحُضور، وهوَ أن يذكر العبدُ مَولاه بِأذكار مَعلومة على صفةِ مَخصوصة، لينالَ بذلكَ المعرفة باللهِ تعالى، بطهارةِ نفسِه من كلِّ خُلُق ذَميم، وتَحليتِها بكلِّ خلقٍ كَريم، طَلباً لِلخروج مِن ظلمةِ الحسّ، وطمعاً في إدراكِ الأسرارِ الروحانيّة. والأوْلى لهُ اتّخاذ مسبحة يُحصي بها ما أرادَ من الأعداد، فيسلم مِن تعبِ حصرِ مقدارِها.
فالذّكر صقال قلوبِ المريدين، ومِفتاح بابِ النفَحات، وسبيلِ توجهِ التجلّيات على القُلوب، وبهِ يحصلُ التخلّق بالأخلاقِ المحمديّة.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 45: What is the difference between the remembrance of the common folk and remembrance of the elect?
Answer: Remembrance is two types. There is the remembrance of the common folk and the remembrance of the elect.

The remembrance of the common folk is remembering the reward and recompense for deeds. It is when the servant remembers his Master in whatever form or remembrance he wishes, while continuing to possess his blameworthy character traits such as showing off, arrogance, conceit, and delusion.

The remembrance of the elect is remembrance done with presence of heart. It is when the slave remembers his Master with the well-known formulas of remembrance and does so in a particular manner in order that he may obtain knowledge of Allah Most High, and all the while purifying himself from every blameworthy character trait and adorning himself with every noble and praiseworthy trait. He does this with the express purpose of extracting himself from the darkness of the material and sensory, desirous of comprehending the spiritual secrets. In pursuit of this, it is better if he takes a rosary (sibha) for himself so it can help him keep count of the particular number he wishes to maintain in remembrance, and save him from the fatigue of keeping count (in his head).

Remembrance, therefore, is the polish for the hearts of the spiritual seekers. It is the key to the door of Divine gifts, the path to Divine disclosures (tajalliyat) entering the heart, and through it one is able to inculcate within himself the Muhammadan qualities of character.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 46) ما هو دليل ورد السادة الصوفية من الكتاب والسنة؟

الجواب: الوِرد بالكسر كما في المِصباح: الوظيفةُ من قراءةٍ ونحو ذلكَ، والجمعُ أوراد يطلقهُ الصوفيّة على أذكارٍ يأمرُ الشيخُ تلميذهُ بذكرِها صباحاً بعدَ صلاةِ الصّبح ومساء بعدَ صلاةِ المَغرب والواردِ في الّلغة: هو الطارقُ القادِم، يقال: وردَ عَلينا فلانٌ أي قدِم، وفي الاصطِلاح: ما يتحفهُ الحقُّ تَعالى قلوب أوليائهِ من النفحاتِ الإلهيَّة فيكسبهُ قوةً محركَة، وربما يدهشهُ أو يغيبهُ عن حسِّه ولا يكونُ إلا بغتةً ولا يدومُ على صاحبِها.⁽¹⁾ والوِرد يضم ثلاثةَ صيغٍ من صِيغ الذّكر المطلوبةِ شرعاً، والتي دَعا اليها كِتابُ اللهِ تعالى، وبيَّنت السنَّة الشَّريفة فَضلها ومثوبَتها:

1- الاستغفار: بصيغةِ [استغفرُ الله] مائةَ مرةٍ بعدَ محاسبةِ النفسِ على الزّلاتِ لتَعود صفحةَ الأعمالِ نقيَّة بَيضاء، وقَد أمَرنا اللهُ تَعالى بقوله: ﴿مَا تُقَدِّمُوا لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ اللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرًا وَأَعْظَمَ أَجْرًا ۚ وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ﴾⁽²⁾ وقد كانَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يُكثر من الاستغفارِ تعليماً لأمّتهِ وتوجيهاً، كما رَوى سيدنا أبو هريرة رضوان الله عليه: (واللهِ إني لأستغفرُ اللهَ وأتوبُ إليهِ في اليومِ أكثرَ من سبعينَ مرة).⁽³⁾ وعن عبد الله بن بسر رضي الله عنه قال: سمعتُ رسولَ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يقول: (طوبَى لِمن وجدَ في صحيفتهِ استغفاراً كَثيرا).⁽⁴⁾

2- الصلاةُ على النبيِّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بصيغةِ: (اللهمَّ صلِّ على سيدِنا مُحمد عبدكَ ورسولكَ النبيِّ الأميِّ وعلى آلهِ وصحبهِ وسلَّم]. مائةَ مرةٍ مع استحضارِ عظمتهِ عليهِ السلام وتذكُّر صفاتهِ وشمائلهِ، والتعلقِ بجنابهِ الرَّفيع محبةً وتشوقاً. وقد أمرَنا اللهُ تَعالى بذلكَ بقولهِ: (إن الله وملائكته يصلون على النبي يا أيها الذين آمنوا صلوا عليه وسلموا تسليما)⁽⁵⁾ وكذلكَ رغبَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه في كثرةِ الصلاةِ والسلامِ عليه فقال: (مَن صلَّى عليَّ واحدةً صلى اللهُ عليهِ عشرا).⁽⁶⁾ ورويَ عن أنس رضي الله عنهُ قال: قالَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (مَن صلَّى عليَّ واحدةً صلَّى اللهُ عليهِ عشرَ صلواتٍ، وحطّت عنهُ عشرُ سيئاتٍ، ورُفعت لهُ عشرُ دَرجات).⁽⁷⁾ وقال صلوات الله وسلامه عليه أيضا: (أولى الناسِ بي يومَ القيامةِ أكثرُهم عليَّ صَلاة).⁽⁸⁾

3- كلمةُ التوحيدِ بصيغَة: (لا إلهَ إلا الله وحدهُ لا شريكَ له، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهوَ على كلِّ شيءٍ قدير) مائةَ مرة، مع التفكيرِ أنه لا خالقٌ ولا رازقٌ ولا نافعٌ ولا ضارٌ ولا قابضٌ ولا باسط.. إلا اللهَ وحده مع محاولةِ محوِ ما يسيطرُ على القلبِ من حبِّ الدُّنيا والأهواءِ والشَّهوات والوساوسِ والشواغلِ والعلائقِ والعوائق الكثيرةِ، حتى يكونُ القلبُ للهِ وحدهُ لا لِسواه. ولِهذا دَعانا اللهُ تَعالى لهذا التوحيدِ الخالصِ فقال: (فاعلم أنه لا إلهَ إلا الله) وكذلكَ رغَّبنا رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه في الإكثارِ من تردادِ كلمةِ التوحيدِ وبينَ أفضليَّتها ومثوبَتها فقال: (أفضلُ الذكرَ لا إلهَ إلا الله).⁽⁹⁾

يقول العلامة ابن علان في شرح هذا الحديث: (إنها "أي لا إله إلا الله" تؤثرُ تأثيراً بيّنا في تطهيرِ القلبِ عن كلِّ وصفٍ ذَميم راسخٍ في باطنِ الذاكر، وسببهُ أن (لا إلهَ) نفيٌ لجميعِ أفرادِ الآلهة و(إلا الله) إثباتٌ للواحدِ الحقّ الواجبِ لذاته، المنزّه عن كلِّ ما لا يليقُ بجلالِه، فبإدمانِ الذّكر لهذهِ المقولَة، ينعكسُ الذّكر من لسانِ الذاكرِ إلى باطنهِ حتَّى يتمكّن فيه، فيضيئَه ويصلحهُ، ثم يضيءَ ويصلحَ سائرَ الجوارِح، ولذا أُمر المريد بإكثارِها والدوامِ عليها).⁽¹⁰⁾

وعن أبي هريرة رضوان الله عليه قال: قال رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (جدِّدوا إيمانكُم، قيلَ يا رسولَ اللهِ كيفَ نجدِّدُ إيمانَنا؟ قال: أكثِروا من قولِ لا إلهَ إلا الله).⁽¹¹⁾ وقال صلوات الله وسلامه عليه أيضاً: (مَن قال: لا إلهَ إلا اللهَ وحدهُ لا شريكَ له، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهوَ على كلِّ شيءٍ قَدير في يومٍ مائةَ مرّة، كانَت لهُ عدلُ عشرِ رِقاب، وكُتبت لهُ مائةَ حسنةٍ، ومُحيت عنهُ مائةَ سيئَة، وكانَت لهُ حرزاً من الشَّيطان يومهُ، ذلكَ حتى يُمسي، ولم يأتِ أحدٌ بأفضلَ مما جاءَ به إلا رجلٌ عملَ أكثرَ مِنه).⁽¹²⁾

معَ الملاحظةِ أن هذا الوِرد يكونُ في الصباحِ والمساءِ، في جلسةٍ يَخلو فيها العبدُ بربِّه، وبذلكَ يكونُ قد افتتحَ نهارهُ بذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى، واختتمهُ بذكرهِ وطاعتِه، ولعلَّه يكونُ من الذينَ قالَ اللهُ تَعالى فيهم: ﴿وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَالذَّاكِرَاتِ أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةً وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾.⁽¹³⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 46: What is the proof in the Quran and the Sunna for the litanies (awrad) used by the Sufis?

Answer: According to the Arabic lexicon al-Misbah, a litany (wird)⁽¹⁾ is “A daily regimen of reading and the like.” Its plural is awrad. The Sufis employ this word to refer to the formulas of remembrance that a Shaykh orders his students to recite in the morning after the Morning Prayer (Fajr), and in the evening after the Sunset Prayer (Maghrib).

“The word warid means the traveler and one who comes. It is said, ‘So-and-so warada to us,’ meaning he came to us. In the technical nomenclature (of the Sufis), the warid is what is gifted by the Real [Allah] upon the hearts of His saints, the gifts of gentle breezes that grant them the power to move, and perhaps startles them or removes them from their sensory perception. By its nature, it comes suddenly and does not remain for long.”⁽²⁾
The litany is composed of three formulas that are sought after and encouraged in both the Sacred law and in the Book of Allah. The noble Sunna has explained their virtue and reward.

The first formula is seeking forgiveness with the expression Astaghfirullah (“I seek Allah’s forgiveness”) one hundred times, after having taken the soul to account for its faults so as to clear the account of deeds and make it clean again. Allah has enjoined us to seek forgiveness: And whatever you bring of good you shall find it with Allah—He is better and greater in reward. And seek the forgiveness of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving, Merciful. (Quran 73:20) The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to seek Allah’s forgiveness frequently in order to teach and guide his Umma. Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه related that he said, ﷺ “By Allah, I seek Allah’s forgiveness and repent unto Him more than seventy times a day!”⁽³⁾ ‘Abdullah b. Busr رضي الله عنه said, “I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, ‘Glad tidings to the one who finds in his scroll (of deeds) abundant prayers seeking forgiveness.’”⁽⁴⁾

The second formula is sending prayers upon the Prophet ﷺ with the expression Allahumma salli ‘ala Sayyidina Muhammad ‘abdika wa rasulika al-nabi al-ummi wa‘ala alihi wa sahbihi wasallim (O Allah, send prayers and peace upon our master Muhammad, Your servant and Messenger, the Primordial Prophet, and upon his Family and Companions) one hundred times, while bringing to mind his tremendous stature ﷺ, remembering his qualities and description, and attaching one’s self to his exalted being out of love and longing for him. Allah has enjoined us to send prayers upon His Prophet: Indeed Allah and His Angels send prayers upon the Prophet. O you who believe, send prayers upon him and abundant salutations of peace. (Quran 33:56) Similarly, the Messenger of Allah encouraged us to send abundant peace and prayers upon him: “Whosoever sends a single prayer upon me, Allah shall send tenfold prayers upon him.”⁽⁵⁾ Anas b. Malik related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Whosoever sends a single prayer upon me, Allah shall send ten prayers upon him, efface ten sins from him, and elevate him ten degrees.⁽⁶⁾ He ﷺ also said, “The closest to me on the Day of Judgment shall be those who sent the most prayers upon me.”⁽⁷⁾

The third formula is the testimony of Divine Oneness in the expression La ilaha illa Allah wahdahu la sharika lahu lahu al-mulk wa-lahu al-hamd wa-huwa ‘ala kulli shay’in qadir (There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners; to Him belongs the dominion and praise, and He has power over all things) one hundred times, or only the formula, la ilaha illa Allah (there is no god but Allah) one hundred times, all the while contemplating on its meaning: that there is no creator, provider, bringer of harm or benefit, constrictor or expander, except Allah alone; while at the same time attempting to efface from the heart all things that seek to dominate over it such as love of the world, vain desires, satanic whisperings, distractions, bad habits, and barriers—so the heart is for Allah alone and no one or nothing else. It is for this reason that Allah Most High invited us to this pure Divine unity when He said, Know that there is no god but Allah. (Quran 47:19). Similarly, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ encouraged us to repeat often the testimony of Divine oneness, and He explained its virtue and reward: “The most virtuous remembrance (is the statement), ‘There is no god but Allah.’”⁽⁸⁾ The erudite Ibn ‘Allan said in his commentary on this hadith:

It has a clear and observable effect in purifying the heart from every blameworthy trait that is rooted in the heart of the one engaged in remembrance. This is because the first half of the statement ‘There is no god…’ is a negation of all individual deities, and the statement ‘but Allah’ is an affirmation of divinity for the One, the Real, the Necessary being, the One who is transcendent beyond everything unbefitting His majesty. So by perpetually remembering these meanings, the remembrance moves from the tongue to the inward until it gains firmness therein, illuminating and rectifying the inside, then moving on to illuminate and rectify the bodily limbs. For this reason, the seeker is enjoined to recite it frequently and abundantly.⁽⁹⁾

Abu Hurayra رضي الله عنه related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, “Renew your faith!” He was asked, “O Messenger of Allah, how do we renew our faith?” He replied, “Say frequently ‘There is no god but Allah.’”⁽¹⁰⁾

The Prophet ﷺ also said, “Whosoever says one hundred times in a day ‘There is no god but Allah, He is Alone and has no partners, to Him belongs the dominion and all praise, and He has power over all things’ shall have a reward equivalent to freeing ten slaves, and shall have written for him one hundred good deeds and have removed from him one hundred sins, and it will be a protection for him against Satan for his entire day until evening arrives. No one brings better than what he has done except one who does more than him.”⁽¹¹⁾

It should be noted that this litany is recited in the morning and evening in a place where the servant can be alone with his Lord. In this way, the servant will begin and end his day in the remembrance of Allah Most High and obedience to Him. Perhaps such a person will be included among those whom Allah mentioned: The men and women who remember Allah in abundance—Allah has prepared for them forgiveness and a mighty reward! (Quran 33:35)⁽¹²⁾
