questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 92) ما هي مراتب المحبة العشر؟
الجواب:
أوَّلها العلاقة: وسمِّيت بذلكَ لتعلقِ القلبِ بالمَحبوب.
الثانية الإرادة: وهي ميلُ القلبِ إلى محبوبهِ وطلبهِ له.
الثالثة الصبابة: وهي انصبابُ القلبِ إلى المحبوبِ بحيثُ لا يملكهُ صاحبه، كانصبابِ الماءِ في المُنحدر.
الرابعة الغرام: وهو الحبُّ اللازمُ للقلبِ لا يفارقُه، بل يلازمهُ كملازمةِ الغريمِ لغريمِه.
الخامسة الوداد: وهو صفوُ المحبةِ، وخالصِها ولبِّها.
السادسة الشغف: وهوَ وصولُ الحبِّ إِلى شغافِ القَلب. قال الإمام "الجنيد" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: الشغَف ألا يرى المحبَّ جفاءً، بل يراهُ عدلاً منهُ ووفاءً.
وتعذيبكم عذبٌ لديَّ وجَورُكم علي بما يقضي الهوى لكم عدل
السابعة العشق: وهو الحبُّ المفرطُ الذي يخافُ على صاحبهِ مِنه.
الثامنة التتيم: وهو التعبُّد والتذلُّل، يقال: تيَّمه الحُب أي ذللَهُ وعبَّده.
التاسعة التعبد: وهو فوقَ التتيُّم، فإن العبدَ لم يبقَ لهُ شَيء من نفسِه.
العاشرة الخلة: انفردَ بها الخليلانِ "إبراهيم" و"محمد" صلوات الله وسلامه عليهما، وهي المحبَّة التي تخللَت روحَ المُحب وقَلبه، حتى لم يبقَ موضعٌ لغيرِ المَحبوب.⁽¹⁾
وقد رأى الصوفية أن سرِّ هذه الحياة يقومُ على حرفينِ اثنَين "الحاء والباء".
وأحسنُ حالة الإِنسان صدقٌ وأكملُ وصفه حاءٌ وباءُ
فالتكاليفُ تسهل وتلذ إذا ما وجد الحب
لولاك يا سر الوجود  ما طاب عيشي ولا وجودي
ولا ترنمت في صلاتي  ولا ركوعي ولا سجودي
وإِذا تمكَّن الحبُّ من القلبِ أخرجَ هذه الدُّنيا الفانيَة من سويدائِه، وعاشَ صاحبهُ حياةً طيبةً منعمَة، لا يعرفُ الهمَّ سبيلهُ إليه.
مرَّ بعضُ الصوفيةِ على رجلٍ يَبكي على قَبر، فسألهُ عن سببِ بُكائه فقال: إن لي حبيباً قَد مات. فقال: لقد ظلمتَ نفسكَ بحبِّك لحبيبٍ يَموت، فلو أنكَ أحببتَ حبيباً لا يَموت، لما تعذَّبت بفراقِه.

وفي واقعِنا أمثلةٌ كثيرةٌ عمَّن يسترخصُ موتهُ عندَ يأسهِ من لقاءِ من يحبُّه، أو انقطاعُ أملهِ مما تعلقَ قلبهُ به من متاعٍ زائِل، فالانتحارُ وحرقُ النفسِ، والتِّرامي على صخرةِ المَوت، أمورٌ كلُّنا نسمعُها عن محبِّين يائسينَ خاسِرين، وقَد قيل:
فإن شئت أن تحيا حياةً هنيئةً فلا تتخذ شيئاً تخاف له فقدا

فأينَ هؤلاءِ من أحبابِ اللهِ ورسولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه الذين أحبُّوا الله، ورَضوا به رباً، وبرسولهِ محمدٍ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه رسولاً، وبالإِسلام ديناً.. فمنهُم من أحبَّ المَوت، ورحَّب به ليَلقى مِن ورائهِ أحبابَه (غداً ألقى الأحبَّة محمداً وصَحبه). قالَ ذلكَ "بلال" رضوان الله عليه عندَ احتضارِه.

ومنهُم من ضحَّى بنفسهِ ودمهِ في ساحاتِ الجِهاد، لينالَ رضوانَ اللهِ ويَحظى بلقائِه.. ومنهُم ومنهُم.. وفرقٌ كبيرٌ بينَ من يضحِّي بنفسهِ في سبيلِ اللهِ تَعالى، وبينَ من يضحِّي بنفسهِ لفقدِ شيءٍ خَسيسٍ تافِه:
أنت القتيل بأي من أحببته فاختر لنفسك في الهوى من تصطفي

وأَعلى وأَغلى الثمراتِ التي يقطفُها المحِب، هو الحبُّ المتبادَل ﴿يُحِبُّهُم وَيُحِبُّونَهُ﴾⁽²⁾ والرِّضى المتبادَل: ﴿رضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ﴾⁽³⁾ والذِّكر المتبادل: ﴿فَاذكُرونِي أذْكُرْكُم﴾.⁽⁴⁾

مرَّ عيسى صلوات الله وسلامه عليه على طائفةٍ من العِباد، قد وهنَت أبدانهُم، وتغيرَت ألوانهُم من العبادَة فقالَ لهم: (مَن أنتُم؟ فقالوا: نحنُ عبادُ اللهِ تَعالى، فقالَ: ولأيِّ شيءٍ تعبَّدتم؟ قالوا: خوَّفنا اللهُ من نارِه، فخفُنا مِنها، فقال: إن اللهَ تَعالى قد أمِّنكم مما خفتُم مِنه. ثم جاوزهُم لآخرينَ أشدُّ منهُم عِبادة، فقال: لأيِّ شيءٍ تعبدتُم؟ قالوا: شَوقنا إلى الجنَّة وما أُعد فيها لأوليائِه، فنحنُ نَرجوها بعبادتِنا.. فقال: إنَّ اللهَ أعطاكُم ما رجوتُم. ثم جاوزهُم ومرَّ بآخرينَ يتعبَّدون فقال: مَن أنتُم؟ قالوا: نحنُ المحبونَ للهِ عزَّ وجَل، لم نعبدهُ خوفاً من نارِه، ولا شوقاً إلى جنتِه، ولكن حباً لهُ وتعظيماً لجلالِه، فقال أنتُم أولياءُ اللهِ حقاً، وقد أُمِرْت أن أُقيمَ معكُم، وأقامَ بينَ أظهرهِم)⁽⁵⁾

ويشيرُ هذا الشاهدُ إِلى أنَّ الناس يتفاوتونَ باختلافِ هِممهم، فمنهُم من يريدُ الدُّنيا، ومنهُم من يريدُ الآخرَة، ومنهُم من يريدُ اللهَ تَعالى. سمعَ بعضُ السادة الصوفيَّة يقرأ: ﴿مِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُريدُ الدُّنيا وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُريدُ الآخِرَة﴾⁽⁶⁾ فقال: وأينَ من يُريد الله؟ ولِهذا قالَ الإمام "علي" كرَّم الله وجهه: (إِنَّ قوماً عَبدوا اللهَ رغبةُ فتلكَ عبادةُ التُّجار، وإِنَّ قوماً عَبدوا اللهَ رهبةً فتلكَ عبادةُ العَبيد، وإن قوماً عَبدوا الله شكراً فتلكَ عبادةُ الأحرار).
وقيلَ في وصفِ الذينَ أرادوا الله، وأحبوهُ دونَ غيرِه:
فما مقصودُهم جناتُ عدنٍ  ولا الحور الحسان ولا الخيام
سوى نظر الجليل وذا مناهم  وهذا مقصدُ القوم الكرامُ
للهِ دُر أقوامٍ إذا جنَّ عليهم الليل سمعتَ لهم أنينَ الخائِف، وإذا أصبحوا رأيتَ عليهِم تغيُّر ألوانهِم:
إذا ما الليل أقبل كابدوه  ويسفر عنهم وهم ركوع
أطار الشوق نومهم فقاموا  وأهل الأمن في الدنيا خشوع
أجسادُهم تصبرُ على التعبُّد، وأقدامهُم ليلها مقيمةٌ على التهجُّد، لا يردُّ لهُم صوتُ ولا دُعاء.. تراهم في ليلهم سجداً ركعاً، وقد ناداهُم المنادي، وأطربهُم الشادي:
يا رجال الليل جِدّوا   رُبَّ صوتٍ لا يُرَدُّ
لا يقوم الليل إِلا   من له حَزم وجدُّ
لو أرادوا في ليلتهِم ساعةَ أن يَناموا أقلقهُم الشوقُ إليهِ فَقاموا، وجذبهُم الوجدُ والغرامُ فَهاموا، وأنشدهُم مريدُ الحضرةِ عن لسانِ الحَضرة وبثّهم، وحملهُم على المناجاةِ وحثَّهم:
حثوا مطاياكم وجدُّوا  إن كان لي في القلوب وجْدُ
قد آن أن تظهر الخبايا  وتُنشَر الصحف فاستعدُّوا

الفرشُ مشتاقةٌ إليهِم، والوسائدُ متأسفَة عليهِم، (النومُ قرمُ إلى عيونهِم)⁽⁷⁾، والراحةُ مرتاحَة إلى جنوبهِم. الليلُ عندهم أجلُّ الأوقاتِ في المراتب، ومسامرهُم عند تهجدهِم يَرعى الكواكِب. هَجروا المنامَ في الظَّلام.. وقلَّدوا بطولِ المَقام، وناجوا ربّهم بأطيبِ كَلام، وأنِسوا بقربِ الملِك العَلاَّم، لو احتجبَ عنهُم في ليلهِم لَذابوا، ولو تغيَّب عنهُم لحظةً لما طابوا، يديمونَ التهجُّد إلى السِّحر، ويتوقعونَ ثمرَ اليقظةِ والسهَر. بلغَنا أنَّ اللهَ تَعالى يتجلَّى للمحبينَ، فيَقول لهُم: مَن أنا؟ فيقولون: أنتَ مالك رقابِنا، فيقول: أنتُم أحبَّتي، أنتُم أهلُ ولايَتي وعنايَتي، هاوَجهي فشاهِدوه، ها كَلامي فاسمَعوه، ها كأسي فاشرَبوه ﴿وسَقاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَراباً طَهوراً﴾⁽⁸⁾، إذا شَربوا طابوا، وإذا طابوا أطرَبوا، وإذا طَربوا قاموا، وإذا قاموا هَاموا. لمّا حملَت ريحُ الصّبا قميصَ "يوسف" عليه السلام، لم يفضفِض ختامُه إلا "يعقوب"، ما عرفهُ أهلٌ كَنعان ومَن عندهُم خَرج، ويهوذا وهوَ الحامِل.⁽⁹⁾

والحبُّ فطرةٌ في النفسِ الزكيَّة، تنزعُ بها إلى تفهُّم حقيقتِها والشوقُ إلى التعرفِ على خالقِها، ويزدادُ الحبُّ كلَّما ازدادَ الإيمان، وبمقدارِ كمالِ النفسِ يكونُ الحبُّ، وعلى قدرِ الحبِّ تكونُ السعادةُ ويكونُ التَّنعيم.. وحبُّ اللهِ تَعالى يَسمو بالذوقِ الإنسانِي، إذ يحوِّل صاحبهُ إلى لطيفةٍ راضيةٍ مطمئنَّة.
ولقد جرَّد السادةُ الصوفيةُ الحبَّ عن المطامعِ والشَّهوات، وأخلَصوا الحبَّ للهِ تَعالى، فليسَ في حبهِم علَّة، ولا لعشقهِم دواءٌ إلا رضى مولاهُم ولبعضِ المحبِّين:
فليتك تحلو والحياة مريرةٌ  وليتك ترضى والأنامُ غضابُ
وليت الذي بيني وبينك عامرٌ  وبيني وبين العالمين خرابُ
إذا صح منك الودُّ فالكلُّ هينٌ وكل الذي فوق التراب ترابُ

عرفَ السادةُ الصوفيَّة طريقَ الحُب، فساروا فِيه، قالَ اللهُ تَعالى في الحديثِ القُدسي: (وما تقرَّب عبدي بشيءٍ أحبَّ إليَّ مما افترضتهُ عَليه، وما يزالُ عبدي يتقرب إليَّ بالنوافلِ حتَّى أُحبَّه، فإذا أحببتُه كنتُ سمعَهُ الذي يسمعُ به، وبصرَهُ الذي يبصرُ به، ويدَهُ التي يبطشُ بها، ورجلَهُ التي يَمشي بها، ولئن سألَني لأعطينَّه، ولئن استعاذَني لأعيذنَّه).⁽¹⁰⁾

وهو أصلُ السلوكِ إلى اللهِ تَعالى، والوصولِ إلى معرفتِه. سُئل "ذو النون المصري" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى عن المحبَّة فقال: (أن تحبَّ ما أحبَّ الله، وتبغضَ ما أبغضَ الله، وتفعلَ الخيرَ كلَّه، وترفضَ كلَّ ما يشغلُ عن الله، وأن لا تخافَ في اللهِ لومةَ لائِم، مع العطفِ على المؤمِنين، والغلظةِ على الكافِرين، واتباعُ رسولِ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه في الدِّين).⁽¹¹⁾ وقال أيضاً: (من علاماتِ المحبِّ لله، متابعةُ حبيبِ الله في أخلاقهِ وأفعالهِ وأمرهِ وسنَّته).⁽¹²⁾

وقال السيد "أحمد الرفاعي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: (من أحبَّ اللهَ علَّم نفسهُ التواضُع، وقطعَ عنها علائقَ الدُّنيا، وآثرَ اللهَ تعالى على جميعِ أحوالِه، واشتغلَ بذكرِه، ولم يترُك لنفسهِ رغبةً فيما سِوى اللهِ تَعالى، وقامَ بعباداتِه). وقال "محمد بن علي الترمذي الحكيم" رحمهُ اللهُ تعالى: (حقيقةَ محبتهِ دوامُ الأنسِ بذكرِه).⁽¹³⁾

وقال "ابن الدباغ" رحمهُ اللهٌ تَعالى: (ولمَّا كانَ مطلبُ ذوي العقولِ الكاملةِ والنفوسِ الفاضلةِ نيلَ السعادةِ القُصوى، التي مَعناها الحياةُ الدائمةُ في المَلأ الأعلى، ومشاهدةُ أنوارِ حضرةِ قُدس المَولى، والتلذذُ بمطالعةِ الجمالِ الإلهيِّ الأسنى، ومعاينةِ مطالعِ النورِ القُدسي الأبهى، وهذهِ السعادةُ لا تحصلُ إلا لنفسٍ زكيَّة، قد سبقَت لها في الأزلِ العنايةُ الربانيةُ بتيسيرِها لسلوكِ الطرقِ العلميةِ والعمليَّة المفضياتِ بها إِلى المحبةِ الحقيقيَّة، والشوقُ إلى الأنوارِ الإلهيَّة؛ وبحصولِ هذه السعادةِ يحصلُ للنفوسِ العارفةِ من اللذةِ والابتهاجِ ما لا عينٌ رأَت، ولا أذنٌ سمِعت، ولا خطرَ على قلبِ بَشر، فيجبُ على كلِّ ذي لُب المبادرةَ إلى تحصيلِ هذا الأمرِ الجَليل، وورودِ هذا الموردِ السلسبيلِ الذي لم يصِل إليهِ من الناسُ إلا القليل. فالعاشقُ يحنُّ إلى هذا الموطنِ الجَليل، وينجذبُ جملةً إلى ظلِّه الظَّليل ونسيمهِ العَليل، وورودِ منهلهِ العَذب، فلا يشيمُ البرقُ إلا لأنه يَأتي من ذلكَ الجناب الرَّفيع، ويخبرُ عن سرِّ جمالهِ البَديع، فلهذا كانَ لمعانُ البروقِ يقطعُ بالشوقِ أفلاذَ كبدِ المُشوق).⁽¹⁴⁾

وبمثلِ هذا الذوقِ وصلَ الصوفيّة إلى الاطمئنانِ والرِّضا في ظلالِ الحُب الإلهِي، ورأوا مُتعاً روحيةً دونَها مُتع الحياةِ وشهواتِها. وحسبُهم أنهُم يسرونَ معَ الله، وينعَمون بقربِه، ويشعرونَ بفضلهِ وجودِه ﴿رضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ﴾⁽¹⁵⁾، ﴿يُحِبُّهُم وَيُحِبُّونَهُ﴾⁽¹⁶⁾ فاختارهُم بعدَما أحبّهم ورضيَ عنهُم، أولئكَ خلاصةُ خلقهِ، وخواصّ أحبابِه، فقيلَ فيهِم:
لله قوم أخلصوا في حُبِّه فاختارهم ورضــــي بهم خداما
قومٌ إِذا جَنَّ الظلامُ عليهمُ أبصرْتَ قوماً سجداً وقياما
يتلذذون بذكره في ليلهم ويكابدون به النهارَ صياما
فسيغنمون عرائساً بعرائسٍ ويُبَوَّؤن من الجِنان خياما
وتقرُّ أعينُهم بما أُخفي لهم وسيسمعون من الجليل سلاما

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 92: What are the ten levels (marātib) of love?
Answer: The scholars have mentioned ten levels of love:
1. Connection (‘alaqa): Named as such because of the heart’s attachment to the Beloved.
2. Desire (irāda): The heart’s inclination toward its Beloved and its seeking of Him.
3. Ardent longing (ṣabāba): The pouring of the heart toward the Beloved such that its owner no longer controls it—like water pouring down a slope.
4. Infatuation (gharām): A love fixed in the heart that never separates from it, like a creditor’s attachment to his debtor.
5. Pure affection (wadād): The clear, unadulterated essence and core of love.

6. Enamored passion (shaghaf): When love reaches the inner lining (shaghāf) of the heart. Imam al-Junayd (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “Shaghaf is when the lover sees no harshness; rather, he sees it all as justice and fidelity from the Beloved.” [A poet said:]

Your torment is sweet to me, and your injustice to me—
By what desire decrees—is justice from You.
7. Passionate love (‘ishq): Excessive love that one fears may harm its possessor.
8. Subservient devotion (tatayyum): Worship and utter humility. It is said: “Love made him subservient (tayyamahu),” meaning it humbled and enslaved him.
9. Total worship (ta‘abbud): A level above subservient devotion, where the servant retains nothing of himself.

10. Intimate friendship (khulla): A rank exclusive to the two intimate friends of Allah—Ibrāhīm and Muḥammad (peace and blessings be upon them both). This is love that permeates the spirit and heart of the lover until no place remains for other than the Beloved.⁽¹⁾
The Sufis held that the secret of this life rests upon two letters: Ḥā’ and Bā’ (forming the word ḥubb—love):

The best state of a person is sincerity,
And his most perfect description is Ḥā’ and Bā’.
When love is present, obligations become easy and delightful:

Were it not for you, O secret of existence,
Neither my life nor my being would be pleasant;
Nor would I chant in my prayer,
Nor in my bowing or prostration.
When love firmly takes root in the heart, it expels this perishable world from its depths, and its possessor lives a good, blessed life untouched by anxiety.

One of the Sufis passed a man weeping at a grave and asked the reason. The man replied, “I had a beloved who died.” The Sufi said, “You have wronged yourself by loving one who dies. Had you loved the One who never dies, you would not suffer from separation.”

In our time we see many who cheapen death when they despair of meeting their beloved or lose hope in something transient to which their heart clung. Suicide, self-immolation, and throwing oneself onto the rocks of death—we hear of these from despairing, losing lovers. It has been said:

If you wish a pleasant life,
Do not take hold of anything whose loss you fear.

How far are these from the lovers of Allah and His Messenger (may Allah’s prayers and peace be upon him), who loved Allah and were pleased with Him as Lord, with Muḥammad as Messenger, and with Islam as religion? Among them were those who loved death and welcomed it to meet their beloveds: “Tomorrow I shall meet my beloveds—Muḥammad and his Companions,” said Bilāl (may Allah be pleased with him) at the point of death.

Others sacrificed their lives and blood on the fields of jihad to attain Allah’s pleasure and the fortune of meeting Him. There is a vast difference between one who sacrifices himself in the path of Allah Most High and one who sacrifices himself for something base and trivial:

You are slain by whomever you love—
So choose for yourself in desire one you select.

The highest and most precious fruit the lover reaps is mutual love: “He loves them and they love Him” (Quran 5:54)⁽²⁾; mutual pleasure: “Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him” (Quran 98:8)⁽³⁾; and mutual remembrance: “So remember Me; I will remember you” (Quran 2:152)⁽⁴⁾.

‘Īsā (peace be upon him) once passed a group of worshippers whose bodies were wasted and faces altered by devotion. “Who are you?” he asked. “We are servants of Allah,” they replied. “Why do you worship?” “We fear His Fire,” they said. “Allah has granted you safety from what you feared,” he replied. He passed another group more intense in worship. “Why do you worship?” “We long for His Paradise,” they said. “Allah has given you what you hoped for,” he replied. Then he passed yet another group. “Who are you?” “We are lovers of Allah,” they answered. “We worship Him neither from fear of His Fire nor longing for His Paradise, but from love of Him and reverence for His Majesty.” ‘Īsā said, “You are the true friends of Allah, and I have been commanded to stay with you.”⁽⁵⁾

This narration shows that people differ according to their aspirations: some seek the world, some the Hereafter, and some seek Allah Most High. When one of the Sufis heard the verse “Among you are those who desire the world, and among you are those who desire the Hereafter” (Quran 3:152)⁽⁶⁾, he asked, “And where are those who desire Allah?” Imam ‘Alī (may Allah ennoble his face) said: “Some worship Allah out of desire (for reward)—that is the worship of merchants. Some worship Allah out of fear—that is the worship of slaves. And some worship Allah out of gratitude—that is the worship of the free.”
Describing those who desire Allah and love Him above all else, it was said:

Their aim is not the gardens of Eden,
Nor the beautiful maidens, nor the pavilions—
Only the vision of the Majestic; that is their longing,
And that is the goal of the noble folk.
May Allah bless those folk who, when night falls, you hear from them the sighs of the fearful, and when morning comes, you see their faces changed:

When night arrives they endure it,
And dawn finds them in bowing.
Longing drives sleep from them so they stand,
While the secure in the world remain in humble prostration.

Their bodies patiently bear the toil of worship; their feet remain standing in night vigil. No supplication or call from them is rejected. You see them in their nights in prostration and bowing. A caller calls to them:

O men of the night, strive earnestly—
How many a voice is never turned away!
None stands the night
Except one with resolve and dedication.

If they wished to sleep even a moment in their night, longing for Him would disturb them and they would rise. Ecstasy and ardent passion would draw them and they would wander in rapture. The seeker of the Divine presence sang to them from the tongue of the Presence and urged them to intimate converse:

Urge on your mounts and proceed earnestly—
If there is truly longing for Me in hearts!
The hour has come for hidden things to appear
And scrolls to be opened—so prepare yourselves!

Their beds long for them, their pillows miss them; sleep is desired by their eyes, yet rest stands aside from their sides. Night is their most exalted time in rank; their night conversations keep company with the stars. They abandoned sleep in darkness and replaced it with prolonged standing. They entreated their Lord with the sweetest speech and found intimacy with the All-Knowing Sovereign King. If He veiled Himself from them in their night their hearts would melt; if He were absent from them even a moment they would find no sweetness. They persist in night vigil until the pre-dawn, awaiting the fruit of wakefulness and sleeplessness.

It has reached us that Allah Most High manifests to the lovers and says to them, “Who am I?” They reply, “You are the Master of our necks.” He says, “You are My beloveds, the people of My friendship and care. Here is My Countenance—so witness It! Here is My speech—so hear It! Here is My cup—so drink from It!” “And their Lord gave them to drink a pure drink.” (Quran 76:21)⁽⁷⁾ When they drink they become pleasant; when pleasant they become delighted; when delighted they stand; when standing they wander in rapture.

When the east wind carried Yūsuf’s shirt (peace be upon him), none knew its scent except Ya‘qūb. It was not recognized by the people of Canaan or those who departed with them, nor by Judah who carried it.⁽⁸⁾

Love is innate in the pure soul; by it the soul is drawn to understand its reality and yearn to know its Creator. Love increases as faith increases. The perfection of the soul corresponds to the perfection of love; happiness and bliss correspond to the degree of love. Love of Allah elevates human taste, transforming its possessor into a contented, tranquil, subtle essence.

The Sufi masters purified love of worldly desires and ambitions, dedicating it purely to Allah Most High. In their love there is no ulterior motive; the only cure for their passionate love is the pleasure of their Master. Some lovers said:

Would that you were sweet though life is bitter,
And would that you be pleased though people are angry.
Would that what is between us were flourishing,
And what is between me and the worlds were in ruin.
If your affection is sound then all is easy,
And everything above dust is but dust.

The Sufis knew the path of love and walked it. Allah Most High said in a Hadith Qudsi: “My servant draws not near to Me with anything more beloved to Me than what I have made obligatory upon him. My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory deeds until I love him. When I love him I am his hearing with which he hears, his sight with which he sees, his hand with which he strikes, and his foot with which he walks. Were he to ask Me I would surely give him, and were he to seek refuge in Me I would surely grant him refuge.”⁽⁹⁾

Love is the foundation of wayfaring to Allah Most High and the means of attaining knowledge of Him. Dhū al-Nūn al-Miṣrī (may Allah have mercy on him) was asked about love and said: “It is to love what Allah loves, hate what Allah hates, do every good deed, reject everything that distracts from Allah, fear no blame of any blamer for Allah’s sake, show kindness to believers and harshness to disbelievers, and follow the Messenger of Allah (may Allah’s prayers and peace be upon him) in religion.”⁽¹⁰⁾ He also said: “Among the signs of loving Allah is following the beloved of Allah in his character, deeds, commands, and Sunna.”⁽¹¹⁾

Sayyid Aḥmad al-Rifā‘ī (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “Whoever loves Allah teaches his soul humility, severs from it worldly attachments, prefers Allah Most High in all his states, occupies himself with His remembrance, leaves no desire for himself besides Allah Most High, and engages in His worship.”
Muḥammad ibn ‘Alī al-Tirmidhī al-Ḥakīm (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “The reality of love for Him is constant intimacy with His remembrance.”⁽¹²⁾

Ibn al-Dabbāgh (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “Since the quest of those with perfected intellects and virtuous souls is to attain supreme felicity—meaning eternal life in the highest assembly, witnessing the lights of the holy presence of the Master, delighting in beholding the supreme Divine beauty, and gazing upon the rising of transcendent lights—and since this felicity is attained only by a pure soul that has been granted lordly solicitude in pre-eternity to traverse the paths of knowledge and action leading to true love and longing for Divine lights (and by attaining this felicity the knowing souls receive delight and joy that no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no heart has imagined), it is incumbent upon every person of understanding to hasten to this noble matter and enter this sweet Salsabīl spring that few have reached. The passionate lover yearns for this noble abode, is drawn wholly to its deep shade and gentle breeze, and to drinking from its sweet spring. He does not gaze at lightning except because it comes from that exalted presence and announces the secret of its wondrous beauty. Hence the flashes of lightning pierce the hearts of the yearning with longing.”⁽¹³⁾

With such taste the Sufis attained tranquility and contentment in the shade of Divine love, beholding spiritual delights surpassing the pleasures and desires of this life. It suffices them that they journey with Allah, delight in His nearness, and sense His bounty and generosity: “Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him” (Quran 98:8)⁽¹⁴⁾; “He loves them and they love Him” (Quran 5:54)⁽¹⁵⁾. He chose them after loving them and being pleased with them. They are the elite of His creation and the choicest of His beloveds. Concerning them it was said:

To Allah belong a people who were sincere in His love,
So He chose them and was pleased with them as servants.
A people who, when night falls upon them,
You see them in prostration and standing.
They delight in His remembrance in their nights,
And endure fasting for Him by day.
They shall gain brides through brides,
And erect pavilions in the gardens.
Their eyes are cooled by what was hidden for them,
And from the Generous they shall hear salutations.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 93) ما هو الكشف؟

الجواب: تعريفه: قال "السيد" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى في تعريفاتِه: (الفراسةُ في اللغة: التثبُّت والنَّظر، وفي اصطلاحِ أهلِ الحقيقَة: هي مكاشفةُ اليَقين، ومعاينةُ الغَيب)⁽¹⁾. وقال "العارف بالله ابن عجيبة" (الفراسةُ هي خاطر يهجم على القلب، أو وارد يتجلى فيه، ولا يخطىء غالباً إذا صَفى القَلب، وفي الحديث: (اتَّقوا فِراسة المؤمِن، فإِنه ينظرُ بنورِ الله)⁽²⁾ وهي على حسبِ قوةِ القربِ والمعرفَة، فكلما قويَ القُرب، وتمكنَت المعرفةُ صدقَت الفراسَة، لأن الروحَ إذا قرَّبت من حضرةِ الحقِّ لا يتجلَّى فيها غالباً إلا الحَق.⁽³⁾

والكشفُ نورٌ يحصلُ للسالكينَ في سيرهِم إلى اللهِ تَعالى، يكشفُ لهُم حجابَ الحُسن، ويزيلُ دونَهم أسبابَ المادةِ نتيجةً لما يأخذونَ به أنفسهُم من مجاهدةٍ وخلوةٍ وذِكر، فتنعكسُ أبصارُهم في بصائرهِم، فينظرونَ بنورِ الله، وتنمَحي أمامهُم مقاييسُ الزمانِ والمَكان، فيطَّلعون على عوالمَ من أمرِ اللهِ اطلاعاً لا يستطيعهُ من لا يزالُ في قيدِ الشهواتِ والشكوكِ والبدعِ العقائديةِ والوساوسِ الشيطانيَّة، ولا تتسعُ له إِلا تلكَ القلوبُ النيّرةِ السليمةِ التي زالَت عَنها ظلماتُ الدُّنيا وغَواشيها، وانقشعَت عَنها غيومُ الشكوكِ ووساوسُها، وكثافةُ المادِّيات وأوضارِها، نَعم، إِنَّ من غضَّ بصرهُ عن المحارِم، وكفَّ نفسهُ عن الشَّهوات، وعمَّر باطنهُ بمراقبةِ اللهِ تَعالى، وتعوَّد أكلَ الحلال، لم يُخطىء كشفهُ وفراستُه، ومن أطلقَ نظرهُ إلى المحرماتِ تنفَّست نفسهُ الظلمانيِّة في مرآةِ قلبه، فطمسَت نورُه، ويرجعُ هذا الكشفُ إلى أن العبدَ إذا انصرفَ عن الحسِّ الظاهرِ إلى الحسِّ الباطن، تغلبَت روحهُ على نفسهِ الحيوانيَّة المتلبسةِ ببدنهِ، والروحُ لطيفة كشَّافة، فيحصلُ له حينئذٍ الكشفُ ويتلقَّى وارداتِ الإِلهام.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 93: What is unveiling (kashf)?

Answer: Sayyid al-Jurjānī (may Allah have mercy on him) said in his Ta‘rīfāt: “In language, firāsa (intuition/discernment) means ascertaining and looking attentively. In the terminology of the people of reality, it is the unveiling of certainty and the direct witnessing of the unseen.”⁽¹⁾

The knower of Allah, Ibn ‘Ajība (may Allah have mercy on him), said: “Firāsa is a sudden thought that strikes the heart, or an inspiration that manifests within it. It rarely errs when the heart is purified. In the hadith it is stated: ‘Beware of the firāsa of the believer, for he sees with the light of Allah.’”⁽²⁾ Its accuracy depends on the strength of one’s nearness and gnosis: the stronger the proximity and the more firmly established the gnosis, the more truthful the firāsa becomes. This is because when the spirit draws near to the Divine Presence of the Real, what usually manifests in it is the truth.⁽³⁾

Unveiling (kashf) is a light that the wayfarers receive during their journey to Allah Most Exalted. It removes from them the veils of beauty and lifts material causes, as a result of the spiritual struggle, seclusion, and remembrance they undertake. Their outward eyes are reflected into their inner insight, so they see with the light of Allah. The measures of time and place vanish before them, and they behold worlds of Allah’s command in a way impossible for one still bound by desires, doubts, doctrinal innovations, or satanic whisperings. Such unveiling is granted only to enlightened, sound hearts from which the darkness and coverings of the world have been removed, the clouds of doubt and whisperings have cleared, and the density and impurities of materiality have dispersed.

Indeed, whoever lowers his gaze from the unlawful, restrains his soul from base desires, fills his inner being with vigilance toward Allah Most High, and accustoms himself to eating only the lawful—such a person’s unveiling and discernment will rarely err. But whoever freely casts his gaze toward the forbidden allows his darkened soul to breathe upon the mirror of his heart, thereby effacing its light.

The reality of this unveiling is that when the servant turns away from the outward senses toward the inward sense, his spirit overcomes the animal self that clings to his body. The spirit is a subtle essence that unveils, and so unveiling occurs for him at that point, and he receives Divine inspirations.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 94) حدثنا عن الكشف عند رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه؟

الجواب: الكشفُ له صلوات الله وسلامه عليه مُعجزة، وللصحابةِ والأولياءِ من بعدِه كَرامة، وكلُّ كرامةٍ لوليِّ معجزةٌ لنبيِّه صلوات الله وسلامه عليه. عن "أنس" رضوان الله عنه قال: أقيمتِ الصَّلاة، فأقبلَ علينا رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بوجههِ فقال: (أقيموا صفوفَكم وتراصُّوا، فإني أراكُم من وراءِ ظَهري).⁽¹⁾

ولمّا كان الكشفُ بعيداً عن عالمِ الحِس، وينمَحي أمامهُ المقياسُ الزمانيّ والمكانيّ، بذلكَ كانَ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يَستوي عندهُ في الرؤيةِ القًرب والبُعد. يقول "أنس" رضوان الله عليه: بعثَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه "زيداً" و"جعفراً" و"ابن رواحة"، ورفعَ الرايةَ إلى زَيد، فأُصيبوا جميعاً، فنعاهُم رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه إلى الناس قبلَ أن يجيءَ الخَبر، فقال: (أخذَ الرايةَ زيد فأُصيب، ثمَّ أخذَها جعفر فأُصيب، ثم أخذَها عبدُ اللهِ بن رواحةَ فأُصيب، وإن عينيّ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه تذرِفان، ثمَّ أخذها خالدُ بن الوليدِ من غير إمرة، ففُتِح له). قالهُ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يومَ غزوةِ مُؤتة.⁽²⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 94: Tell us about the unveiling (kashf) of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?

Answer: The unveilings granted to him ﷺ were miracles (mu‘jiza), while for the Companions and the saints after him they were miraculous gifts (karāma). Every karāma granted to a saint is a mu‘jiza when attributed to his Prophet ﷺ.

Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported: “The iqāma for prayer was called, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ turned to us with his face and said: ‘Straighten your rows and stand close together, for indeed I see you from behind my back.’”⁽¹⁾

Since unveiling is far removed from the sensory realm and effaces the measures of time and place, near and far were equal in his vision ﷺ. Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent Zayd, Ja‘far, and Ibn Rawāḥa. He raised the banner for Zayd. They were all struck down. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ informed the people of their martyrdom before the news arrived. He said: ‘Zayd took the banner and was struck down. Then Ja‘far took it and was struck down. Then ‘Abdullāh ibn Rawāḥa took it and was struck down.’ And the eyes of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ were shedding tears. Then Khālid ibn al-Walīd took it without appointment, and victory was granted to him.” He ﷺ said this on the day of the Battle of Mu’ta.⁽²⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 95) هل ورد الكشف في القرآن الكريم وما هي أدلته؟
الجواب: قالَ اللهُ تَعالى في حقِّ "إبراهيم الخليل" عليه السلام: ﴿وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُرِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ مَلَكُوتَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلِيَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُوقِنِينَ﴾.⁽¹⁾
وكذلكَ ما أخبرَ اللهُ عزَّ وجلَّ عن "الخضر" عليه السلام، حين صحِب "موسى" عليه السلام في المسائلِ الثَّلاث:

الأولى: انكشفَ لـ "الخضر" أنَّ السفينةَ التي ركبَها مَجاناً في طريقهِم عبرَ البَحر، سيأخذُها ملكٌ غاشمٌ ظلماً، فخرقَها ليعيبَها ولينقذَها من شرِّ ذلكَ الغاصِب مكافأةً للمعروفِ بالمَعروف: ﴿أَمَّا السَّفِينَةُ فَكَانَتْ لِمَسَاكِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ فِي الْبَحْرِ فَأَرَدتُّ أَنْ أَعِيبَهَا وَكَانَ وَرَاءَهُم مَّلِكٌ يَأْخُذُ كُلَّ سَفِينَةٍ غَصْبًا﴾.⁽²⁾

الثانية: كشفَ لهُ عن الغُلام، إن بَقي حياً فسيقتُل أبويهِ في كبرِه، ويوقعهُما في الُكفر، فقتلهُ رحمةً بأبويهِ المؤمنَيْن، واستجابةً لإرادةِ اللهِ تَعالى بإبدالهِ بخيرٍ منهُ زكاةً ورحمَة: ﴿وَأَمَّا الْغُلَامُ فَكَانَ أَبَوَاهُ مُؤْمِنَيْنِ فَخَشِينَا أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا طُغْيَانًا وَكُفْرًا ۝ فَأَرَدْنَا أَن يُبْدِلَهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهُ زَكَاةً وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْمًا﴾.⁽³⁾

الثالثة: كشفَ له الكنزَ الذي تحتَ الجدِار، وكانَ لغلامينِ يتيمينِ من أبٍ صالح، فأقامَ الجدارَ حفظاً للكِنز، ورحمةً للغلامَين، ومحبةً لأبيهِما الصَّالح، بلا أجرٍ وبلا مُقابل، مروءةً وإخلاصاً: ﴿وَأَمَّا الْجِدَارُ فَكَانَ لِغُلَامَيْنِ يَتِيمَيْنِ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ وَكَانَ تَحْتَهُ كَنزٌ لَّهُمَا وَكَانَ أَبُوهُمَا صَالِحًا فَأَرَادَ رَبُّكَ أَن يَبْلُغَا أَشُدَّهُمَا وَيَسْتَخْرِجَا كَنزَهُمَا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ﴾.⁽⁴⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 95: Has unveiling (kashf) been mentioned in the Noble Qur’an? What are the proofs in support of it?

Answer: Allah Most Exalted said concerning Ibrāhīm the intimate friend (peace be upon him): “And thus did We show Ibrāhīm the kingdom of the heavens and the earth that he would be among those of certainty.” (Quran 6:75)⁽¹⁾
Similarly, Allah Most Mighty and Majestic informed us about al-Khiḍr (peace be upon him) when he accompanied Mūsā (peace be upon him) in the three matters:

First: It was unveiled to al-Khiḍr that the boat they boarded free of charge to cross the sea would be seized by an oppressive king who confiscated every good vessel by force. So he damaged it deliberately to make it undesirable and thereby save it from the tyrant’s injustice, repaying a favor with a favor: “As for the ship, it belonged to poor people working at sea. So I intended to cause defect in it as there was after them a king who seized every [good] ship by force.” (Quran 18:79)⁽²⁾

Second: The state of the boy was unveiled to him: had he lived, he would have grown up to kill his parents and plunge them into disbelief. So he killed him out of mercy for his believing parents and in fulfillment of Allah’s will to replace him with one better in purity and closer in mercy: “And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared that he would overburden them by transgression and disbelief. So we intended that their Lord should substitute for them one better than him in purity and nearer to mercy.” (Quran 18:80–81)⁽³⁾

Third: The treasure beneath the wall was unveiled to him; it belonged to two orphan boys whose father had been righteous. So he repaired the wall to protect the treasure, out of mercy for the two orphans and love for their righteous father—all without reward or compensation, purely out of chivalry and sincerity: “And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and beneath it was a treasure for them, and their father had been righteous. So your Lord intended that they reach maturity and extract their treasure, as a mercy from your Lord…” (Quran 18:82)⁽⁴⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 96) كيف كان الكشف عند الصحابة الكرام رضوان الله عليهم أجمعين؟

الجواب: الكَشف عند سيدِنا "أبي بكر" رضوان الله عليه، وهو الذي شهدَ اللهُ له بالصدِّيقية بقوله: ﴿وَالَّذِي جَاءَ بِالصِّدْقِ﴾ وهوَ ﴿رَّسُولُ اللَّهِ﴾ ﴿وَصَدَّقَ بِهِ﴾ وهو أبو بَكر الصدِّيق رضوان الله عَليه.. وإنني أذكرُ واقعةً واحدةً من كَثير، تكشفُ لَنا الغطاءَ عَن ذلك، ومِن أين لإنسانٍ أن يُحصي مآثرَ "أبي بكر" رضوان الله عليه.

(عن "عروة" عن أبيهِ رضوان الله عليهما، عن "عائشة" رضوان الله عليها: أن "أبا بكر" لما حضرتهُ الوفاة دَعاها فقال: إنهُ ليسَ في أهلي بَعدي أحدٌ أحبُّ إِليَّ غنىً مِنك، ولا أعزُّ عليَّ فقراً مِنك، وإِني كنت نحلتك⁽¹⁾ من أرضٍ بالعاليةِ جداد يعني: صرام -عشرينَ وسقاً⁽²⁾- فلو كنت جَددته تمراً عاماً واحداً انحازَ لَك، وإِنما هُو مالُ الوارث، وإِنما هُما أخواكِ وأختاكِ. فقلتُ: إِنما هي "أسماء" فقال: وذاتُ بطن ابنةٍ خارِجة، قد أُلقي في رَوعي أنها جاريةٌ فاستَوصي بها خيراً، فولدَت "أم كلثوم").⁽³⁾

قالَ "التّاج السبكي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: وفيه كرامَتان لـ "أبي بكر" رضوان الله عليه: إحداهُما إخبارهِ أنهُ يموتُ في ذلكَ المَرض، حيثُ يقول (وإنما هو اليومُ مالُ وارِث) والثانيَة إخبارُه بمولودٍ يولدُ له، وهوَ جارِية، والسرُّ في إِظهارِ ذلكَ استطابَة قلبِ "عائشة" رضوان الله عليها في استرجاعِ ما وهبهُ لَها، ولم تقبضهُ وإعلامُها بمقدارِ ما يخصُّها، لتكونَ على ثِقة، فأخبرَها بأنهُ مال وارث، وأنَّ مَعها أخوينِ وأختَين.⁽⁴⁾
الكشفُ عند "عمر بن الخطاب" الخليفةُ الثاني رضوان الله عليه، وقد شهِد لهُ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه أنَّه من الملهَمين:

عن "أبي هريرة" رضوان الله عليه قال: قالَ رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (لقَد كانَ فيمَن قبلكُم من الأمَم ناسٌ مُحدَّثون، فإن يكُ في أمَّتي أحدٌ فإنَّه عُمر).⁽⁵⁾ فإن أمَّته صلواتُ الله وسلامه عليهِ أفضل الأمَم، وإذا ثبتَ أنهم وُجدوا في غيرِها فوجودهِم فيها أَولى، وإنما أوردهُ موردُ التأكيد، كقولِ القائِل: إن كانَ لي صَديق ففُلان، يريدُ اختصاصَ كمالِ الصَّداقة لا نَفيها عن غَيره، والمحدَّث: هو الملهَم الصادِق الظَّن، وهو من أُوقع في قلبهِ شيءٌ من قِبل المَلأ الأعلى، فيكون كالذي حَدثه غيرُه.

قال "التاج السبكي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: (كان "عمر" رضوان الله عليه قد أمرَ "سارية بن زنيم الخلجي" على جيشٍ من جيوشِ المسلمين، وجهَّزه على بلادِ فارِس، فاشتدَّ على عسكرِه الحال على بابِ نهاوُند وهو يحاصِرها، وكثرَت جموعُ الأعداء، وكادَ المسلمونَ ينهزِمون، و"عمر" رضوان الله عليهِ بالمدينَة، فصعِد المِنبر وخَطب، ثم استغاثَ في أثناءِ خطبتِه بأعلى صوتِه: (يا "سارِية": الجَبل، من استرعَى الذئبَ الغنَم فقد ظلَم⁽⁶⁾ فأسمعَ اللهَ تَعالى "سارية" وجَيشه أجمَعين، وهم على بابِ نهاوند صَوت "عمر"، فلَجئوا إلى الجَبل، وقالوا هذا صوتُ أميرِ المؤمِنين، فنَجوا وانتصَروا.

وقال "التاج السبكي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: (لم يَقصد إظهار الكَرامة، وإنما كُشف له، ورأى القَوم عياناً، وكانَ كمَن هوَ بين أظهرهِم حَقيقة، وغابَ عن مجلسِه بالمَدينة واشتغلَت حَواسه بِما دهمَ المُسلمين، فخاطبَ أميرهم ومَن هُو مَعه).⁽⁷⁾
ففي هذه القصَّة شَيئان:
الأول: الكشفُ الصَّحيح والرؤيةُ العيانيَّة عن بُعد آلافِ الأميال، وأين "التلفزيون" في مثلِ هذهِ القصِّة الواقعةِ قبلَ أربعةَ عشرَ قرناً؟
الثاني: إبلاغُ صوتهِ "سارية" على هذا البُعد الشاسِع.
ورأى "عمر" رضوان الله عليه قوماً من مذحج فيهِم الأشتر، فصعَّد النظرَ فيهِ وصوَّب ثمَّ قال: (قاتلهُ الله إِني لأرى المسلمينَ منه يوماً عصيباً فكانَ منهُ ما كان).⁽⁸⁾

وأخرجَ "ابن عساكر" عن "طارق بن شهاب" قال: (إن كانَ الرجلَ ليحدِّث "عمر" بالحديثِ فيكْذِبُه الكِذبة فيقول: احبِس هذه، ثم يحدِّثه بالحَديث فيقول: احبِس هذه، فيَقول له: كلّ ما حدثتكَ حقٌّ إلا ما أمرتَني أن أحبِسه). وأخرجَ عن "الحسن" قال: (إِن كانَ أحد يعرفُ الكذِب إذا حُدّث فهو "عمر بن الخطاب".⁽⁹⁾

وأخرجَ البيهقي في الدلائلِ عن "أبي هدية الحمصي" قال: (أخبرَ "عمر" بأنَّ أهلَ العراقِ حَصبوا أميرهُم، فخرجَ غَضبان، فصلَّى فَسَها في صلاتِه، فلما سلَّم قال: اللهمَّ إنهُم قد لبِسوا عليّ فالبِس عَليهم، وعجِّل عليهم بالغلامِ "الثقفي" يحكمُ فيهِم بحكمِ الجاهليَّة، لا يقبلُ من محسنهِم، ولا يتجاوزُ عن مسيئهِم).
قلت: (أشارَ به إلى الحجَّاج، قال "ابن لهيعة": وما وُلدَ الحَجاج يومئِذ).
كشفَ "عثمان بن عفان" رضوان الله عليه:

ذكر "التاج السبكي" رحمهُ اللهُ تعالى في الطَّبقات وغيره: (أنه دخلَ على عُثمان رضوانُ اللهِ عليهِ رَجل، كان قد لقِي امرأةً في الطَّريق، فتأمَّلها، فقالَ له "عثمان" رضوان الله عليه: يدخل أحدكُم، وفي عينيهِ أثرَ الزِّنا، فقالَ الرجل: أوحيَ بعدَ رسولِ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه؟ قال: لا، ولكنَّها فِراسة المؤمن). وإنما أظهرَ عثمانَ هذا تأديباً للرَّجل، وزجراً له عن شيءٍ فعلَه.⁽¹⁰⁾
الكشف عند علي بن أبي طالب رضوان الله عليه:

الذي ربَّاه رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه في حِجره، ولما آخى النبيُّ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بينَ أصحابهِ قالَ له: (أنتَ أخي)⁽¹¹⁾ وقالَ له أيضاً: (ألا تَرضى أن تكونَ منِّي بمنزلة "هارون" من "موسى"؟⁽¹²⁾)

عن "الأصبغ" رحمهُ الله تعالى قال: (أتَينا مع "عليّ" فمررْنا بموضعِ قبرِ "الحسين"، فقال "علي": ههنا مُناخ ركابهِم، وههنا موضعُ رحالهِم، وههنا مهراقُ دمائهِم، فتيةٌ من آلِ محمدٍ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه يُقتلون بهذه العَرصة، تبكي عليهِم السماءُ والأرض).⁽¹³⁾
وقال "علي" رضوانُ الله عليهِ لأهلِ الكوفة: (سينزلُ بكم أهلُ بيتِ رسولِ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، فيستغيثونَ بكُم فلَم يُغاثوا) فكانَ منهم في شأنِ الحسينِ ما كان.⁽¹⁴⁾
ولَو أردنا أن نستقصيَ تراجمَ الصحابةِ الكِرام رضوان الله عليهم أجمَعين في كشفهِم وفراستهِم، لخرَجنا عن موضوعِنا في رسالتِنا هذه.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 96: What can you tell us about the unveiling experienced by the noble Companions (may Allah be pleased with them all)?

Answer: The unveiling experienced by our master Abu Bakr —concerning whom Allah bore witness to his truthfulness: “And the one who brought the truth and believed in it…” (Quran 39:33)—is exemplified in one incident among many, as it is nearly impossible to enumerate the virtues of Abu Bakr .

‘Urwa related from his father, from ‘A’isha , that when Abu Bakr was near death he called her and said: “There is no one in my family after me whom I wish to be wealthy more than you, nor whose poverty is harder on me than yours. I had gifted you land in al-‘Aliya—twenty awsaq of date palms (meaning clusters ready for harvest). If you had harvested it for one year it would have been yours. But it is now the property of heirs, and they are your two brothers and two sisters.” She said, “There is only Asma’.” He replied, “And the one in the womb who will soon be born. It has been inspired to me that it is a girl, so treat her well.” She later gave birth to Umm Kulthum.⁽¹⁾

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “This contains two miracles of Abu Bakr : first, his foretelling that he would die from that illness (as indicated by his words, ‘It is now the property of heirs’); second, his foretelling of a child to be born—a girl. The secret in his revealing this was to bring comfort to ‘A’isha’s heart regarding the return of what he had gifted her (which she had not yet taken possession of) and to inform her precisely of what belonged to her, so she could be at ease. Thus he told her it was heirs’ property and that she had two brothers and sisters.”⁽²⁾
The unveiling of ‘Umar b. al-Khattab , the second Caliph, whom the Messenger of Allah  testified was among the inspired:

Abu Hurayra  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: “Among the nations before you were people who were inspired (muhaddathun), and if there is to be anyone like that in my nation, it is ‘Umar.”⁽³⁾ Since his nation is the best of nations, and inspired people existed in previous nations, their existence in this nation is more fitting. The wording is for emphasis, as one might say, “If I have a friend, it is so-and-so,” intending complete friendship without negating it from others. An inspired person receives truthful inner promptings from the higher assembly, as though someone else had informed him.

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “‘Umar  had appointed Sariya b. Zunaym al-Khalji as commander over a Muslim army sent to the lands of Persia. The situation at the gate of Nahawand grew severe as they besieged it. Enemy forces multiplied and the Muslims were near defeat. Meanwhile ‘Umar was in Medina. He ascended the pulpit and delivered the sermon. Then, in the midst of it, he cried out at the top of his voice: ‘O Sariya! To the mountain! Whoever entrusts the wolf with the sheep has wronged himself!’⁽⁴⁾ Allah caused Sariya and his entire army to hear ‘Umar’s voice while they were at the gate of Nahawand. They took refuge on the mountain, saying, ‘That is the voice of the Commander of the Believers!’ They were saved and gained victory.”

Taj al-Subki added: “He did not intend to manifest a miracle. Rather, it was unveiled to him and he saw the people directly, as though he were truly among them. He was absent from his gathering in Medina, his senses occupied with what threatened the Muslims, so he addressed their commander as one present with him.”⁽⁵⁾
This incident contains two points:
First: Accurate unveiling and direct vision across thousands of miles. Where does television stand compared to this real event from fourteen centuries ago?
Second: His voice reaching Sariya over such vast distance.
‘Umar also saw a group from Madh-haj including al-Ashtar, looked intently at him, and said, “May Allah kill him! I see the Muslims having a difficult day from him.” And later this came to pass.⁽⁶⁾

Ibn ‘Asakir recorded from Tariq b. Shihab: “A man would speak to ‘Umar. Whenever he lied, ‘Umar would say, ‘Hold this one back!’ He would continue, and ‘Umar would repeat, ‘Hold this one back!’ The man said, ‘Everything I said was true except what you ordered me to hold back!’”⁽⁷⁾
Ibn ‘Asakir also recorded from al-Hasan: “If anyone knows a lie when it is spoken to him, it is ‘Umar b. al-Khattab!”⁽⁸⁾

Al-Bayhaqi recorded in his Dala’il from Abu Hadya al-Himsi: “‘Umar was informed that the people of Iraq had stoned their governor, so he came out angry, prayed, and was forgetful in his prayer. After the salutation he said, ‘O Allah! They have confused me, so confuse them! Hasten upon them the Thaqafi child who will rule them with the rule of Jahiliyya—he accepts no good from their good-doers and overlooks no evil from their evildoers!’” (He referred to al-Hajjaj. Ibn Lahi‘a said, “Al-Hajjaj was not yet born at that time.”)⁽⁹⁾
The unveiling of ‘Uthman b. ‘Affan :

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) mentioned in al-Tabaqat and elsewhere: “A man entered upon ‘Uthman  who had met a woman on the road and looked at her. ‘Uthman said to him, ‘Some of you enter, and in his eyes are traces of fornication!’ The man asked, ‘Is there revelation after the Messenger of Allah ?’ He replied, ‘No, rather it is the intuition (firasa) of the believer.’” ‘Uthman only manifested this to discipline and rebuke the man for what he had done.⁽¹⁰⁾
The unveiling of ‘Ali b. Abi Talib :

He was raised by the Messenger of Allah  in his house. When the Prophet paired the Companions as brothers, he said to him, “You are my brother.”⁽¹¹⁾ He also said, “Are you not pleased to be to me as Harun was to Musa?”⁽¹²⁾

Asbagh (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “We were with ‘Ali when we passed by the site of Husayn’s grave. ‘Ali said, ‘Here is where their mounts rested, here is where their loads were placed, and here is where their blood was shed. Youth from the family of Muhammad  will be killed on this plain. The sky and earth weep over them.’”⁽¹³⁾

‘Ali  also said to the people of Kufa: “The family of Allah’s Messenger  will come to you seeking your aid, but they will not receive it.” Later the events concerning Husayn unfolded as foretold.⁽¹⁴⁾
If we were to recount all the biographies of the noble Companions (may Allah be pleased with them all) regarding their unveilings and inspirations, we would depart from the scope of this book.
<div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 96: What can you tell us about the unveiling experienced by the noble Companions (may Allah be pleased with them all)?

Answer: The unveiling of our master Abu Bakr —whom Allah bore witness to as the truthful one: “And the one who brought the truth and believed in it…” (Quran 39:33)—is illustrated in one incident among many, as it is nearly impossible to enumerate the virtues of Abu Bakr .

‘Urwa related from his father, from ‘A’isha , that when Abu Bakr was near death he called her and said: “There is no one in my family after me whom I wish to be wealthy more than you, nor whose poverty is harder on me than yours. I had gifted you land in al-‘Aliya—twenty awsaq of date palms (meaning clusters ready for harvest). If you had harvested it for one year it would have been yours. But it is now the property of heirs, and they are your two brothers and two sisters.” She said, “There is only Asma’.” He replied, “And the one in the womb who will soon be born. It has been inspired to me that it is a girl, so treat her well.” She later gave birth to Umm Kulthum.⁽¹⁾

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “This contains two miracles of Abu Bakr : first, his foretelling that he would die from that illness (as indicated by his words, ‘It is now the property of heirs’); second, his foretelling of a child to be born—a girl. The secret in his revealing this was to bring comfort to ‘A’isha’s heart regarding the return of what he had gifted her (which she had not yet taken possession of) and to inform her precisely of what belonged to her, so she could be at ease. Thus he told her it was heirs’ property and that she has two brothers and sisters.”⁽²⁾
The unveiling of ‘Umar b. al-Khattab , the second Caliph, whom the Messenger of Allah  testified was among the inspired:

Abu Hurayra  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: “Among the nations before you were people who were inspired (muhaddathun), and if there is to be anyone like that in my nation, it is ‘Umar.”⁽³⁾ Since his nation is the best of nations, and inspired people existed in previous nations, their existence in this nation is more fitting. The wording is for emphasis, as one might say, “If I have a friend, it is so-and-so,” intending complete friendship without negating it from others. An inspired person receives truthful inner promptings from the higher assembly, as though someone else had informed him.

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “‘Umar  had appointed Sariya b. Zunaym al-Khalji as commander over a Muslim army sent to the lands of Persia. The situation at the gate of Nahawand grew severe as they besieged it. Enemy forces multiplied and the Muslims were near defeat. Meanwhile ‘Umar was in Medina. He ascended the pulpit and delivered the sermon. Then, in the midst of it, he cried out at the top of his voice: ‘O Sariya! To the mountain! Whoever entrusts the wolf with the sheep has wronged himself!’⁽⁴⁾ Allah caused Sariya and his entire army to hear ‘Umar’s voice while they were at the gate of Nahawand. They took refuge on the mountain, saying, ‘That is the voice of the Commander of the Believers!’ They were saved and gained victory.”

Taj al-Subki added: “He did not intend to manifest a miracle. Rather, it was unveiled to him and he saw the people directly, as though he were truly among them. He was absent from his gathering in Medina, his senses occupied with what threatened the Muslims, so he addressed their commander as one present with him.”⁽⁵⁾
This incident contains two points:
First: Accurate unveiling and direct vision across thousands of miles. Where does television stand compared to this real event from fourteen centuries ago?
Second: His voice reaching Sariya over such vast distance.
‘Umar also saw a group from Madh-haj including al-Ashtar, looked intently at him, and said, “May Allah kill him! I see the Muslims having a difficult day from him.” And later this came to pass.⁽⁶⁾

Ibn ‘Asakir recorded from Tariq b. Shihab: “A man would speak to ‘Umar. Whenever he lied, ‘Umar would say, ‘Hold this one back!’ He would continue, and ‘Umar would repeat, ‘Hold this one back!’ The man said, ‘Everything I said was true except what you ordered me to hold back!’”⁽⁷⁾
Ibn ‘Asakir also recorded from al-Hasan: “If anyone knows a lie when it is spoken to him, it is ‘Umar b. al-Khattab!”⁽⁸⁾

Al-Bayhaqi recorded in his Dala’il from Abu Hadya al-Himsi: “‘Umar was informed that the people of Iraq had stoned their governor, so he came out angry, prayed, and was forgetful in his prayer. After the salutation he said, ‘O Allah! They have confused me, so confuse them! Hasten upon them the Thaqafi child who will rule them with the rule of Jahiliyya—he accepts no good from their good-doers and overlooks no evil from their evildoers!’” (He referred to al-Hajjaj. Ibn Lahi‘a said, “Al-Hajjaj was not yet born at that time.”)⁽⁹⁾
The unveiling of ‘Uthman b. ‘Affan :

Taj al-Subki (may Allah have mercy on him) mentioned in al-Tabaqat and elsewhere: “A man entered upon ‘Uthman  who had met a woman on the road and looked at her. ‘Uthman said to him, ‘Some of you enter, and in his eyes are traces of fornication!’ The man asked, ‘Is there revelation after the Messenger of Allah ?’ He replied, ‘No, rather it is the intuition (firasa) of the believer.’” ‘Uthman only manifested this to discipline and rebuke the man for what he had done.⁽¹⁰⁾
The unveiling of ‘Ali b. Abi Talib :

He was raised by the Messenger of Allah  in his house. When the Prophet paired the Companions as brothers, he said to him, “You are my brother.”⁽¹¹⁾ He also said, “Are you not pleased to be to me as Harun was to Musa?”⁽¹²⁾

Asbagh (may Allah have mercy on him) said: “We were with ‘Ali when we passed by the site of Husayn’s grave. ‘Ali said, ‘Here is where their mounts rested, here is where their loads were placed, and here is where their blood was shed. Youth from the family of Muhammad  will be killed on this plain. The sky and earth weep over them.’”⁽¹³⁾

‘Ali  also said to the people of Kufa: “The family of Allah’s Messenger  will come to you seeking your aid, but they will not receive it.” Later the events concerning Husayn unfolded as foretold.⁽¹⁴⁾

If we were to recount all the biographies of the noble Companions (may Allah be pleased with them all) and the examples of their unveilings and inspirations, we would depart from the scope of this book.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 97) كيف يكون كشف العارفين؟

الجواب: روي عن الإمام "الشافعي" و"محمد بن الحسن" رحمهُما الله تعالى: (أنَّهما كانا بفناءِ الكَعبة، ورجلٌ على بابِ المسجدِ الحَرام فرأيتُ فقيراً عليه خِرقتان، فقلتُ في نَفسي: هذا وأشباههُ كلّ على النَّاس، فناداني وقال: ﴿وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ فَاحْذَرُوهُ﴾⁽¹⁾ فاستغفرتُ اللهَ في سرِّي.. فناداني وقال: ﴿وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِ﴾⁽²⁾. ثم غابَ عنِّي، ولم أرَه).⁽³⁾

ومثلَ هذا وقعَ لغيرِه، يقول "خير النسَّاج" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: (كنتُ جالساً في بَيتي، فوقعَ لي أن "الجنيد" بالباب، فنفيتُ عن قَلبي ذلك، فوقعَ ثانياً وثالثاً، فخرَجت، فإذا "الجنيد"، فقال: لِمَ لم تخرُج مع الخاطرِ الأوَّل؟).⁽⁴⁾

وحُكي عن "إبراهيم الخوّاص" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى قال: (كنتُ في بَغداد في جامعِ المَدينة، وهناكَ جماعةٌ من الفُقراء، فأقبلَ شابٌ ظريفٌ طيِّب الرائِحة، حسنُ الحُرمة، حسنُ الوَجه، فقلتُ لأصحابِنا: يقعُ لي أنه يَهودي، فكلهُم كرِهوا ذلك، فخرجتُ وخرجَ الشَّاب، ثم رجعَ إليهِم وقال: إِيش قال الشِّيخ؟ فاحتشَموه، فألحَّ عليهِم فقالوا: قال: إنَّك يَهودي. قال: فجاءَني، وأكبَّ على يَدي وأسلَم، فقيل: ما السَّبب؟ قال نجدُ في كتبِنا أنَّ الصِّديق لا تُخطئ فِراسته فقلت: أمتحنُ المُسلمين، فتأملتهُم فقُلت: إن كانَ فيهِم صدّيق، ففي هذهِ الطائفة لأنهُم يقولونَ حديثهُ سبحانَه، فلبّستُ عليهم، فلما اطّلع عليّ وتفرَّس فيَّ علمتُ أنَّه صدّيق، وصارَ الشابُ من كبارِ الصوفيَّة). ولا عجبَ في ذلك، فقد أخبرَ عن هذا رسولُ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه بقوله: (إنَّ للهِ عِباداً يعرفونَ الناسَ بالتوسُّم).⁽⁵⁾

ووقفَ "نصراني" على "الجنيد" رحمهُ اللهُ تعالى، وهو يتكلمُ في الجامعِ على النَّاس، فقال: أيُّها الشَّيخ: ما مَعنى حَديث: (اتَّقوا فراسَة المؤمِن، فإنهُ ينظرُ بنورِ الله؟) فأطرق "الجنيد" ثم رفعَ رأسهُ وقال: أسلمْ فقَد جاءَ وقتُ إسلامِك، فأسلمَ الغُلام.⁽⁶⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 97: What can you tell us about the unveiling experienced by the gnostics (al-‘ārifīn)?

Answer: It was narrated from Imam al-Shāfi‘ī and Muḥammad ibn al-Ḥasan (may Allah have mercy on both of them) that they were in the courtyard of the Ka‘ba and a man was at the door of the Sacred Mosque. One of them said, “I think he is a carpenter.” The other said, “I think he is a blacksmith.” Those who were with the two approached the man at the door of the Sacred Mosque and asked him (about his profession), to which he replied, “Previously I was a blacksmith, but these days I am a carpenter.”⁽¹⁾

Similar incidents happened to others as well. Khayr al-Nassāj (may Allah have mercy on him) said, “Once, when I was sitting in my house, it occurred to me that Junayd was at the door. I cast the thought from my heart, but the same thought appeared a second and third time, so when I went out, lo and behold, it was Junayd. He said, ‘Why didn’t you come out after the first passing thought?’”⁽²⁾

It was related that Ibrāhīm al-Khawwāṣ (may Allah have mercy on him) said, “Once in Baghdad, when I was in the large congregational mosque in the city, there was a group of dervishes and among them was a young man of elegant appearance, fine fragrance, pleasant countenance, and handsome face. I said to our companions, ‘It seems to me that he is a Jew,’ yet they seemed incredulous to the suggestion. I left and soon thereafter the young man left. He returned to them a while later and asked, ‘What did the Shaykh say?’ They were ashamed to inform him, but he continued to pressure them until they finally informed him, saying, ‘He says that you are a Jew.’ He immediately came over to me, took my hand, and embraced Islam. It was asked of him regarding his reason for embracing Islam, and he replied, ‘We find it in our scriptures that the truthful person’s intuition does not err, so I wanted to test the Muslims. I thought deeply about them and said to myself, “If there is to be found a truthful person among them, he will surely be found among this group, for they speak the words of the Exalted”; so I deceived them, and when he (Ibrāhīm al-Khawwāṣ) discovered me and saw me for who I truly was, I knew that he was a truthful person.’ This young man later became one of the major Sufis.”⁽³⁾
This should not come as a surprise, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ informed us of it when he said, “Indeed to Allah belongs servants who know the people through intuitive signs (tawassum).”⁽⁴⁾

Once when Junayd was speaking to people in a large congregational mosque, a Christian called out to him, “O Shaykh! What is the meaning of the hadith, ‘Beware of the intuition of the believer, for he looks with the light of Allah’?” Junayd lowered his head for some moments and then he looked up and said, “Embrace Islam, for the time for your Islam has come.” Thereafter, the young man embraced Islam.⁽⁵⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 98) ما هي الأسباب التي قد تحجب عن الكشف؟

الجواب: إنَّ كلَّ معصيةٍ لها كَدر، وتورثُ نكتةً سوداءَ في القَلب فيكونَ رَيْناً، كما قالَ تَعالى: ﴿كَلاَّ بَلْ رانَ عَلى قُلوبِهِم مَا كَانوا يًكْسِبُونَ﴾⁽¹⁾. إلى أن يستحكِم والعياذُ بالله، فيظلِم القلب وتغلقُ أبوابُ النورِ فيُطْبع عَليه، فلا يَبقى سبيلٌ إلى التوبَة، كما قال تعالى: ﴿وَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ﴾.⁽²⁾

إذا عرفتَ هذا فالصغيرةُ من المَعاصي تورِث كدراً بقدرِها – أي قدراً صغيراً- قريبُ المحوِ بالاستغفارِ وغيرهِ من المكفِّرات، ولا يدركهُ إلا ذو بصرِ حادٍّ كـ "عثمان" رضوان الله عليه، حيثُ أدرَك هذا الكِدر اليَسير، فإنَّ تأمُّل المرأةِ والنظرِ إليها أدركهُ "عثمان" وعرفَ أصلَه.⁽³⁾ وهذا مقامٌ عالٍ يخضَع لهُ كثيرٌ من المَقامات. وإذا انضمَّ إلى الصغيرةِ صغيرةً أخرى ازدادَ الكدر، وإذا تكاثرَت الذنوبُ حتى وصلَت - والعياذُ بالله - إلى ما وصفناهُ من ظلامِ القُلوب، صارَ بحيثُ يشاهدهُ كلُّ ذي بَصر، فمن رأى متضمخاً بالمَعاصي قد أظلَم قلبُه، ولم يتفرَّس فيهِ ذلك فليعلمَ أنهُ إنَّما لا يبصرهُ لما عندهُ من العَمى المانعِ للإبصار، وإلا فلَو كانَ بصيراً لأبصرِ هذا الظلامَ الدَّاجي، فبقدرِ بصرهِ يُبصر، فافهَم ما نتحفَك به).⁽⁴⁾
فَالفراسة أمرٌ جائزُ الوُقوع، وهي منحةٌ إلهيةٌ يكرمُ الله بها عبادهُ الصالحينَ الذينَ تمسَّكوا بدينهِم، وحفِظوا جوارحهُم، وصَقلوا قلوبهُم، وهذَّبوا نفوسهُم.

قال "المناوي" في "شرح الجامع الصغير" عند قولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (إنَّ لكل قومٍ فراسَة، وإنما يعرفُها الأشراف). قاعدةُ الفراسَة وأساسُها الغَض عن المحارِم، قال "الكرماني": مَن عمّر ظاهرهُ باتِّباع السنِّة، وباطنهُ بدوامِ المُراقبة، وكفَّ نفسهُ عن الشَّهوات، وغضَّ بصرَه عن المُخالفات، واعتادَ أكلَ الحلال، لم تٌخطئ فراستهُ أبداً. فمن وُفِّق لذلكَ أبصرَ الحقائقَ عياناً بقلبِه).⁽⁵⁾

وعلى كلٍ فالقلوبُ تختلفُ باختلافِ صقلِها وتنظيفِها من أدرانِ الذُّنوب المظلمَة، فهيَ كالزُّجاج كلَّما صُقل ازدادَ ثمنُه، وكشفَ الجراثيمَ التي لا تُرى.. فأينَ زجاجُ النافذةِ من زجاجِ المِجهر، كذلكَ لا تقاسُ القلوبُ الصافيةُ المصقولةُ بالقلوبِ المكدرةِ المظلِمة، ولا تُقاس الملائكةُ بالشَّياطين.
فمن جدَّ وجد، ومن سارَ على الدَّربِ وصَل، ومن أتقنَ المقدمةَ وصلَ إِلى النتيجَة، والبداياتُ تدلُّ على النِّهايات.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 98: What are some of the causes that may veil or block spiritual unveiling (kashf)?
Answer: Imam Yusuf al-Nabahani said:

Every sin brings with it a cloudiness and deposits a black spot upon the heart, producing rust, as the Exalted said: Nay, rather rust has covered their hearts due to what they used to earn. (Quran 83:14)⁽¹⁾ This continues until it becomes firmly entrenched—and we seek refuge in Allah!—darkening the heart and closing the doors of light, sealing it so that no path remains for repentance, as the Exalted said: And their hearts were sealed so they do not understand. (Quran 9:87)⁽²⁾

If you understand this, know that minor sins produce cloudiness in proportion to their weight—a small amount that is quickly removable by seeking forgiveness and other expiatory acts. Only those with sharp insight, such as ‘Uthman , perceive such minute cloudiness. ‘Uthman  perceived the slight cloudiness caused by contemplating a woman and recognized its origin.⁽³⁾ This is a lofty station that opens the way to many other stations. When one minor sin is joined to another, the cloudiness increases. When sins accumulate—Allah forbid—until they reach the level we described and the heart becomes darkened, it becomes outwardly visible to anyone with eyes. So whoever sees a person steeped in sins with a darkened heart yet cannot perceive it with spiritual insight should know that the only reason he fails to see it is his own blindness that prevents perception. Otherwise, if he truly had insight he would see this enveloping darkness. His vision corresponds to the strength of his sight—so understand what we present to you.⁽⁴⁾

Spiritual intuition (firāsa) is a rationally possible phenomenon and a Divine gift by which Allah honors His righteous servants who adhere firmly to their religion, guard their limbs, polish their hearts, and refine their souls. Al-Munāwī, in his commentary on al-Jāmi‘ al-Ṣaghīr, said regarding the hadith “Every people have an intuition, yet it is known only by the noble ones”:

The foundation and basis of intuition lie in lowering the gaze from the unlawful. Al-Kirmānī said: “Whoever adorns his outward with adherence to the Sunna, fills his inward with constant vigilance toward Allah, restrains his soul from base desires, lowers his gaze from what is contrary to the law, and accustoms himself to eating only the lawful—such a person’s intuition will never err. Whoever is granted success in this will behold realities directly with his heart.”⁽⁵⁾

In any case, hearts differ according to how well they are polished and cleansed from the filth of dark sins. They are like glass: the more it is polished the more valuable it becomes and the more it reveals germs invisible to the naked eye. How does window glass compare to microscope glass? Likewise, there is no comparison between pure, polished hearts and darkened, sullied hearts—just as angels are not comparable to devils. One who strives earnestly will find; one who walks the path will reach; one who masters the preliminaries will attain the result. Beginnings indicate endings.

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 99) ما معنى الإلهام، وما هو دليله؟

الجواب: قال الشريف "الجرجاني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى في تعريفاتِه: (الإلهام: ما يُلقى في الرُّوعِ بطريقِ الفَيض). وقيل: الإلهام ما وقعُ في القلبِ من عِلم. وهوَ يَدعو إلى العملِ من غيرِ استدلالٍ بآيَة، ولا نظرٍ في حجَّة).⁽¹⁾

والإلهامُ إما أن يكونَ من قِبَلِ اللهِ تَعالى، أو من قِبل ملائكتِه، يُفْهَم منهُ أمرٌ أو نهيٌ أو ترغيبٌ أو تَرهيب. أما الذي من قِبل اللهِ تَعالى: فحَكى لنا حضرةُ اللهِ تَعالى في كتابهِ عن "مريم" رضوانُ اللهِ عَليها حينما أوَتْ إِلى شجرةِ النَّخل في أيامِ الشِّتاء، فخاطبهَا بإِلهامٍ ووحيٍ من دونِ واسطَة وقالَ لها: ﴿وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ تُسَاقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا ۝ فَكُلِي وَاشْرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيْنًا﴾⁽²⁾ . قال الإمام "فخر الدين الرازي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى عندَ تفسيرِ هذه الآية: (إِنَّ ذلكَ كانَ على سبيلِ النفثِ في الرَّوع والإلهامِ والإلقاءِ في القَلب، كما كانَ في حقِّ "أمِّ موسى" عليهِ السلام في قوله: ﴿وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ﴾.⁽³⁾

وكذلكَ أخبرَنا عن "أُم موسى" عليهِ السَّلام، حينما ضاقَ بَها الحالُ من أمرِ ابنِها عليهِ السَّلام، وداهمَها جنودُ فرعَون لقتلِه، فألهُمها، وأوحى إليها بلا واسِطة، فقال تعالى: ﴿وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِي وَلَا تَحْزَنِي ۖ إِنَّا رَادُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ﴾.⁽⁴⁾ ألقَت ابنَها وفلذةُ كبدِها بينَ أمواجِ البحرِ الخضم، إلى أين يذهبُ هذا الولدُ الكَريم بينَ هياجِ موجِ البحرِ يا تُرى؟ إنَّه الهلاكُ بعينِه، لكنها كانَت على يقينٍ من أمرِها، لِما اعتادَت من سماعِ الوَحي الذي يأتيَها من ربِّها بلا واسِطة، في خلوتِها وجلوتِها.
وأما الإلهامُ من قِبل الملائِكة: فالمَلك يحدِّث الإنسان، كما قالَ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (وأما لَمَّةُ الملك فإيعادٌ بالخيرِ وتصديقٌ بالحقِّ، فمن وجدَ ذلكَ فليعلَم أنهُ من اللهِ فليحمدِ الله).⁽⁵⁾

قال الإِمام "فخر الدين الرازي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى عندَ قولهِ تَعالى: ﴿وَإِذْ قَالَتِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ اصْطَفَاكِ وَطَهَّرَكِ وَاصْطَفَاكِ عَلَىٰ نِسَاءِ الْعَالَمِينَ﴾.⁽⁶⁾ اعلَم أن مَريم عليها السَّلام ما كانَت من الأنبِياء لقولِه تعالى: ﴿وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِي إِلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرَىٰ﴾⁽⁷⁾ وإذا كانَ كذلكَ كانَ إرسالُ جِبريل عليه السلام كرامةً لهَا، وكلَّمها شفاهاً، وليسَ هذا خاصاً بها، بَل هناكَ كثيرٌ من الصالِحين كلَّمَتهم الملائكَة عليهِم السَّلام).. فقَد رُوي أن رسولَ الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه قال: (إن رجلاً زارَ أخاً لهُ في قريةٍ أخرى، فأرصدَ اللهُ على مَدْرجتِه مَلَكاً، فلما أتى عَليه قال: أينَ تُريد؟ قال: أُريدُ أخاً لي في هذهِ القريَة، قال: هل لكَ عليهِ من نِعمة ترُبُّها؟ قال: لا، غيرَ أنِّي أحببتهُ في اللهِ عزَّ وجلّ، قال: فإِني رسولُ اللهِ إليكَ بأن اللهَ قد أحبَّك كما أحببتهُ فيه).⁽⁸⁾

ومعنى: أرصدَ اللهُ على مدرجتهِ ملكاً: أي وكَّله بحفظِ المدْرجَة، وهيَ الطَّريق وجعلهِ رصَداً: أي حافِظاً مُعَداً. ومعنى تَرُبُّها: أي تحفظُها وتُربيها كما يربي الرجلُ ولدَه. وخطابُ الملائكةِ في القرآنِ الكَريم للمؤمنِ أو الإنسانِ عامَّة كَثيرة ومنها: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا تَتَنَزَّلُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَلَّا تَخَافُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَبْشِرُوا بِالْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ ۝ نَحْنُ أَوْلِيَاؤُكُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ﴾.⁽⁹⁾

وكونُ الملائكةِ أولياءً للأرواحِ الطيبةِ الطاهرة، حاصلٌ من جهاتٍ كثيرةٍ معلومةٍ لأرباب المكاشفاتِ والمشاهَدات، فهُم يَقولون: كما أنَّ تلكَ الولايةُ كانَت حاصلةً في الدُّنيا، فهي تكونُ باقيةً في الآخِرة، فإن تلكَ العلائقُ ذاتيةٌ لازِمة غير قابلةٍ للزَّوال، بَل كأنَّها تصيرُ بعدَ الموتِ أقوى وأَبقى، وذلك لأنَّ جوهرَ النفسِ من جنسِ الملائِكة، وهي كالشعلةِ بالنسبةِ إلى الشَّمس، والقطرةِ بالنسبةِ إلى البَحر، والتعلُّقات الجسمانيةِ هيَ التي تَحول بينَها وبينَ الملائِكة، كما قَال صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (لولا أنَّ الشياطينَ يحومونَ على قلوبِ بني آدمَ لنَظروا إلى ملكوتِ السَّماوات). فإِذا زالَت العلائقُ الجسمانيةُ والتدبيراتُ البدنيَّة، فقد زالَ الغطاءُ والوطاء، فيتصلُ الأثرُ بالمؤثِّر، والقطرةُ بالبحرِ والشعلةُ بالشَّمس، فهذا هُو المرادُ من قولِه: ﴿نَحْنُ أَوْلياؤُكُمْ فِي الحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَفي الآخِرَة﴾.⁽¹⁰⁾

وقد كانَ "عمران بن الحصين" رضوان الله عليه يسمعُ تسبيحَ الملائكةِ حتَّى اكتَوى، فانحبسَ ذلكَ عَنه، ثم أعادهُ اللهُ إليه. وروى "ابن الأثير" رحمه الله تعالى في "أسد الغابة" بسنده إليه أن رسول الله صلوات الله وسلامه عليه نَهى عن الكَيّ، قال "عمران": فاكتَوينا، فما أفلَحنا ولا نَجحنا. قال: وكانَت الملائكةُ في مرضهِ تُسلم عَليه، فاكتَوى ففقدَ التَّسليم ثم عادَت إليه.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 99: What is the meaning of inspiration (ilham), and what is the proof for it?

Answer: Al-Sharif al-Jurjani (may Allah have mercy on him) said in his Ta‘rifat: “Inspiration is that which is cast into the inner self by way of effusion. It has also been said: Inspiration is knowledge that occurs in the heart, which calls to action without inference from a verse or rational proof.”⁽¹⁾

Inspiration is either from Allah Most Exalted or from the angels. Through it, one understands commands, prohibitions, encouragement, or warnings. As for inspiration directly from Allah Most Exalted, He narrated to us in His Book concerning Maryam (peace be upon her) when she sought shelter under a date palm in the winter days, and He addressed her with inspiration and revelation without intermediary: “And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe fresh dates. So eat and drink and be comforted…” (Quran 19:25). Imam Fakhr al-Din al-Razi (may Allah have mercy on him) said in his commentary on this verse: “This was by way of effusion into the inner self, inspiration, and casting into the heart, just as it was with the mother of Musa (peace be upon him) in His saying: And We inspired the mother of Musa.” (Quran 28:7)⁽²⁾

Similarly, Allah informed us about the mother of Musa (peace be upon him) when she was in distress over her son’s fate and was surrounded by Pharaoh’s soldiers seeking to kill him. Allah inspired her and revealed to her without intermediary: “And We inspired the mother of Musa, ‘Suckle him. But when you fear for him, cast him into the river and fear not nor grieve. Indeed, We will return him to you and make him one of the messengers.’” (Quran 28:7) She cast her son—the delight of her heart—into the raging waves of the river. Where would this noble child go amid the roaring waves? It appeared to be certain destruction, yet she was fully certain because she was accustomed to receiving revelation from her Lord without intermediary, both in solitude and in public.

As for inspiration from the angels, the angel speaks to the human being, as the Prophet ﷺ said: “…As for the angelic prompting, it is a promise of good and confirmation of truth; whoever finds that should know it is from Allah and praise Him.”⁽³⁾

Imam Fakhr al-Din al-Razi (may Allah have mercy on him) said regarding the verse And [mention] when the angels said, “O Maryam, indeed Allah has chosen you and purified you and chosen you above the women of the worlds” (Quran 3:42): “Know that Maryam was not among the prophets, because Allah said: And We sent not before you [any messengers] except men to whom We revealed from among the people of cities (Quran 12:109). If so, then the sending of Jibril to her was an honor for her, and he spoke to her directly. This is not exclusive to her; many righteous people have been addressed by the angels (peace be upon them).”⁽⁴⁾

Abu Hurayra related that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “A man visited his brother in another village, and Allah stationed an angel on his path. When he reached him, the angel asked, ‘Where are you going?’ He said, ‘I want to visit my brother in this village.’ The angel asked, ‘Have you any favor upon him that you seek to maintain?’ He said, ‘No, I only love him for Allah’s sake.’ The angel said, ‘I am Allah’s messenger to you, to tell you that Allah loves you just as you love him for His sake.’”⁽⁵⁾

The meaning of “Allah stationed an angel on his path” is that He appointed an angel to guard the way and watch over him, as a father watches over his child. The phrase “maintain” means to preserve and nurture it as one nurtures his child. The angels’ addressing of believers or people in general appears frequently in the Qur’an, including: Indeed, those who have said, “Our Lord is Allah” and then remained on a right course—the angels will descend upon them, [saying], “Do not fear and do not grieve but receive good tidings of Paradise, which you were promised. We [angels] were your allies in the worldly life and [are so] in the Hereafter…” (Quran 41:30–31)⁽⁶⁾

The fact that the angels are allies to pure and good souls is established through many known aspects for those who possess unveilings and direct witnessing. They say: Just as this alliance existed in the worldly life, it remains in the Hereafter. These connections are intrinsic and permanent, not subject to removal—rather, after death they become stronger and more enduring. This is because the essence of the soul is of the same genus as the angels. It is like a spark relative to the sun, or a drop relative to the sea. Only bodily attachments form a barrier between the soul and the angels, as the Prophet ﷺ said: “Were it not for the devils encircling the hearts of the children of Adam, they would gaze upon the kingdom of the heavens.” When bodily attachments and bodily management are removed, the veil is lifted, and the effect connects to the Causer, the drop to the sea, the spark to the sun. This is the intended meaning of His saying: We [angels] were your allies in the worldly life and [are so] in the Hereafter.⁽⁷⁾

‘Imran ibn al-Husayn  used to hear the glorification of the angels until he underwent cauterization (as medical treatment), after which it stopped—then Allah restored it to him. Ibn al-Athir (may Allah have mercy on him) recorded in Usud al-Ghaba with his chain to him (‘Imran) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade cauterization. ‘Imran said: “We underwent cauterization and thereafter neither succeeded nor were saved.” He added: “During his illness the angels used to greet him; after cauterization he lost the greetings, then they returned.”

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 100) هل يعتبر الإلهام من العلم اللَّدني؟
الجواب: سَمّى الصوفيَّة العِلم الناتِج من الإلهام عِلماً لدنياً حاصِلاً بمحضِ فضلِ اللهِ وكرمهِ بغيرِ واسطة عبارَة. وقد قالَ بعضُهم:
تعلّمْنَا بلا حرفٍ وصوْتِ  قرأْناه بلا سهْوٍ وفوْتِ

يَعني بطريقِ الفيضِ الإلهي، والإلهامِ الرَّباني، لا بطريقِ التعليمِ الَّلفظي، والتدريسِ القَولي. وقد سُئل الإمام "الغزالي" عَن الإلهام فقال: (الإلهامُ ضوءٌ من سِراج الغَيب، يسقطُ على قلبٍ صافٍ لطيفٍ فارِغ). كلُّ هذا يدلُّ على إمكانِ الكشفِ وصحةِ الإلهام، إذا كانَ القلبُ صافياً فارغاً من علائقِ الدُّنيا وهمومِها، ومِنْ صدأِ الذنوبِ وظُلماتها. فالشياطينُ الظلمانِية لا تقعُ إلا على القلوبِ العَفِنة، كما يقعُ الذُّباب على الأواني الوسِخة، فتُحجبُ القلوبُ عن مطالعةِ ما حُجب عَنها، يقولُ النبي صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (لولا أنَّ الشياطينَ يحومونَ على قلوبِ بني آدم لنَظروا إلى ملكوتِ السَّماوات)⁽¹⁾ وتُصرَفُ وسوستُها عن تلكَ القلوبِ بذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى ومراقبتِه: (إنَّ الشيطانَ واضعٌ خطمه على قلبِ ابنِ آدم، فإنْ ذَكَرَ الله خنسَ وإن نسِي الْتقم قلبُه).⁽²⁾ لأن القلبَ إذا اعتادَ الوَسوَسة، والغفلَة عن ذكرِ اللهِ تَعالى مَرِض. وأمَّا إذا اعتادَ الذِّكر، وسُقيَ بأنوارِه، وسطُعت عليهِ شمسُ تجلياتِ اللهِ تَعالى حيِيَ وكانَ في عِداد الأحياء، يقولُ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه: (مثَلُ الذي يذكرُ ربَّه، والذي لا يذكرُ ربَّه مثلُ الحيِّ والميِّت)..

فإذا واظبَ المؤمنُ على ذِكر اللهِ تَعالى، وكانَ مستقيماً على شرعهِ متحلياً بالتَّقوى، مستأنساً بربهِ صارَ حياً بالله. ويقولُ القَوم: (القلوبُ نَوعان: قلبٌ لا يولَد ولم يئن لهُ أن يولَد، بَل يظلُّ جنيناً في بطنِ الشَّهوات والغيِّ والضَّلال، وقلبٌ وُلد، وخرجَ إلى فضاءِ التَّوحيد، وحلَّق في سماءِ المعرِفة، وخلُص من ظلماتِ النفسِ وشهواتِها واتِّباع هَواها، فقرَّت عينُه باللهِ تَعالى وأنارَت جوانبَهُ أشعةُ اليَقين، وجعلتهُ مرآةً شفافَة، لا سبيلَ للشيطانِ إِليه، ولا سلطانَ لهُ عَليه. وليسَ هذا بِبعيد، فالطاقةُ الروحيةُ قد انطلقَت إلى عالمِ الغَيب، وصار صاحبُها حَيَّاً بعدَ أن كانَ ميتاً، ومنوراً بعد أن كانَ مظلماً، وملكياً بعدَ أن كانَ شيطانياً، (أَوَ من كانَ مَيْتاً فأحيَيناهُ وجعلنا لهُ نوراً يمشي بهِ في الناس)⁽³⁾. وكما قيل:
فللكثافةِ أقوامٌ لها خُلقوا   وللمحبةِ أكبادٌ وأجفان
وأدنى النَّصيب من هذا العِلم التصديقُ به والتَّسليم لأهلِه، وأقلّ عقوبَة من ينكرهُ أن لا يُرزَق منه شيئاً، وهوَ عِلم الصدِّيقين والمقرَّبين.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 100: Is inspiration considered “Divinely vouchsafed knowledge” (‘ilm ladunni)?

Answer: The Sufis refer to the knowledge that arises through inspiration as ‘ilm ladunni (Divinely vouchsafed knowledge), for it is attained purely by Allah’s favor and generosity, without any verbal intermediary. Some have said:

We learned without letter or sound,
And read it without lapse or omission.
This means it was acquired by way of Divine effusion and Lordly inspiration, not through verbal instruction or spoken teaching. When Imam al-Ghazali was asked about inspiration, he replied:

Inspiration is a light from the lamp of the unseen that falls upon a pure, subtle, and empty heart. All of this indicates the possibility of unveiling and the validity of inspiration when the heart is clear and free from worldly attachments and concerns, and from the rust of sins and their darkness. Satanic forces only pounce upon corrupt hearts, just as flies settle on filthy vessels, thereby veiling the hearts from beholding what is concealed from them. The Prophet ﷺ said, “Were it not that the devils encircle the hearts of the children of Adam, they would gaze upon the kingdom of the heavens.”⁽¹⁾ Their whisperings are repelled from those hearts by the remembrance of Allah and vigilance toward Him: “Satan places his beak upon the heart of the son of Adam; when he remembers Allah, he withdraws, and when he forgets, he devours it.”⁽²⁾ When the heart becomes accustomed to whisperings and heedlessness of Allah’s remembrance, it falls ill. But when it becomes accustomed to remembrance and is nourished by its lights, and the sun of Allah’s theophanies shines upon it, it comes alive and is counted among the living. The Prophet ﷺ said, “The likeness of the one who remembers his Lord and the one who does not remember his Lord is like that of the living and the dead.”⁽³⁾

When the believer perseveres in Allah’s remembrance, remains steadfast upon His Sacred Law, adorns himself with piety, and finds intimacy with his Lord, he becomes alive through Allah. The Folk (the Sufis) say: “Hearts are of two types. One is a heart that cannot be born, that remains a fetus in the womb of vain passions, deviation, and misguidance. The other is a heart that is born; it emerges into the vast expanse of Divine unity, soars in the sky of gnosis, and is freed from the darkness of the lower-self, its desires, and following its caprice. His eye finds coolness in Allah; the rays of certitude illuminate his sides and make him a transparent mirror. Satan has no path to him and no authority over him. This is not far-fetched, for such a person’s spiritual energies have ascended to the unseen realm, and its possessor becomes alive after being dead, illuminated after being darkened, angelic after being satanic: Or is one who was dead and We gave him life and made for him a light by which to walk among the people… (Quran 6:122). As it was said:

The dense have people created for them,
And love has livers and eyelids.

The least share of this knowledge is to believe in it and submit to its masters. The least punishment for one who denies it is that he is granted nothing of it—it is the knowledge of the truthful ones and those brought near.
