Question 109: Did the early pious forebears (al-Salaf al-Salih) gather between the Spiritual Reality and the Sacred Law?

Answer: The scholars of the Sacred Law among the jurists and hadith masters used to follow in the footsteps of the greatest Messenger , gathering between the Sacred Law, the Spiritual Path, and the Spiritual Reality. They would observe their rites of worship having actualized the secret of sincerity within them; tasting their sweetness and comprehending their inner secrets. They had many spiritual struggles in training their souls and purifying their hearts. Due to the piety, righteousness, and gnosis they attained, they reached their high degrees in knowledge and Allah bestowed upon them understanding of His book, and depth regarding His Sacred Law. Allah caused the Umma to benefit from their knowledge through the epochs, so when considering their abiding effects and their blessed scholarly endeavors, it is as if they are alive.

The Hanafi jurist and author of al-Durr, al-Haskafi quoted that Abu ‘Ali al-Daqqaq (may Allah have mercy on him) said, “I have taken this spiritual path from Abu al-Qasim al-Nasrabadhi, who said, ‘I took it from Shibli, who in turn took it from al-Sari al-Saqati, who in turn took it from Ma‘ruf al-Karkhi, who in turn took it from Dawud al-Ta’i, who took knowledge and the spiritual path from Abu Hanifa  All of them lauded him and testified to his virtue.”’ The author of al-Durr [al-Haskafi] commented:

How astounding is your condition, dear brother! Is there not a good example for you in these esteemed notables? Are they under suspicion for this testimony and boasting, even though they are the Imams of this spiritual path and the masters of the Sacred Law and Spiritual Reality? Whoever comes after them in this matter is nothing more than one of their followers, and all who oppose what they have relied upon are rejected innovators.⁽¹⁾

Perhaps you are astonished to hear that the great Imam, Abu Hanifa al-Nu‘man (may Allah have mercy on him) would initiate the likes of these esteemed and notable saints from the righteous Sufis into the spiritual path! So why should the jurists not follow in the footsteps of this Imam, adhering to his way, gathering between the Sacred Law and the Spiritual Reality, so that perhaps Allah will cause others to benefit from their knowledge just as they benefited from their greatest Imam, the notable master, the treasure-store of piety and scrupulousness, Abu Hanifa (may Allah have mercy on him)? Speaking about Imam Abu Hanifa and commenting on the words of al-Haskafi, Ibn ‘Abidin said:

He is the chivalrous knight in this field, for the foundation of the science of the Spiritual Reality is knowledge, action, and self-purification, and he [Abu Hanifa] was described with these qualities by the bulk of the salaf. Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal said about him, “He was at such a level of knowledge, scrupulousness, renunciation, and preference for the Hereafter that no one else could attain. He was struck with the whip in pressure to take the position of judgeship, but he did not do it.” ‘Abdullah b. al-Mubarak said, “There is no one who has greater right to be followed than Abu Hanifa, for he was an Imam, a God-fearing, pure, scrupulous, and knowledgeable jurist. Such knowledge was unveiled to him that was unveiled to no other by way of vision, understanding, insight, or piety.” [Sufyan] al-Thawri said to someone who informed him that he had just come from seeing Abu Hanifa, “You have just come from seeing the most devout worshiper on the face of the earth!”⁽²⁾
From this we see that the Mujtahid Imams and practicing scholars are the true Sufis.

Someone might retort, “If the path of Sufism was legislated, then the Mujtahid Imams would have authored works concerning it, yet we see no books authored by them on this topic.” To this, Imam al-Sha‘rani (may Allah have mercy on him) responded:

The only reason the Mujtahid Imams did not author books on it was because of the dearth of inner maladies in their time and their relative safety from ostentation and hypocrisy. Even if it is assumed that the people of their times were not safe from those maladies, they still only manifested in small numbers, and it would have been unlikely for one to see any defects among them. During that time, the major concern of the Mujtahids was in gathering evidence that was spread out in the lands and battle trenches in the possession of the followers (Tabi‘un) and those after them. Such evidence was the source for every science and by it the scales of every legal ruling were known. That was more important for them than occupying themselves with going into detail regarding the inner deeds of some people—deeds which do not manifest an outward rite of the religion, and might not even fall under the ruling of a particular legal category.

No one possessed of intelligence would say that the likes of Imam Abu Hanifa, Malik, al-Shafi‘i, or Ahmad (Allah be pleased with all of them) could know of ostentation, conceit, pride, envy, or hypocrisy in themselves while failing to take steps to struggle against them or take themselves to account. Were it not that they knew themselves to be free of those faults and maladies, they would have given precedence to curing them over every knowledge.⁽³⁾

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 110) هل دس أعداء الإسلام على العلوم الإسلامية شيئاً من أكاذيبهم؟

الجواب: نعم، وذلكَ في علومِ التفسيرِ والحديثِ والتاريخِ والتصوُّف. أمَّا التفسير: فكثيراً ما نقرأُ في كتبهِ بعضُ الإسرائيلياتِ التي ليسَت إلا أساطيرَ كاذِبة، وعقائدَ غيرِ إسلاميَّة، نقلَها إلى الدِّينِ الإسلاميّ اليَهود الذين اعتنَقوا الإسلامَ غيرَ مُخلصين، أو مُخلصين ولكِن علِقت بأذهانهِم هذهِ الأساطيرَ حينَ كانوا على دينِ اليهوديَّة، فنقَلوها عَن كتُب أنبيائهِم التي دخلَها التَّحريف والتَّغيير، وتقبَّلها بعضُ المسلمينَ على أنَّها صَحيحة.

وذلكَ كالإخبارِ بأنَّ سيّدنا "أيوب" صلوات الله وسلامه عليه مَرضَ حتَّى ظَهرَ الدُّودُ على جَسدِه، وكَنسبةِ المَعاصي إلى بعضِ الأنبِياء، فقَد زَعموا أن "داود" عليه السلام عَشِقَ امرأةَ بعضِ جنودِه، ثم أَرسلَ زوجَها لبعضِ المواقعِ الحربيةِ لقتلِه، فقُتل وتزوَّجها. كَما زَعموا أن "يوسف" عليه السلام همَّ بامرأةِ العزيزِ هَمَّ فُحْشٍ وَسوء، ولفَّقوا في ذلكَ قصصاً وحِكاياتٍ لا تليقُ بمقامِ الرُّسل الكِرام، الذينَ عصمهُم اللهُ من كلِّ سوءٍ وفاحِشة.

وأما الحديث: فلقد حاولَ الدسَّاسون المُغرضون تشويهَ معالمِ الإسلامِ عَن طريقِ وضعِ أحاديثَ مكذوبةٍ مُفتراةٍ على لِسان المُصطفى صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، يقصدونَ بذلكَ تحطيمُ العَقيدة، ودَسِّ الأفكارِ الهدَّامة، كالتجسيمِ والتشبيهِ والفوقيةِ والجهةِ وغيرِ ذلكَ من العقائدِ الفاسدَة.

كَما وضعوا أحاديثَ في الترغيبِ والترهيبِ ما أنزلَ اللهُ بها من سُلطان. وإذا قيلَ لهُم: لم تكذبونَ على رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، وهوَ يقول: (من كذبَ عليَّ متعمداً فليتبوَّأ مقعدهُ من النَّار).⁽¹⁾ قالوا: نحنُ نكذبُ لهُ لا عَليه، كَما كانَ بعضهُم يضعُ الحديثَ تقرباً إلى الحُكَّام وتزلّقاً إلى المُلوك، رغبةً في مطمعٍ دنيويٍّ ومكسبٍ مادِّي. ولكنَ اللهَ تَعالى قيَّض لسنَّة رسولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه علماءَ مخلصينَ غيُّورين، مَحَّصوا تلكَ الأحاديث، وبيَّنوا الصحيحةَ من الضعيفَة، والموضوعةِ من الحسنَة، والمشهورةِ من الغريبَة، كـ "المُزّي" و"الزين العراقي" و"الذهبي" و"ابن حجر" وغيرهم.

وأمَا التاريخ: فقد كانَ ميداناً خصباً للدسِّ والافتِراءِ، حيثُ ألصقَ المضلِّلون في تاريخِ الإسلامِ قصصاً وحوادثَ من نسيجِ خيالهِم، حاوَلوا بذلكَ تشويهِ سيرةِ الخُلفاء وملوكِ الإسلام، كَما نَسبوا إلى هارونَ الرَّشيد أُموراً غريبةً مُنكرة، نجدُها في أكاذيبِ ألفِ لَيلة ولَيلة.. قُصد بِها التَّضليل والتَّشكيك.

ولكنَّ المؤرخينَ المسلمينَ كـ "الذَّهبي" و"الطبري" و"ابن كثير" و"ابن الاثير" وغيرِهم، قَد دَوَّنوا التاريخَ الاسلامِي وهذَّبوه ونَفوا عنهُ الدَّخائل، وأخرَجوه نقياً سليماً، فَعلى طالبِ الحقيقةِ أن يعودَ إلى هذهِ المراجعِ الصحيحةِ كَي يميزَ الخبيثَ منَ الطَّيب والغَثّ من السَّمين.
وأما التصوف: منهُم من أدخلَ في كُتب التصوُّف أفكاراً منحرِفة، وعباراتٍ سيِّئة ما أنزلَ اللهُ بِها مِن سلطانٍ كقولهِم:
وما الكلب والخنزير إلا الهنا وما الرب إلا راهب في كنيسة
﴿كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَاهِهِمْۚ إِنْ يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا﴾.⁽²⁾

ومنهُم من أرادَ أن يُفسد دينَ المُسلمين بأشياءَ أُخر تمسُّ عقائدهم، فنُسب إلى بعضِ رجالِ الصوفيَّة أقوالاً تُخالف عقيدةَ أهلِ السُّنة والجَماعة، كالقولِ بالحلولِ والاتِّحاد، وبأنَّ الخالقَ عينُ المَخلوق، والكونُ عينُ المكون.

ومنهُم من حاولَ تشويهَ تاريخِ رجالِ الصوفيَّة، ونَزعِ ثقةِ النَّاس بهِم، فدُسَّ في كتبهِم حوادثَ وقصصاً مِن نسجِ خيالِه، فيها ارتكابٌ للمُنكرات واقترافٌ للآثامِ والكبائِر، كما نجدُ ذلكَ كثيراً في الطبقاتِ الكُبرى لـ "الإمام الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى، وهوَ مِنها بريءٌ كما سيَأتي.
فإن قالَ قائِل:

إنَّ ما نُسب إلى الصوفيَّة من أقوالٍ مخالفةٍ هيَ حقاً من كلامهِم، بدليلِ وجودِها في كتبهِم المطبوعةِ المنشورَة. نَقول: ليسَ كلُّ ما في كُتب الصوفيَّة لهم، لأنها لَم تسْلم من حَمَلاتِ الدَّس والتَّحريف، وما أحوجَنا إلى تضافُر جهودِ المؤمنينَ المخلصينَ لتنقيةِ هذا التراثِ الإسلامِي الثَّمين، ممَّا لَحِق به من دَسٍّ وتَحريف.

ولَو ثبتَ بطريقٍ صحيحٍ عن بعضِ الصوفيَّة، كلامٌ مخالفٌ لحدودِ الشريعَة فَقول: ليسَت كلمةَ فردٍ واحدٍ حُجَّة على جَماعة، شِعارها ومذهبُها التمسُّك بالكتابِ والسنَّة، حتَّى إنَّهم لَيقولون: إنَّ أولَ شرطِ الصوفيِّ أن يكونَ واقفاً عندَ حدودِ الشريعَة، وألاَّ ينحرفَ عَنها قيدَ شَعرة. فإذا هُو تخَطَّى هذا الشَّرط، ووصفَ نفسهُ بأنَّه صوفِي، فقَد اختلقَ لنفسهِ صِفةً ليسَت فيه، وزَعم ما ليسَ لَه. وإليكَ – أخي المؤمِن – بعضَ أمثلةِ الدَّس المُفتراة على الصوفيَّة والعُلماءِ في كتبهِم:

يقول "ابن الفراء" في طبقاتهِ نقلاً عن "أبي بكر المروزي" و"مسدد" و"حرب" إنَّهم قَد رَووا الكثيرَ من المسائِل، ونسَبوها للإمام "أحمد بن حنبل".. وبعدَ أن يفيضَ في ذِكرِ هذهِ المسائِل يَقول: (رجُلان صالحانِ بُليا بأصحابِ سوء: "جعفر الصادق" و"أحمد بن حنبل"، أمَّا "جعفر الصادق" فَقد نُسبت إليه أقوالٌ كثيرة، دوِّنت في فقهِ الشيعةِ الإماميَّة على أنَّها له، وهوَ مِنها بَريء. وأمَّا الإمام "أحمد" فقَد نَسب إليهِ بعضُ الحنابلةِ آراءً في العقائدِ لَم يقُل بها).⁽³⁾
وسُئل الإمام الفَقيه "ابن حجر الهيثمي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى ونفعَنا به: في عقائدِ الحنابلَة ما لا يَخفى على شريفِ علمكُم، فهَل عقيدةُ الإمامِ "أحمد بن حنبل" رضي الله عنهُ كعقائدهِم؟

فأجابَ بقولِه: (عقيدةُ إمامِ السُّنة "أحمد بن حنبل" رضوان الله عليه، موافقةٌ لعقيدةِ أهلِ السُّنة والجماعةَ، من المبالغةِ التامَّة في تنزيهِ اللهِ تَعالى عَمَّا يقولُ الظالمونَ والجاحدونَ علواً كبيراً، من الجهةِ والجسميةِ وغيرِها مِن سائرِ صفاتِ النَّقص، بَل وعَن كُلِّ وصفٍ ليسَ فيهِ كَمالٌ مُطلق، وَما اشتُهر بينَ جهلةِ المنسوبينَ إلى هذا الإمامِ الأعظمِ المُجتهد: مِن أنَّه قائلٌ بشيءٍ من الجِهة أو نَحوها فكذِبٌ وبُهتانٌ وافتراءٌ عَليه، فلُعن مَن نسبَ إليه، أو رَماهُ بشيءٍ مِن هذهِ المَثالب، التي برَّأهُ اللهُ مِنها.. وقد بيَّن "الحافظ" الحُجَّة القدوةَ والإمام "أبو الفرج بن الجوزي" من أئمةِ مذهبهِ المبرئينَ من هذهِ الوصمةِ القبيحةِ الشنيعَة، أنَّ كُل ما نُسب إليهِ من ذلكَ كَذِبٌ عليهِ وافتراءٌ وبُهتان.. وأن نصوصهُ صريحةٌ في بطلانِ ذلك، وتنزيهُ اللهِ تَعالى عَنه، فاعلَم ذلك، فإنهُ مُهم).

وأمَّا الإمام "علي بن أبي طالب" رضوان الله عليه فقد دُسَّ عَليه كِتاب "نهج البلاغة" أو أكثرُه، فقد ذكرَ "الذهبي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى في ترجمةِ "علي بن الحسين الشريف المرتضى" أنه: (هوَ المتَّهم بوضعِ كتابِ نهجِ البَلاغة، ومَن طالعهُ جزمٌ بأنهُ مكذوبٌ على أميرِ المؤمنين "علي" كرَّم اللهُ وجهَه، ففيهِ السَّبُّ الصّراح والحطُّ على السيِّدين "أبي بكر" و"عمر" رضوانُ اللهِ عليهِما، وفيهِ من التناقضِ والأشياءِ الركيكةِ والعباراتِ التي مَن له معرفةً بنَفس الُقرشيينَ الصَّحابة، وبنَفَس غيرِهم من بعدهِم من المتأخِّرين، جزمَ بأنَّ الكتابَ أكثرهُ أكاذيب).⁽⁴⁾

وممَّن دُسَ عليهِم الإمام "الشعراني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى، وأكثرَ ما دُسَّ عليهِ في "الطبقات الكبرى" ولقد أوضحَ ذلكَ في كتابهِ لطائفُ المِنن والأخلاق فقال: (ومما مَنَّ اللهَ تباركَ وتَعالى به عليَّ، صَبري على الحسدةِ والأعداء، لمَّا دسُّوا في كُتبي كلاماً يخالفُ ظاهرَ الشريعَة، وصاروا يستفتونَ عليَّ زوراً وبهتاناً، ومكاتبتهُم فيَّ لِباب السُّلطان، ونَحو ذلك. اعلَم يا أخي أن أوَّل ابتلاءٍ وقعَ لي في مِصر مِن نحوِ هذا النَّوع، أنني لما حججْتُ سنةَ سبعٍ وأربعينَ وتسعمائَة، زَوَّر عليَّ جماعةٌ مسألةً فيها خرقٌ لإجماعِ الأئمةِ الأربعَة، وهوَ أنَّني أفتيتُ بعضَ الناسِ بتقديمِ الصلاةِ عن وقتِها إذا كانَ وراءَ العبدِ حاجَة، قالوا: وشاعَ ذلك في الحَج، وأرسلَ بعضَ الأعداءِ مُكاتباتٌ بذلك إلى مِصر مِن الجبَل، فلما وصلتُ إلى مِصر، حصلَ فيها هرجٌ عَظيم، حتَّى وصلَ ذلكَ إلى إقليمِ الغربيَّة والشرقيَّة والصعيدِ وأكابرِ الدولةِ بِمصر، فحصلَ لأصحابي غايةُ الضَّرر، فما رجعتُ إلى مِصر إلا وأجدُ غالبَ الناسِ ينظر إليَّ شذراً، فقُلت: ما بالُ النَّاس؟ فأخبَروني بالمُكاتبات التي جاءتهُم من مكَّة، فلا يعلمُ عددَ من اغتابَني، ولاثَ بعَرضي إلا اللهَ عزَّ وجَل.

وقد ذكرَ المؤرِّخ الكبير "عبد الحي بن العماد الحنبلي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى في كتاب "شذرات الذهب في أخبار من ذهب" ترجَمة الشيخ "عبد الوهاب الشعراني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى (وقد حسدهُ طوائفٌ فدسُّوا عليهِ كلماتٍ يخالفُ ظاهرَها الشَّرع، وعقائدَ زائغةٍ ومسائلَ تخالفُ الإجماع، وأقاموا عليهِ القيامةَ وشنَّعوا وسبُّوا، ورموهُ بكلِّ عَظيمة، فخذلهُم اللهُ وأظهرهَ اللهَ عليهِم، وكانَ مواظباً على السُّنة، ومُبالغاً في الوَرع، مُؤثِراً ذَوي الفاقةِ على نفسهِ حَتى بملبوسِه، مُتحملاً للأذى، مُوزعاً أوقاتهُ على العِبادة، ما بينَ تصنيفٍ وتسليكٍ وإفادَة.. وكانَ يُسمَعُ لزاويتهِ دوِيُّ كدَوِيِّ النَّحل ليلاً ونهاراً، وكان "يحيي" ليلةَ الجُمعة بالصلاةِ على المُصطفى صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، ولَم يزَل مُقيماً على ذلك معظَّماً في صدورِ الصُّدور، إلى أن نقلهُ اللهُ تَعالى إلى دارِ كرامتِه).⁽⁵⁾

وقالَ الفقيهُ الكبير "أحمد بن حجر الهيثمي المكي" رحمهُ اللهُ تعالى: (وإياكَ أن تغترَّ بما وقعَ في كِتاب "الغنية" لـ "إمام العارفين" وقُطب الإسلامِ والمُسلمين الشيخ "عبد القادر الجيلاني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى، فإنه دُسَّ عليهِ فيها من سينتقمُ اللهُ مِنه، وإلا فهوَ بريءٌ من ذلك، وكيفَ تروّج عليهِ هذه المسألةُ الواهيةُ مع تضلُّعهِ من الكتابِ والسنةِ وفقهِ الشافعيةِ والحنابلَة، حتَّى كانَ يُفتي على المَذهبين. هذا معَ ما انضمَّ لذلكَ مِن أنَّ الله مَنَّ عليهِ من المعارفِ والخوارقِ الظاهرةِ والباطِنة. وما أنبأَنا عنهُ ما ظهرَ عليهِ وتواترَ من أحوالِه.. إلى أن قال: فكيفَ يتصوَّر أو يتوهمُ أنهُ قائلٌ بتلكِ القبائحِ التي لا يصدُرُ مثلُها إلا عن اليهودِ وأمثالهِم، ممن استُحكم فيهِم الجهلُ بالله وصفاتهِ، وما يجبُ لهُ وما يجوزُ وما يستَحيل.. سبحانَك، هذا بُهتان عَظيم).⁽⁶⁾
وكذلكَ دسُّوا على الإمام "الغزالي" عدَّة مسائلَ في كتاب "الإحياء" وظفرَ القاضي "عياض" بنسخةٍ من تلكَ النُّسخ فأمرَ بإحراقِها.⁽⁷⁾

قال "الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى: (وممَّا دسُّوا على الغزالي، وأشاعهُ بعضهُم عنه قولهُم عنه إنَّه قال: (إن للهِ عباداً لو سألوهُ أن يَقيم الساعةَ الآن لأقامَها). فإِن مِثل ذلكَ كذبٌ وزورٌ على الإمام حُجة الإسلامِ رضوانُ اللهِ عليه، يجبُ على كُلِّ عاقلٍ تنزيهِ الإمامِ عَنه، لأنهُ يردُّ النصوصَ القاطعةَ الواردَة في مقدِّمات الإمامِ فذلكَ مدسوسٌ عليهِ من بعضَ الملاحِدة، وقَد رأيتُ كتاباً كاملاً مشحوناً بالعقائدِ المخالفة لأهلِ السنَّة والجماعة، صنَّفه بعضُ الملحدينَ ونسبهُ إلى الإمام "الغزالي" فاطَّلع عليهِ الشيخ "بدر الدين ابن جماعة" فكتبَ عَليه: (كذبَ واللهِ وافتَرى من أضافَ هذا الكتابَ إلى حُجَّة الإسلام).⁽⁸⁾

وقال أيضاً: (وكذلكَ دَسُّوا عليَّ أنا في كِتابي المُسمى "بالبحرِ المورود" جملةً من العقائدِ الزائِغة، وأشاعوا تلكَ العقائدَ في مِصر ومكَّة نحوَ ثلاثِ سِنين، وأنا بريءٌ مِنها كما بَيَّنْتُ في خُطبةِ الكتابِ لمَّا غيرتُها، وكانَ العلماءُ كتَبوا عليهِ وأجازوه، فَما سكنَت الفتنةُ حَتى أرسلتُ إليهِم النُّسخة التي عَليها خطوطُ العلماءِ).
هذا وقَد مَلأ خصومُه الدُّنيا حولهُ حقداً وحسداً، وافتراءً وكذباً وتضليلاً، لاسيَّما في كتبهِ المعروفَة، وأشهرُها "الطبقات الكبرى".

فلَو قارنَ المُنْصِفُ بينَ كلامِ "الشعراني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى الذي يُعلنُ فيهِ تمسُّك الصوفيّةِ بالشَّريعة، وقَد مَرَّ بكَ في بحثٍ بين الحقيقةِ والشَّريعة وبينَ كلامهِ في "الطبقات الكبرى" لرَأى تبايناً ظاهراً، ولظهرَ لهُ كذبُ ما في الطَّبقات.

وكذلكَ دَسُّوا على الشيخ "محي الدين بن عربي" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى، قال "الشعراني": (كان رضوانُ اللهِ عَليه متقيداً بالكتابِ والسُّنة)، يقول: (كلُّ مَن رَمى ميزانَ الشريعةِ من يدِه لحظةً هَلك..) إلى أَن قال: (وهذا اعتقادُ الجماعةِ إلى قيامِ السَّاعة)، وجميعُ ما لم يفهمهُ الناسُ من كلامهِ إنَّما هو لعُلِّو مَراقيه، وجميعُ ما عارضَ من كلامهِ ظاهِر الشريعةِ وما عليهِ الجُمهور، فهوَ مدسوسٌ عَليه، كما أخبرَني بذلكَ سيدي "أبو طاهر المغربي" نزيلُ مكَّة المشرَّفة، ثم أخرجَ لي نسخةَ الفتوحاتِ التي قابلَها على نسخةِ الشَّيخ التي بخطِّه في مدينة "قونيه" فلَم أرَ فيها شيئاً مما كنتُ توقفتُ فيهِ وحذفتهُ حينَ اختصرتُ الفُتوحات.. ثمَّ قال "الشعراني" رحمهُ اللهُ تَعالى: (إذا عَلمت ذلك، فيُحتمل أن الحسدةَ دسُّوا على الشيخِ في كُتبه، كَما دسُّوا في كُتبي أنا، فإنَّه أمرٌ قد شاهدتهُ عن أهلِ عَصري في حَقي، فاللهُ يغفرُ لنا ولهُم آمين).⁽⁹⁾

ذكرَ الفَقيه "الحنفي" صاحب "الدر المختار" أنَّ: (مَن قالَ عن "فصوصِ الحكم" للشيخ "محي الدين بن عربي" إنه خارجٌ عن الشَّريعة، وقد صنَّفهُ للإضلال، ومَنْ طالعهُ مُلحد، ماذا يلزمُه؟ أجاب: نَعم، فيهِ كلماتٍ تُباين الشَّريعة، وتكلِّف بعض المتصلِّفين لإرجاعِها إلى الشَّرع، لكن الذي تيقنْتُه أنَّ بعضَ اليهودِ افتراها على الشَّيخ قدَّس اللهُ سِرَّه، فيجبُ الاحتياطُ بتركِ مُطالعةِ تلكَ الكَلمات). وقال َالعلاَّمة "ابن عابدين" رحمه الله تعالى في حاشيتهِ على "الدر المختار"، عندَ قولهِ "لكن الذي تيقَّنته": وذلكَ بدليلٍ ثبتَ عِنده، أو لسببِ عدمِ اطلاعهِ على مُراد الشَّيخ فيها، وأنهُ لا يمكن تأويلُها، فتعيَّن عندهُ أنَّها مُفتراة عَليه، كما وقعَ للشيخ "الشعراني" أنَّه افتَرى عليهِ بعضُ الحُسَّاد في بعضِ كتبهِ أشياءِ مُكفرِّة، وأشاعَها عَنه، حتى اجتمعَ بعلماءِ عصرِه، فأخرجَ لهُم مسودَّة كتابهِ التي عَليها خطوطُ العلماءِ فإذا هيَ خاليةٌ عمَّا افْتُرِي عَليه، وقال "ابن عابدين" أيضاً عندَ قولِه: "فيجب الاحتياط": لأنه إن ثبُت افتراؤها فالأمرُ ظاهِر، وإلاَّ فلا يُفهم كلُّ أحدٍ مرادَه فيها، فيُخشى على الناظِرِ فيها من الإنكارِ عَليه، أو فَهمِ خِلاف المُراد).⁽¹⁰⁾
ومنَ المدسوسِ على الشيخ "محي الدين" رحمه الله تعالى أيضاً: القولُ بأنَّ أهلِ النارِ يتلذَّذون بدخولهِم النَّار، وأنَّهم لو خَرجوا منها تعَذبوا بذلكَ الخُروج.
قال "الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى: (وإن وُجد نحوَ ذلكَ في شيءٍ من كُتبه فهوَ مدسوسٌ عَليه، فإِني مررتُ على كتاب "الفتوحات المكية" جميعهُ، فرأيتهُ مشحوناً بالكلامِ على عذابِ أهلِ النَّار).⁽¹¹⁾

وقالَ أيضاً: (كذبَ مَنْ دسَّ في كِتاب "الفصوص والفتوحات" أن الشيخ "محي الدين بن عربي" قالَ بأن أهلَ النارِ يتلذَّذون بالنَّار، وأنَّهم لو أُخرجوا مِنها لاستَغاثوا وطَلبوا الرُّجوع إليها، كما رأيتُ ذلكَ في هذينِ الكِتابين.. وقَد حَذفت ذلكَ من "الفتوحات" حالَ اختصاري لها، حتَّى وردَ عن الشيخ "شمس الدين الشريف" بأنهم دسُّوا على الشيخِ في كتبهِ كثيراً من العقائدِ الزائغةِ التي نُقلت عن غيرِ الشيخ، فإن الشَّيخ من كُمَّل العارفينَ بإِجماعِ أهلِ الطَّريق، وكانَ جليسَ رسولِ اللهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه على الدَّوام، فكيفَ يتكلَّم بما يهدِم شيئاً من أركانِ شريعتِه، ويُساوي بينَ دينهِ وبينَ جميعِ الأديانِ الباطِلة، ويجعلُ أهلَ الدارينِ سَواء، هذا لا يعتقدهُ في الشيخِ إلا من عُزل عنهُ عقلُه، فإياكَ يا أخي أن تُصدق، من يضيفُ شيئاً من العقائدِ الزائغةِ إلى الشَّيخ، واحمِ سمعكَ وبصركَ وقلبَك، وقد نصحتُك والسَّلام.. وقد رأيتُ في عقائدِ الشيخ "محي الدين الواسطي" ما نصُّه: (ونعتقدُ أنَّ أهلَ الجنَّة والنارِ مخلَّدون في دارَيْهما، لا يخرجُ أحدٌ منهُم من دارهِ أبدَ الآبدينَ ودهرَ الداهِرين.. وقال: ومُرادنا بأهلِ النارِ الذينَ هُم أهلُها من الكُفار والمشركينَ والمنافقينَ والمعطِّلين، لا عُصاة الموحِّدين فإنَّهم يخرجونَ من النارِ بالنُّصوص).⁽¹²⁾

ويؤيِّد ما ذكرناهُ بأنَّ هذا القولُ مدسوسٌ على الشيخ "محي الدين" ما ذكرهُ الشيخ نفسهُ في البابِ الحادي والسبعينَ وثلثمائةٍ منَ الفُتوحات، عندما تُغلق أبوابُ النَّار، كيفَ يصيرُ أهلُها كقطعِ اللحمِ حينَما تَغلي بِهم النارُ ويصيرُ أعلاها أسفَلها.

وكذلكَ ما ذكرهُ الإمام "الباجوري الشافعي" في شرحهِ على جوهرةِ التَّوحيد: (وما يُقال بتمرُّن أهلِ النارِ بالعَذاب، حتى لو أُلْقُوا في الجنةِ لتألَّموا، مدسوسٌ على القومِ "الصوفية" كيفَ وقَد قالَ تَعالى: ﴿فَذُوقُوا فَلَن نَّزِيدَكُمْ إِلَّا عَذَابًا ﴾.⁽¹³⁾
فكيفَ يعتقد مسلمُ هذهِ العقيدةَ الفاسدةَ التي تخالفُ عقيدةَ أهلِ السنةِ والجماعَة، وقَد نصَّ على ذلكَ الشيخ "محمد بن يوسف الكافي" بعدَ أن ذكرَ فريقُ الجنَّةِ وأنَّهم مُخلَّدون فيها ومُنعَّمون.

ذكرَ فريقَ أهلِ النارِ فَقال: (وفريقُ السَّعيرِ خالدونَ فيهِ أبداً، لا ينقطعُ عنهُم ألمُ العَذاب، وقالَ بعضُهم: (ينقطعُ عنهُم، وينقلبُ في حقِّهم استلذاذاً، بحيثُ لو عُرضَت عليهِم الجنةُ لأَبوْها، لِما هُم فيه من الاستِلذاذ) ومعتقِدُ هذا كافرٌ بلا شَكٍّ ولا رَيب، لتكذيبهِ اللهَ تَعالى في خبرِه: ﴿إنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُوْلَئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةُ اللَّهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ ﴿161﴾ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا لاَ يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلاَ هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ﴾.⁽¹⁴⁾

وفي خبرهِ أيضاً: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِنَا سَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِمْ نَارًا كُلَّمَا نَضِجَتْ جُلُودُهُم بَدَّلْنَاهُمْ جُلُودًا غَيْرَهَا لِيَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ﴾⁽¹⁵⁾ وغيرَ ذلكَ من الآياتِ الدالةِ على استمرارِ عذابهم.⁽¹⁶⁾

ومِما نُسب إلى الشيخ "محي الدين ابن عربي" رحمهُ الله تعالى أيضاً افتراءً عليهِ، القولُ بسقوطِ التَّكاليف؟ يقولُ العلامة "الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى: (وقد ذكرَ الشيخ "محي الدين" أنه لا يجوزُ لوليٍّ قَط المبادرَة إلى فعلِ معصيةٍ اطَّلع من طريقِ كشفهِ على تقديرِها عليه، كما أنهُ لا يجوزُ لمن كُشفَ له أن يَمرض في اليومِ الفُلاني من رمَضان، أن يبادرَ للفطرِ في ذلكَ اليَوم، بل يجبُ عليهِ الصبرُ حتى يتلبَّس بالمرَض، لأنَّ اللهَ تَعالى ما شرعَ الفطرَ إلا مع التلَبُّس بالمرَض أو غيرهِ من الأعذار. قال: (ومذهبُنا هذا ومذهبُ المحققينَ من أهلِ اللهِ عزَّ وجل).⁽¹⁷⁾

ومما دُسَّ على العارفِ الكبيرِ الشيخ "إبراهيم الدسوقي" رحمه الله تعالى قوله: (أذِن لي ربِّي أن أتكلَّم وأقولَ أنا الله، فقالَ لي: قُل: أنا الله ولا تُبالِ) وفي هذا من الشَّناعة والاجتِراء، ما يُغني عن الإطالَة.

ومما دُسَّ على "رابعة العدوية" رحمها الله تعالى، قولُها عن الكَعبة: (هذا الصَّنم المعبودُ في الأرض) وهذا "ابن تيمية" نفسهُ يُكذِّب نسبةَ هذا القولِ إليها، ويُبين أنَّه مدسوسٌ ومكذوبٌ عَليها، فقد قالَ حينَ سُئل عن ذلك: (وأما ما ذُكرَ عن رابِعة مِن قولِها عَن البَيت: إنَّه الصَّنم المعبودُ في الأرض، فَهو كذبٌ على رابَعة المؤمنَة التقيَّة.. ولَو قالَ هذا مَن قال، لكانَ كافراً يُستتاب، فإِن تابَ وإلاَّ قُتل، وهوَ كَذب.. فإنَّ البيتَ لا يعبدهُ المُسلمون، ولكنهُم يعبدونَ ربَّ البيتِ، بالطَّواف به والصَّلاة إليه).⁽¹⁸⁾

ولَو ذهَبنا - أخي المؤمن - نستَقصي ألوانَ التزييفِ في التاريخِ الإسلامِي والتصوُّف لما وَسِعَتنا هذهِ الرِّسالة، إذ التصوُّف كانَ نصيبهُ من الدسِّ والافتراءِ أعظمَ مِن غيرِه، لأن المزيَّفين أدرَكوا أن التصوُّف هو روحُ الإسلام، وأن الصوفيَّة هُم قوَّته النافِذة الضَّخمة وشعلتُه الوضَّاءة المُشرقة، فأرادوا أن يُطفئوا هذا النُّور.. قال تعالى: ﴿يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطْفِئُوا نُورَ اللَّهِ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَاللَّهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكَافِرُونَ﴾.⁽¹⁹⁾

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>
Question 110: Is it true that some of the enemies of Islam have tampered with the contents of books on the Islamic sciences and inserted their false doctrines and claims in them?
Answer: Yes, tampering has occurred in works on the sciences of Quranic exegesis (tafsir), hadith, history, and Sufism.

As for Quranic exegesis, often in the books of Quranic exegesis we read certain Israelite traditions that are nothing more than false fairytales and un-Islamic beliefs transmitted by Jews who embraced Islam insincerely, or by Jews who were sincere in their Islam, but because of their fondness for these fairytales during their time as Jews, they quoted them from the books of their Prophets that had undergone tampering and alteration, and afterwards some of the Muslims accepted them at face value.

Examples of such tampering include reports stating that our master Ayyub  was so sick that worms had infested his body, and reports attributing disobedience to some of the Prophets, such as their claim that Dawud  fell in love with the wife of one of his soldiers and ordered him to go out to battle so he would be slain, and that after he went out and was killed in battle Dawud married her, or that Yusuf  desired to engage in lewd acts with the wife of ‘Aziz. In all of these reports they pieced together stories and tales that are not befitting of the rank of the noble Messengers whom Allah has infallible and divinely protected from every evil and lewd act.

As for hadith: The biased partisans and interpolators have attempted to deface the signposts of Islam by forging hadith and ascribing them to the Messenger  in order obliterate correct belief and add destructive beliefs such as anthropomorphism, likening Allah to the creation, ascribing to Him directionality and limits, and other corrupt beliefs.

Similarly, they have forged hadith used for encouraging good works and discouraging from bad works, having no authority from Allah for that. When it was said to them, “Why are you lying against the Messenger of Allah  when he said, ‘Whosoever intentionally lies against me, then let him take his seat in the fire’?” They responded, “We are lying for him, not against him.” Similarly, some people forged hadith in order to draw near to the rulers or to cozy up with kings, seeking thereby worldly ambitions and material gains.

Despite all this, Allah has safeguarded the Sunna of His Messenger  with sincere and protectively jealous scholars who sifted through these hadith reports and clarified the weak from the authentic, and the fabricated from the acceptable, and the well-known from the strange; scholars such as al-Mizzi, al-Zayn al-‘Iraqi, al-Dhahabi, Ibn Hajar, and others.⁽¹⁾

As for history: As a field it was ripe for tampering and fabrications, hence many misguided people introduced stories from their own imaginations into the history of Islam, attempting to malign the character of the Caliphs and kings of Islam, such as the strange and rejected things attributed to Harun al-Rashid found in One Thousand and One Nights—things added with the sole intent of spreading doubt and error.

However, the verifying Muslim historians, such as al-Dhahabi, al-Tabari, Ibn Kathir, Ibn Athir, Ibn Hisham, and others codified Islamic history, corrected it, and negated foreign elements added to it, releasing it clean and sound. Hence, one who seeks reality must refer to these sound reference works in order to distinguish the good from the bad and the useful from the harmful.
As for Sufism: Some have added deviant ideas to the books of the Sufis or evil expressions for which Allah has given no authority, such as their words:

The dog and the pig are naught but our object of worship
And the Lord is naught but a monk in the church!
Grave is the word that comes out of their mouth, they speak not but a grievous lie! (Quran 18:5)

Some of them wanted to corrupt the faith of the Muslims through other means that would negatively affect their beliefs, so they ascribed to some of the Sufis statements in opposition to the doctrine of Ahl al-Sunna wa al-Jama‘a, such as the belief in union and indwelling (with the Divine), that creation and the Creator are one and the same, and that the created universe and the Maker are one.

Some of them attempted to malign the history of the Sufis and remove the people’s confidence in them, so out of their own imagination they fabricated events and stories into the books of the Sufis that mention the Sufis falling into evil acts and engaging in lewd acts and committing major sins. We see many examples of this in the Tabaqat of Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) and he—as we shall demonstrate—is innocent of such things.

If one were to retort, “Those things attributed to the Sufis are really from their works, because they are found in their published works today,” we would respond: Not everything that is found in the books of the Sufis is actually from them, for these works were not safe from tampering and fabrications of others. We are in great need of sincere believers to expend their utmost efforts in cleansing our valuable Islamic heritage from the tampering and fabrications that have crept into it. But even if the words of some Sufis that exceeded the limits of the Sacred Law were affirmed by way of an authentic route of transmission, we say that one individual cannot be taken as a proof against an entire group whose motto and way is firm adherence to the Book and the Sunna. The Sufis themselves say that the first condition of a Sufi is that he be in conformity with the limits set by the Sacred Law and that he should not deviate from those limits one iota. If such a person neglected this condition yet took on the name “Sufi” for himself, he has taken on a characteristic that is not found within him and he has claimed what is not for him to claim.

Consider, dear believer, some examples of tampering found in the books of the Sufis and other scholars. In his Tabaqat, Ibn al-Farra’ quoted many issues in law attributed to Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal from Abu Bakr al-Marrudhi, Musaddad, and Harb. After mentioning these issues, he said, “There are two righteous men who have been tried with evil companions: Ja‘far al-Sadiq and Ahmad b. Hanbal. As for Ja‘far al-Sadiq, many statements were attributed to him that found their way into the jurisprudence of the Twelver Shiites and were ascribed to him even though he is innocent of them. As for Ahmad b. Hanbal, some of the Hanbalis attributed to him views in theology that he never said.”⁽²⁾

The jurist and Imam, Ibn Hajar al-Haytami (Allah have mercy upon him and allow us to benefit from him) was asked, “Contained within the creed of the Hanbalis are certain views that are not hidden from the likes of your noble self—so was the creed of Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal  like their creed?” He answered:

Concerning the doctrine of the Imam of Ahl al-Sunna, Ahmad b. Hanbal—Allah be well pleased with him and make him well pleased, and may He grant him the loftiest of gardens as his resting-place and destination, and may He bestow upon us and him His bounties, and grant him a dwelling in the loftiest reaches of Paradise—his doctrine was in absolute conformity with the belief of Ahl al-Sunna with the utmost confirmation of Allah’s transcendence, being far exalted above those matters that the oppressors and deniers attribute to Him, such as (He, the Exalted) possessing directionality and other qualities of imperfection, and every attribute that does not imply absolute perfection.

Those things that are well-known among the ignoramuses who ascribe themselves to this great Imam and Mujtahid and that are attributed to him, such as the claim that he believed that Allah was contained in a direction, or any such thing, is a complete lie, slander and fabrication against him. Cursed be those who ascribed such things to him or who accused him of any of these faults from which Allah has declared his innocence! The hadith master, shining exemplar, and proof, Imam Abu al-Faraj Ibn al-Jawzi—from the Imams of his school who were free of this ugly and abominable trait!—explained that all of these things attributed to him are lies against him, inventions, and fabrications, and that his actual words explicitly invalidate these claims and declare Allah’s transcendence beyond them. Take note of this, for it is important.⁽³⁾

As for Imam ‘Ali b. Abi Talib , all or most of the book Nahj al-Balagha was forged in his name. In Imam al-Dhahabi’s biographical notice on ‘Ali b. al-Husayn al-Sharif al-Murtada, he said, “He is the one accused of forging the book Nahj al-Balagha. Whoever peruses the work can authoritatively state that it is a lie against the Leader of the Believers ‘Ali , for in it are explicit insults and detractions from the two masters Abu Bakr and ‘Umar—Allah be pleased with the both of them. Because of the numerous contradictions and flimsy things within it, in addition to the expressions it contains, anyone who has knowledge of the literary style of the Qurayshi Companions and those after them can authoritatively state that most of the book is false.”⁽⁴⁾

Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) was another author whose works suffered from tampering. Most of the fabrications against him are found in his Tabaqat al-Kubra. In his book, Lata’if al-Minan wal-Akhlaq, al-Sha‘rani explained:

Among the many favors that Allah has bestowed upon me is the patience I have observed with my envious foes who forged words in my books that go against the outward Sacred Law. After this, they began to seek legal verdicts against me based upon these lies and slanders, and they also began to write letters to the government against me, and other such things.

Dear brother, you should know that the first tribulation I faced in Egypt was from this. When I performed the pilgrimage in the year 947, a group of people forged in my name a piece of writing that violated the consensus of the four Imams. This writing stated that I delivered a legal verdict to some people allowing them to offer their prayers before the proper time if there was a need to do so. This was disseminated during the Hajj, and some enemies in al-Jabal wrote to people in Egypt about this. When I returned to Egypt, there was great shock, as news of this reached the northern and western regions, including Upper Egypt (Saeed) and some of the prominent government officials. My companions suffered a great deal of harm. When I finally returned to Egypt I found that people scattered and ran away from me. I said to myself, “What is wrong with these people?” They informed me of the letters they received from Mecca. No one but Allah knows how many people slandered me behind my back or detracted from my honor….

In his book Shadharat al-Dhahab, the great historian ‘Abd al-Hayy b. al-‘Imad al-Hanbali (Allah have mercy upon him) penned a biographical notice for Shaykh ‘Abd al-Wahhab al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him). He said:

Different factions were envious of him, and so they tampered with some of his books by adding in them words whose outward meaning opposed the Sacred Law, as well as heretical points in doctrine and issues that opposed scholarly consensus. They raised a ruckus against him, insulted him, cursed him, and accused him of every enormity and iniquity. Nevertheless, Allah left them to their devices and made him victorious over them. He stuck assiduously to the Sunna and was very exacting in his scrupulousness. He would show preference to the people of straightened means over his own self, even in his clothing. He used to patiently endure the annoyances and harms of others. He would divide his time between performing acts of worship, writing books, assisting others in the spiritual path, and providing benefit…[A] sound similar to the sound of bees humming would be heard emitting from his Zawiya night and day. He used to spend each Thursday night awake sending prayers upon al-Mustafa  and he remained firm upon that. He was esteemed and held in high regard in the hearts of the elect until Allah Most High took him to His Abode of Honor.⁽⁵⁾
The esteemed jurist Ahmad b. Hajar al-Haytami al-Makki (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

Beware lest you are deceived by the contents of the Ghunya of the Imam of the Gnostics and the Axial-Saint (Qutb) of Islam the Muslims, Shaykh ‘Abd al-Qadir al-Jilani. It has been tampered, with fabrications added to it in his name by those upon whom Allah shall take vengeance. He is innocent of that. How can this feeble and untenable issue be spread on his behalf, considering his erudition in the Book and the Sunna, and the jurisprudence of the Shafi‘i and Hanbali schools—so much so that he used to deliver legal verdicts based on the both of them? Not to mention the other things that Allah bestowed upon him, such as the mystic sciences and miracles, both outward and inward, and the reports concerning him that have reached the level of mass transmission….

How can it be imagined or conceived that he would utter such contemptible things, the likes of which could only come from Jews or their ilk, who are firmly steeped in ignorance regarding Allah and His Attributes, and what is necessary, possible, and impossible for Him—Glorified are You, this is but a manifest slander!⁽⁶⁾

Similarly, Imam al-Ghazali’s work Ihya’ ‘Ulum al-Din was subject to tampering and several things were surreptitiously added to it. When Qadi ‘Iyad managed to obtain a copy of the Ihya’ with said tampering, he ordered it to be burnt. Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

Among the things they forged against al-Ghazali and which was subsequently spread by some was the claim that he said, “Indeed to Allah belongs servants who, if they asked Him to establish the Last Hour right now, He would do so.” These are lies and falsehoods uttered against the Imam and proof of Islam (Allah be well pleased with him and he be pleased with Him). It is obligatory upon every intelligent person to declare the Imam innocent of such things, for they oppose the clear-cut texts regarding the portents leading up to the Last Hour and are tantamount to denial of the Law giver  in what he has informed. If such words are found in the works of Imam al-Ghazali then they are forgeries placed by some heretical disbelievers. I have seen a book crammed with corrupt beliefs that oppose the doctrine of Ahl al-Sunna wal-Jama‘a, and it was written by some disbelieving heretics and ascribed to Imam al-Ghazali. Shaykh Badr al-Din b. Jama‘a perused the work and wrote on its cover, “By Allah, the one who ascribes this book to the Proof of Islam has lied and invented a calumny!” …

Similarly, in my book al-Bahr al-Mawrud they forged many false beliefs against me and spread those beliefs about me all through Egypt and Mecca for around three years. I am innocent of those things, as I explained in the opening statement of the book after I edited them. The scholars had written their commendations for it and given permission for it, yet the controversy did not quieted until I sent them the original copy on which they had their handwritten signatures.⁽⁷⁾

His opponents had filled the region with malice, envy, slanders, lies, and accusations of misguidance, especially with regards to his well-known books, the most famous of them being the Tabaqat al-Kubra. But if a fair minded person were to compare the words of al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) in which he openly speaks of how the Sufis strictly adhere to the Sacred Law and his alleged words in the Tabaqat al-Kubra, he would see an obvious disparity between the two and the lies contained in the Tabaqat would become clear to him.
In a similar vein, people forged things against Shaykh Muhyi al-Din Ibn ‘Arabi (Allah have mercy upon him). Imam al-Sha‘rani said:

He  used to restrict himself to the Book and the Sunna and would say, “Everyone who discards the scale of the Sacred Law for even one moment will be destroyed.”…This is the doctrine of the group (the Sufis) until the establishment of the Last Hour. The reason people have misunderstood his words is because of their highly advanced nature. All of his words that contravene the outward Sacred Law and the way of the majority are fabricated against him. I was informed of this by Sayyidi Abu Tahir al-Maghribi, who resides in Mecca the Venerated. He took out a copy of the Futuhat that he compared with a handwritten copy of the Shaykh (Ibn ‘Arabi) in Konya. I did not see anything in it of the [objectionable] matters I found and refrained from mentioning when I summarized the Futuhat…If you are aware of this fact, then it is probable that envious people fabricated things in his books just as they fabricated things in my books, for that is witnessed by the people of my time concerning me—may Allah forgive us and them, Amin.⁽⁸⁾
The Hanafi jurist and author of al-Durr al-Mukhtar said:

Regarding the one who says that the Fusus al-Hikam of Shaykh Muhyi al-Din Ibn ‘Arabi is outside of the fold of the Sacred Law, and that he authored it with the intention of leading others astray, and that whoever reads it is a heretic—what do such statements imply for such a person?

He replied: Yes, there are words within it that are inconsistent with the Sacred Law, and some self-important people have taken the trouble to make such words fit with the Sacred Law; however, what I am certain of is that some Jews have forged those things against the Shaykh—may Allah sanctify his secret. It is obligatory to take due precaution and not read those words.
The erudite Ibn ‘Abidin (Allah have mercy upon him) said in his gloss upon al-Durr al-Mukhtar, commenting on the words of the author, “…however, what I am certain of…”:

That is according to a proof that he considered sound, or because he had not learned of the intent of the Shaykh’s words and thought that it was not possible to interpret them. Based upon these reasons, it seemed certain to him that these things were forged in his name, just as it occurred with Shaykh al-Sha‘rani when some envious folk forged things against him in his books that were tantamount to disbelief and were subsequently spread about him, to the point that he gathered with the scholars of his time and presented them his original copy with the signatures of the scholars on it, and it was found to be free of those things that were added…

“[I]t is obligatory to take due precaution…” because, if its forgery is affirmed, then the matter is clear; that said, however, the import of the Shaykh’s words are not understood by all people, so it is feared for the one who reads that he might rebuke him or misunderstand his intent.⁽⁹⁾

Another forgery made against Shaykh Muhyi al-Din (Allah have mercy upon him) is his alleged statement that the denizens of Hell shall experience delight in the fire, and that if they are taken out they will experience torment. Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) said, “If anything of the sort is found in his books then it is most certainly a forgery against him, for I have read through the entire Futuhat and found it crammed with words speaking of the torment of the denizens of the Hellfire.”⁽¹⁰⁾ He also said:

The one who tampered with Shaykh Muhyi al-Din Ibn ‘Arabi’s Futuhat and Fusus—as I have seen in them—has lied in his claim that he said that the denizens of the Hell-fire take delight in the Fire and that if taken out, they would seek to return. When I summarized the Futuhat I removed these passages. It was related by Shaykh Shams al-Din al-Sharif that they forged many false beliefs in Ibn ‘Arabi’s books that were quoted from other than him. The Shaykh, according to the consensus of the people of the spiritual path, was from the perfected Gnostics. He was the constant companion of the Messenger of Allah , so how could he speak of anything that would demolish anything of his Sacred Law, make his religion equal with all the false religions, and make the people of the two Abodes [Hellfire and Paradise] equal? No one believes this regarding the Shaykh except one who has lost his mind. So beware, dear brother, lest you believe those who attribute such false beliefs to the Shaykh. Safeguard your hearing, sight, and heart. I have given you my sincere counsel, so with peace!

In the creed of Shaykh Muhyi al-Din al-Wasiti I saw the following statement, “And we believe that the inhabitants of Paradise and Hell will abide therein eternally—no one among them will exit from his abode for all time and eternity… [B]y the inhabitants of the Hell-fire, we mean those who are its inhabitants among the disbelievers, idol-worshippers, hypocrites, and atheists—not the disobedient monotheists, for they will exit the Fire, as is affirmed in clear-cut textual proofs.”⁽¹¹⁾

Another fact that buttresses our argument that this view is fabricated in the name of Shaykh Muhyi al-Din is what he himself mentioned in section 371 of the Futuhat, speaking of when the doors to the Hellfire shall close and how its inhabitants shall be like boiled pieces of flesh, and that its uppermost portion shall be its lowermost portion. Similarly, Imam al-Bajuri al-Shafi‘i said in his explanation of Jawhara al-Tawhid, “As for what is said regarding the supposed delight felt by the inhabitants of the Fire when they are tormented, and that if taken out they would experience pain, that is a fabrication against the Folk (the Sufis). How can this not be the case when the Exalted said, So taste (the punishment) and never will We increase you save in punishment. (Quran 78:30)”⁽¹²⁾ How can a Muslim believe in such a corrupt doctrine that opposes the doctrine of Ahl al-Sunna wa al-Jama‘a? This was stated by Shaykh Muhammad Yusuf al-Kafi. After mentioning the faction of Paradise and stating that they shall abide in Paradise forever experiencing delight, and after mentioning the faction of Hell, he said:

The faction of the Inferno shall abide therein forever and their pain of torment will be unceasing. Some have said that their punishment shall cease and that it shall be transformed into delight for them, so much that, if Paradise was presented to them, they would refuse it. Anyone who believes this is a disbeliever without doubt, for he has denied the words of Allah: Indeed those who rejected faith and died as disbelievers, upon them shall be the curse of Allah, the angels, and all of mankind. They shall abide therein eternally. The punishment shall not be lightened for them nor shall they be reprieved. (Quran 2:161–162) And he has denied: Indeed those who reject Our signs, We shall drive them into a Fire. Every time their skins are roasted through We shall replace them with other skins so they may taste the punishment. Indeed, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise. (Quran 4:56) There are similar verses proving that their punishment is continual.⁽¹³⁾

Another fabrication made against Shaykh Muhyi al-Din (Allah have mercy upon him) is the allegation that he believed legal responsibilities can be dropped. The erudite Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

Shaykh Muhyi al-Din mentioned that it is never permissible for a saint to go forward with an act of disobedience that he is inspired to do by supposed spiritual unveiling, just as it is not permissible for one to break his fast in Ramadan if he receives a spiritual premonition that he will fall ill that day. On the contrary, it is obligatory upon him to observe patience until he experiences the actual illness, for Allah Most High has only legislated breaking the fast when there is actual illness or other valid excuses. This is our position and the position of the realized ones among the people of Allah Most High.⁽¹⁴⁾

Another individual who faced fabrications is the great Gnostic, Shaykh Ibrahim al-Dasuqi (Allah have mercy upon him). He was alleged to have said, “My Lord gave me permission to speak and say ‘I am Allah.’ He said to me, ‘Say “I am Allah” without concern.’” This is too egregious and vile to deserve a long winded response.⁽¹⁵⁾ Rabi‘a al-‘Adawiyya (Allah have mercy upon her) was also alleged to have said about the Ka‘ba, “This idol that is worshipped on the earth.” Ibn Taymiyya denied the attribution of this statement to her and explained that it was a forgery and lie against her. When asked about this, he responded:

As for what is mentioned regarding the statement of Rabi‘a concerning the Sacred house being an idol worshipped on the earth, this is a lie against Rabi‘a, the pious believing woman. Whoever would have said this, no matter who they might be, would have been a disbeliever and repentance would be sought from them, and had they not repented they would have been killed. This is a lie. The Sacred House is not worshipped by the Muslims; rather, through circling around it and prayer towards it they worship the Lord of that House.⁽¹⁶⁾

If we, dear believer, were to go into detail concerning the varieties of tampering found in Islamic history and Sufism, this book could not be able to contain them all. For certain, Sufism’s share of tampering and forgery has been much more than the share found in other sciences, and that is because the forgers realized that Sufism is the spirit of Islam and that the Sufis are its powerful force and its source of illumination. They wanted to extinguish this light, but the Exalted said, They wish to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah will complete His light even if the disbelievers detest it. (Quran 61:8)

questionArabic: <div style='text-align: right; direction: rtl; padding: 20px 25px;'>
سؤال 111) كيف نستطيع تأويل كلام السادة الصوفية، ودفع ما ألصق بهم من تهم كخروجهم في كلامهم عن النصوص الشرعية التي أباحها الدين؟
الجواب: إن ما نراهُ في كُتب السَّادة الصوفيَّة من الأمورِ التي يخالفُ ظاهرَها نصوصُ الشريعةِ وأحكامِها، هي:
- إمَّا أن تكونَ مدسوسة ًعليهِم من قِبل الزنادقَة والحسدَة وأعداءِ الإسلام كَما بيَّنا.

- وإمَّا أن يكونَ كلاماً قابلاً للتأويل، تحدَّثوا بهِ من بابِ الإشارةِ أو الكنايةِ أو المَجاز، كَما نَرى ذلكَ في كثيرٍ من الكلامِ العرَبي، ونجدهُ بارزاً في كتابِ اللهِ تَعالى في مواطنَ عديدَة، كَما في قولِه تَعالى: ﴿وَأُشْرِبُوا فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْعِجْلَ بِكُفْرِهِمْ﴾⁽¹⁾ أي حُب العجل. وقولِه تعالى: ﴿وَاسأَلِ القَريَةَ ﴾⁽²⁾ أي أهلُ القريَة. وقولهِ عزَّ وجل: ﴿أَوَ مَن كَانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْنَاهُ﴾.⁽³⁾ أي كانَ ميَّت القلبِ، فأحيا اللهُ تَعالى قلبَه. وقولهِ تَعالى: ﴿لِتُخْرِجَ النَّاسَ مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ﴾⁽⁴⁾ أي من ظُلمات الكُفر إلى نورِ الإِيمان.

كَما نلاحظُ في بعضِ الآياتِ القرآنيةِ الكريمةِ تعارضاً في الظاهِر، ولكنَّا لَو تعمَّقنا في فهمِها، ودقّقنا في مدلولِها ومتعلِّقها، لوجَدناها قابلةً للتأويل، وبذلكَ لا نستطيعُ أن نقول: إنَّ في القرآنِ تعارضاً أو تصادماً. فمثلاً، يقولُ اللهُ تَعالى: ﴿إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ﴾⁽⁵⁾ ويقولُ في موطنٍ آخر: ﴿وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِي إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ﴾.⁽⁶⁾ فقَد يَرى من ليسَ عندهُ علمٌ في التفسيرَ أنَّ بين النصَّيْنِ تعارُضاً، لأن الأولَ ينفِي عن الرسولِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه الهِداية، والثاني يُثبت لهُ الهِداية، ولكنهُ لو سألَ أهلَ الذِّكر لأخبروهُ أنَّ الهدايةَ في الآيةِ الأولى بِمعنى خلْقِ الهِداية، وأما معناها في الآية الثانيةِ الدلالةُ والإرشاد. فَلا تعارضَ بين النصَّين عندَ أهلِ الفَهم.

وكذلكَ نجدُ أنَّ بعضَ الأحاديثِ النبويةِ الشريفةِ لا يصحُّ حملُها على ظاهرِها، بَل لابدَّ مِن تأويلِها على معانٍ تلائمُ باقي نصوصِ الشَّرع، وتطابقُ صريحَ القرآنِ الكَريم، وفي هذا المَعنى يقولُ الإمام "الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى: (وقد أجمعَ أهلُ الحقِّ على وجوبِ تأويلِ أحاديثِ الصِّفات، كحديث: (ينزِلُ ربُّنا تباركَ وتَعالى كلَّ ليلةٍ إلى سماءِ الدُّنيا حتَّى يَبقى ثلثُ الليلِ الآخَر، فيَقول: مَن يَدعوني فأستجيبَ له؟ من يسألُني فأعطيَه؟ من يَستغفرُني فأغفرَ له؟).⁽⁷⁾ وقد بلغَ بأحدِ الضالِّين أن يقول: (وكانَ على مِنبر، فنزلَ درجةً منهُ وقالَ للناس: ينزلُ ربكُم عن كُرسيهِ إلى السَّماء؛ كَنزولي عن مِنبري هذا، وهذا جهلٌ ليسَ فوقهُ جَهل).

ومِن جملةِ التأويلِ في الحَديث، تأويلُ حديث (إنَّ اللهَ خلقَ آدمَ على صورتِه) قال العلامة "ابن حجر الهيثمي" رحمه الله تعالى مؤولاً ذلك: (ويصحُّ أن يكونَ الضميرُ للهِ تَعالى كَما هوَ ظاهرُ السِّياق، وحينئذٍ يتعيَّن أن المرادَ بالصورةِ الصِّفة، أي أنَّ اللهَ تَعالى خلقَ آدمَ على أوصافِه، من العلمِ والقدرةِ وغيرِهما، ويؤيِّد هذا الحديثَ الصحيحَ عن أمِّ المؤمنينَ "عائشة" رضوان الله عليها: (كان صلوات الله وسلامه عليه خُلُقُه القرآن)⁽⁸⁾ وحديث (تخلَّقوا بأخلاقِ اللهِ تَعالى).

فالمطلوبُ من الكاملِ أن يطهِّر أخلاقهُ وأوصافهُ من كلِّ نَقص، ليحصُل لهُ نوعٌ من التأسِّي بأخلاقِ ربِّه، أي صفاتِه، وإلا فشتَّان ما بينَ أوصافِ القديمِ والحادِث. وبِهذا التقريرُ يُعلّم أنَّ هذا الحديثَ غايةُ المدحِ لآدم عليهِ السَّلام، حيثُ أوجدَ اللهُ فيهِ صفاتٍ كصفاتهِ تَعالى بالمَعنى الذي قرّرته.. إلى أن قال: (والحاصلُ أن الحديثَ إن أُعيد الضميرُ فيهِ إلى اللهِ تَعالى، وجبَ تأويلهُ على ما هوَ المعروفُ من مذهبِ الخَلف الذي هو أحكمُ وأعلمُ، خلافاً لفرقةٍ ضلُّوا عن الحَق، وارتكَبوا عظائمَ من الجهةِ والتجسيمِ اللذيْن هُما كفرٌ عندَ كثيرٍ من العُلماء.. أعاذَنا اللهُ من ذلكَ بمنِّه وكرمِه).⁽⁹⁾

فإذا كانَ كلامُ سيدِ المُرسلين صلوات الله وسلامه عليه وَقد أوتيَ الفصاحةَ والبلاغةَ ووضوحَ اللفظِ وإشراقِ التعبيرِ وجوامعَ الكلِم، قد احتاجَ في بعضِ الأحيانِ إلى التأويل، بحملِ معانيهِ على غيرِ ما يفيدهُ ظاهرُ لفظِه، فإنَّ كلامَ غيرهِ من أمَّته ممَّن لم يصل شأوهُ في البيانِ والفصاحةِ قابلٌ للتأويلِ محتملٌ للتفسيرِ من بابٍ أولى.

ومن ناحيةٍ أخرى، فإنَّ لكلِّ فنٍّ من الفنونِ أو علمٍ من العُلوم، كالفقهِ والحديثِ والمنطقِ والنحوِ والهندسةِ والجبرِ والفلسفةِ اصطلاحاتٍ خاصةٍ به، لا يعلمُها إلا أربابُ ذلكَ العِلم، فهَل يفهمُ الطبيبُ اصطلاحَ المهندِس؟ أو يفهمَ المهندسُ اصطلاحَ الطبيبِ حينَ يُعبر كلٌّ منهُما عن آلاتهِ ومسمَّيات فنِّه؟ ومن قرأَ كتبَ علمٍ من العلومِ دونَ أن يعرفَ اصطلاحاتِه، أو يطَّلع على رموزهِ وإشاراتِه، فإنه يؤولُ الكلامَ تأويلاتٍ شتَّى مُغايرة لما يقصدُها العُلماء، ومناقِضة لما يريدهُ الكاتِبون، فيتيهُ ويضِل.

وللصوفيَّة اصطلاحاتهِم التي قامَت بعضُ الشيءِ مقامَ العبارةِ في تصويرِ مدركاتهِم ومواجيدهِم، حينَ عجزَت اللغةُ عَن ذلك، فلا بدَّ لِمن يريدُ الفهمَ عنهُم مِن صحبتهِم حتَّى تتَّضح لهُ عباراتهِم، ويتعرّف على إشاراتهِم ومصطلحاتهِم، فيستبيَّن لهُ أنهُم لم يخرُجوا عَن الكتابِ والسنَّة، ولم ينحرِفوا عن الشَّريعةِ الغرَّاء، وأنَّهم هُم الفاهِمون لروحِها، الواقِفون على حقيقَتها، الحارِسون لتراثِها.

قالَ بعضُ العارفين: (نحن قومٌ يُحرم النظرُ في كتبِنا عَلى مَن لَم يكُن اطَّلع عَليها ممَّن ليسَ من أهلِها جهلَها ثمَّ عاداها). لأن الإنسانَ عدوٌّ لما جهِل، ولذلكَ قال السيد "علي بن وفا" رحمه الله تعالى: (إنَّ من دَوَّن المعارفَ والأسرار، لمْ يدوِّنها للجُمهور، بَل لو رَأى مَن يُطالع فيها ممَّن ليسَ هُو بأهلِها لنهاهُ عَنها).

وتوضيحاً للموضوع نقول: إنَّ كلامَ السادةِ الصوفيةِ في تحذيرِ من لا يفهمُ كلامهُم ولا يُعرف اصطلاحاتهِم من قراءةِ هذهِ الكُتب ليس من قُبيل كَتم الِعلم، ولكن خوفاً مِن أن يَفهم الناسُ من كتبهِم غيرَ ما يقصِدون، وخشيةَ أن يؤولوا كلامهُم على غيرِ حقيقتِه، فيقَعوا في الإنكارِ والاعتِراض، شأنُ من يجهلُ علماً من العُلوم.. لأنَّ المطلوبَ من المؤمنِ أن يخاطبَ الناسَ بما يناسبهُم من الكلامِ وما يتفقُ مع مستواهُم في العلمِ والفهمِ والاستِعداد، ولهذا أفردَ "البخاري" في صحيحهِ باباً في ذلك فَقال: (بابُ مَن خصَّ بالعلمِ قوماً دونَ قومٍ كراهيةَ أن لا يَفهموا.. وقالَ "علي" رضوان الله عنه: (حدَّثوا الناسَ بما يَعرفون، أتحبون أن يُكذَّبَ اللهُ ورسولهُ؟).

قال العلامة "العيني" رحمه الله تعالى في شرحهِ لهذا الحَديث: (تركَ بعضُ الناسِ من التخصيصِ بالعلمِ لقصورِ فهمِهم، والمرادُ كلِّموهم على قدرِ عقولهِم، وفي كتابِ العلمِ لـ "آدم بن إياس" عن "عبد الله بن داود" عن معروفٍ في آخره: (ودَعوا ما يُنكرون).. أي عَلى ما يشتبهُ عليهِم فهمُه، وفيهِ دليلٌ على أن المتشابهَ لا ينبَغي أن يُذكَر عند العامَّة، ومثلهُ قولُ "ابن مسعود" رضوان الله عنه، ذكرهُ "مسلم" في مقدمةِ كتابه بسندٍ صَحيح قال: (ما أنتَ بمحدثٍ قوماً حديثاً لا تبلَغهُ عقولُهم إلاَّ كانَ لبعضهِم فِتنة).. لأنَّ الشخصَ إذا سمعَ ما لا يفهمُه، وما لا يتصوَّر إمكانهُ يعتقدُ استحالتهُ جهلاً، فلا يصدِّق وجودَه، فإذا أُسنِدَ إلى اللهِ ورسولهِ يلزمُ تكذيبَهُما)..

وقال الشيخ "أحمد زروق" في قواعده: (في كلِّ علمٍ ما يخصُّ وما يعًم، فليسَ التصوفُ بأولى مِن غيرهِ في عمومهِ وخصوصهِ، بل يلزمُ بذلَ أحكامِ الله المتعلقةِ بالمعامَلات من كلٍ عموماً، وما وراءَ ذلك على حسبِ ما قابلهُ لا قدَّر قائِله، الحديث: (حدِّثوا الناسَ بما يَعرفون، أتحبونَ أن يُكذَّب اللهُ ورسولَه؟).⁽¹⁰⁾

وقيل لـ "الجنيد" رحمه الله تعالى: (يسألكَ الرجلانِ عن المسألةِ الواحدَة، فتجيبُ هذا بخلافِ ما تجيبُ هذا؟ فقال: الجوابُ على قدرِ السائِل. قالَ عليهِ السَّلام: (أُمِرنا أن نكلِّم الناسَ على قدرِ عقولهِم).⁽¹¹⁾

ولهذا ذكرَ الشيخ "محي الدين بن عربي" رحمهُ الله تعالى في البابِ الرابعِ والخَمسين من "الفتوحات" ما نصُّه: (اعلَم أن أهلَ اللهِ لم يضَعوا الإشاراتِ التي اصطَلحوا عَليها فيما بينَهم لأنفسهِم، فإنهُم يعلمونَ الحقَّ الصريحَ في ذلك، وإنَّما وضعوها منعاً للدخيلِ بينهُم، حتى لا يعرفَ ما هُم فيه، شفقةً عليهِ أن يسمعَ شيئاً لم يصِل إليهِ فينكرهُ على أهلِ الله، فيُعاقَبُ بحرمانِه، فلا ينالهُ بعدَ ذلكَ أبداً، قال: ومن أعجبِ الأشياءِ في هذهِ الطَّريق، بل لا يوجدُ إلا فيها، أنهُ ما من طائفةٍ تحملُ علماً من المنطقيِّين والنُّحاة وأهلِ الهندسةِ والحسابِ والمتكلمينَ والفلاسفَة، إلا ولهُم اصطلاحٌ لا يعلمهُ الدَّخيل فيهِم إلا بتوقيفٍ منهُم، لابدَّ من ذلك، إلا أهلَ هذهِ الطريقةِ خاصَّة، فإن المريدَ الصادقَ إذا دخلَ طريقهُم، وما عندهُ خبرٌ بما اصطلَحوا عَليه، وجلسَ معهُم، وسمِع منهُم ما يتكلَّمون به من الإشارات، فَهِمَ جميعَ ما تكلَّموا به، حتَّى كأنَّه الواضعُ لذلكَ الاصطِلاح، ويشاركهُم في الخوضِ في ذلكَ العِلم، ولا يستغرِب هو ذلكَ من نفسِه، بل يجدُ علمَ ذلكَ ضرورياً لا يقدرُ على دفعِه، فكأنهُ ما زالَ يعلمُه، ولا يدري كيفَ يحصلُ لهُ ذلك، هذا شأنُ المريدِ الصَّادق، وأما الكاذِب فلا يعرفُ ما يَسمع، ولا يدري ما يَقرأ، ولم يزَل علماءُ الظاهرِ في كلِّ عصرٍ يتُوقونَ في فهمِ كلامِ القوم، وناهيكَ بـ "الإمام أحمد بن سريج" حضرَ يوماً مجلِس "الجنيد" فقيل له: ما فهمتَ مِن كلامِه؟ فقال: لا أدري ما يَقول؟ ولكِن أجدُ لكلامهِ صولةً في القَلب ظاهِرة، تدلُّ على عملٍ في الباطنِ وإخلاصٍ في الضَّمير، وليسَ كلامهُ كلامٌ مُبطل.. ثم إنَّ القومَ لا يتكلمونَ بالإشارةِ إلا عندَ حضورِ من ليسَ مِنهم، أو في تأليفهِم لا غَير.. ثم قال: (ولا يَخفى أن أصلَ الإنكارِ من الأعداءِ المبطلينَ إنما ينشأُ مِن الحسَد، ولَو أنَّ أولئكِ المنكِرين تَركوا الحسَد وسلَكوا طريقَ أهلِ الله، لَم يظهَر منهُم إنكارٌ ولا حسَد، وازدادوا علماً إلى علمهِم. ولكِن هكذا كانَ الأمر، ولا حولَ ولا قوَّة إِلا بالله).⁽¹²⁾

وقال الشيخ "عبد الكريم الجيلي" رحمه الله تعالى في كتابه "الإنسان الكامل": (ثمَّ ّألتمسُ من الناظرِ في هذا الكِتاب، إلا وهوَ مؤيَّد بكتابِ اللهِ وسنةِ رسولهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه، وأنهُ إذا لاحَ لهُ شيءٌ من كلامهِ بخلافِ الكتابِ والسنَّة، فليعلَم أنَّ ذلكَ من حيثُ مفهومِه، لا مِن حيثُ مُرادي الذي وضعتُ الكَلام لأجلِه، فليتوقَّف عن العملِ به مع التَّسليم، إلى أن يفتحَ الله عليهِ بمعرفتِه، ويحصلَ لهُ شاهدٌ من ذلكَ من كتابِ اللهِ وسنَّة نبيهِ صلوات الله وسلامه عليه.. إلى أن قال: (واعلَم أن كلَّ علمٍ لا يؤيدهُ الكتابُ والسنَّة فهوَ ضلالَة، لا لأجلِ ما لا تجدُ أنتَ له ما يؤيدُه، فقَد يكونُ العلمُ في نفسهِ مؤيَّداً بالكتابِ والسنَّة، ولكن قلَّة استعدادكَ منعكَ من فهمِه، فلم تستطِع أن تتناولهُ بيدكَ من محلِّه، فتظنُّ أنه غيرَ مؤيدٍ بالكتابِ والسنَّة، والطريقُ في هذا التسليمُ).
ويتبيَّن لنا من هذهِ النصوصِ التي نقَلناها عن الفُقهاء الأعلامِ والسادةِ الصوفيَّة أمورٌ أهمُّها:
أ- أنهُ لا يصحُّ لغيرِ السالكِ في طريقِ الصوفيَّة، أن يطالعَ كتبهِم، خشيةَ أن يفهمَها على غيرِ حقيقتِها، وخلافَ ما يريدهُ مؤلفوها، لأنهُ بعيدٌ عن فهمِ اصطلاحاتهِم، ومعرفةِ إشاراتهِم.
غيرَ أن كُتب السادةِ الصوفيةِ تنقسمُ إلى ثلاثةِ أقسام:
1- القسم الأول: يبحثُ عن تصحيحِ العبادات، وحُسن إقامتِها بصورتِها وروحِها، من الخشوعِ والحضورِ فيها معَ اللهِ تَعالى، مع مراعاةِ آدابِها الظاهِرة.

2- القسم الثاني: يبحثُ في مجاهدةِ النفسِ وتزكيتِها، والقلبِ وأحوالِه، من تخليتهِ عن صفاتهِ الناقصةِ كالشكوكِ والوساوسِ والرياءِ والحقدِ والغلِّ والسُّمعة والجاهِ والحسدِ وغيرِها من الصفاتِ المذمومَة، وتحليتهِ بالصفاتِ الكاملةِ كالتوبةِ والتوكُّل والرِّضا والتَّسليم والمحبةِ والإِخلاصِ والصدقِ والخشوعِ والمراقبةِ وغيرِها من الصفاتِ الحسنَة.

3- القسم الثالث: يبحثُ عن المعارفِ الربانيَّة والعلومِ الوهبيةِ والأذواقِ الوجدانيةِ والحقائقِ الكشفيَّة، ومعظمُ كتبِ الشيخ "محي الدين ابن عربي" رحمه الله تعالى من هذا القِسم، كـ "الفتوحاتِ المكية" و"الفصوص" وكذلكَ كتاب "الإنسان الكامل" للشيخ "عبد الكريم الجيلي" رحمه الله تعالى.. وعلى أمثالِ هذه الكُتب ينصبُّ التحذيرُ من قراءتِها لغيرِ السالكينَ العارفينَ من الصوفيَّة.. وتُسمى هذه العُلوم: "علومَ المكاشَفة".

ب - أن التصوُّف لا يُنال بقراءةِ الكُتب ولا بمعرفةِ الاصطِلاحات، بل لابدَّ من السلوكِ معَ رجالهِ ومجالسةِ أهلِه. قال الشيخ "الشعراني" رحمه الله تعالى: (سمعتُ سيدي "علياً الخواص" رضوانُ اللهِ عنهُ يقول: إياكَ أن تعتقدَ يا أخي إذا طالعتَ كُتب القَوم، وعرفتَ مُصطلحهُم في ألفاظهِم أنكَ صِرت صوفياً، إنما التصوُّف التخلُّق بأخلاقهِم، ومعرفةِ طرقِ استنباطهِم لجميعِ الآدابِ والأخلاق التي تحلَّوا بها من الكِتاب والسُّنة).⁽¹³⁾
ج - أنَّ السادةَ الصوفيَّة إنما وَضعوا هذه الرموزَ والإشاراتِ كي لا يأخُذ علمَهم إلا مَنْ سارَ في طريقهِم، وقد بيّنا أن التصوُّف لا يُنال بقراءةِ الأوراق، بَل بصحبةِ أهلِ الأذواق.
د - أن النصوصَ التي فيها الكُفر والزِّيغ والمُرُوق من الدِّين مدسوسةٌ على القومِ حتماً، لما رأيتُ من تمسُّكهم بالكتابِ والسُّنة مما مرَّ معكَ من نُقُول.

هـ - أن ما ثبتَ عنهُم بالتأكيد، ويُمكن تأويلهُ وحملهُ على وجهٍ صحيحٍ من عقيدةِ أهلِ الحَق، أهلِ السنَّة والجماعَة، وجبَ تأويلهُ عَليها، لأنَّها هي عقيدتَهم التي يعتقدونَها ويصرِّحون بها، ويثبتونَها دائماً في مقدماتِ كُتبهم كما هي سُنتهم، وانظُر إن شئتَ مقدمةَ "الرسالة القشيرية" و"الفتوحات المكية" و"التعرُّف لمذهبِ أهلِ التصوُّف" و"إحياء علوم الدين" وغيرِها من الكُتب.

و - أن ما نُسب إليهم مما لا يمكنُ تأويلهُ على وجهٍ صَحيح، إن صحَّ عنهُم فهو مردودٌ على صاحبِه، لا نسلِّمه له ولا نعتقِده، بل نقولُ بكفرٍ مُعْتَقَدِه، ولكنَّا لا نكفِّر شخصاً معيناً، لأنَّا لا نَدري خاتمَته، ولأنَّنا مسؤولونَ أولاً وآخراً عن عقيدةِ أهلِ الحّق، أهلِ السُّنة والجماعَة، لا عن عقيدةِ أيِّ إنسانٍ آخر.

questionEnglish: <div style='text-align: left; direction: ltr; padding: 20px 25px;'>

Question 111: How can we interpret the words of the esteemed Sufis and defend them against allegations, such as the notion that their words contravene the Sacred Law or forbid what has been permitted in the religion?

Answer: The content we see in the books of the Sufis whose purported meanings go against the revealed texts and rulings within the Sacred Law are either fabrications against them penned by heretics, people of envy, and enemies of Islam as we explained earlier, or they are words that accept interpretation and were spoken by way of spiritual allusion, metaphor, or metonymy, as is found abundantly in the Arabic language and what we find in abundance in the Book of Allah. We find this, for instance, in His words, They absorbed the calf in their hearts. (Quran 2:93). This phrase means “love for the calf”. In another example, Allah said, Ask the village. (Quran 12:82) This phrase means “ask the inhabitants of the village”. In another example, Allah said, Is the one who was dead who We then brought to life… (Quran 6:122). This means “one whose heart was dead, and was brought to life by Allah Most High”. In another example, Allah said, So We can take the people out of darkness into the light. (Quran 14:1) This means “from the darkness of disbelief into the light of faith”.

Similarly, we sometimes find what appear to be contradictions in the wordings used in certain Quranic verses, but if we look deeply in an attempt to understand them and go into detail and analyze their meanings and connections with other verses, we will see that they accept sound interpretation. As such, we are unable to say that the Quran contains contradictions. Take for example the words of the Exalted, Verily you cannot guide whom you love. (Quran 28:56) In another verse, however, He said, And indeed you certainly guide unto the straight path. (Quran 42:52) Now, someone who has no knowledge of Quranic exegesis might think that there is mutual contradiction between these two verses, because in the first verse guidance is negated from the Messenger , and in the second verse it is affirmed. If only that person were to ask the people of the reminder (ahl al-dhikr), they would have informed him that the guidance mentioned in the first verse refers to creating guidance, and in the second verse, guidance means showing the way and imparting instruction. Hence, according to the people of understanding there is no contradiction between the two verses.

We see likewise in some of the Prophet’s noble hadith narrations that it is not correct to understand them in their outwardly apparent expression. Instead, they must be interpreted in a way that is in harmony with rest of the Divinely revealed texts and in total congruence with the explicit meanings of the Holy Quran. Speaking in this regard, Imam al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

The people of the truth concur that it is obligatory to interpret the hadith narrations that pertain to the Divine Attributes, such as the Hadith, “Our Lord, the Blessed and Exalted, descends each night to the lowest heavens of the world until the last third of the night. He says, ‘Who calls upon Me so I may respond to him? Who asks Me so I may give him? Who seeks My forgiveness so I may forgive him?’”⁽¹⁾ One misguided person had the audacity to say while on the pulpit and descending down one step, “Your Lord descends from His Footstool to the heavens just as I have descended from this pulpit here.” This is ignorance beyond which there is no ignorance.⁽²⁾

Another example of interpretation given to hadith reports is the interpretation given to the Hadith, “Indeed, Allah created Adam in his image.”⁽³⁾ The erudite Ibn Hajar al-Haytami (Allah have mercy upon him) interpreted this:

The upshot is that if the pronoun in this Hadith refers to Allah, it is obligatory to interpret it according to the well-known way of the latter generations (khalaf), that is both wiser and more precise, as opposed to a faction that has been misled away from the truth, who have uttered gross enormities such as affirming a direction (jiha) [for Allah], and affirming corporeality (tajsim), both of which are considered disbelief by many scholars—may Allah, out of His generosity and munificence grant us refuge from that!⁽⁴⁾

If the words of the chief of the Messengers  who was given complete eloquence in rhetoric, word-choice, expressions and succinct, compendious speech—were occasionally in need of being interpreted and having their meanings explained in a way that is different from what the outwardly apparent expressions suggest, then even more so can the words of others from his Umma who have not reached his level of eloquence and clarity accept interpretation and explanation.

In addition, every branch of knowledge or science, such as the sciences of jurisprudence, hadith, logic, grammar, engineering, mathematics, and philosophy, has its own special technical nomenclature that only the purveyors of those sciences know. Does a physician, for example, understand the nomenclature of the engineer, or does an engineer understand the terminology of the physician when the both of them speak of the tools and terms of their trade?

Whoever reads the books of a particular science without knowing its technical nomenclature or becoming familiar with its allusions and specially designated terms will inevitably interpret the words in ways that are completely at odds with the intent of its scholars and the meanings of the authors, and such a person will become lost and misled.

The Sufis have their own technical nomenclature that is based, to a certain extent, upon symbolic expressions that capture their comprehensions and ecstatic experiences, since mere language is inadequate for that. For this reason, anyone who wishes to understand things from them must keep their company until their expressions become clear and he becomes familiar with their allusions and technical nomenclature, thereby gaining certainty that they have not parted ways with the Book and the Sunna or deviated from the noble Sacred Law, and that, on the contrary, they are the ones who understand its spirit, who are based upon its realities, and who vigilantly guard its heritage.

One of the Gnostics said, “We are a Folk whose books are forbidden to those who are not upon our path.”⁽⁵⁾ That is because the purpose of recording those sciences is to have them reach their proper recipients. One who is not from them yet peruses their works will be ignorant of their contents and will, as a result, despise them, for man is an enemy to what he is ignorant of. For this reason, the Sayyid, ‘Ali b. Wafa’ (Allah have mercy upon him) said, “The one who recorded these mystical sciences and secrets did not record them for the general public, for had he seen his works being read by those who are not from its people, he would have forbidden them from that.”⁽⁶⁾

To clarify this point: the warning delivered by the esteemed Sufis regarding those who do not understand their words or the technical nomenclature found in their books is not the same as concealing knowledge; rather, it was out of fear lest people read their works and misunderstand their intent, and out of fear that their words might be misinterpreted, thereby leading people to rejection and turning away—which is the state of someone who is ignorant of a particular science. What is sought from the believer is that he address people in words that are both appropriate for them and suitable to their level of knowledge and understanding. Regarding this, Imam al-Bukhari singled out a chapter in his Sahih, “Chapter: Regarding One Who Chose Some People with Knowledge and Not Others Lest they Misunderstand. ‘Ali  said, ‘Speak to the people according to their level of knowledge; would you like them to belie Allah and His Messenger?’”⁽⁷⁾
The erudite al-‘Ayni commented on this narration:

Some people were not given knowledge due to their limited understanding. This means that one must speak to people according to their intellectual capacity. There is a report found in the Book of Knowledge of Adam b. Iyas, on the authority of ‘Abdullah b. Dawud, on the authority of Ma‘ruf …: “And abstain from [transmitting] what they will deny.” This means one should abstain from relating things that will confuse them. This shows that it is inappropriate to mention the allegorical (mutashabih) [verses of Quran or hadith reports] to the common folk. This is similar to statement of Ibn Mas‘ud, “Never will you speak to a people regarding something they cannot comprehend except that it will be a tribulation for some of them.” This is because when a person hears something he does not understand, or something that he cannot conceive as possible, he ignorantly believes that it is rationally impossible and therefore denies its existence—so if he does not understand is linked to Allah or His Messenger, it implies his denial of them both.⁽⁸⁾
Shaykh Ahmad Zarruq (Allah have mercy upon him) said in his principles:

Every branch of knowledge contains things that are general and specific. Sufism is no different from the other sciences in this regard. It contains things that are general and things that are specific. As such, is incumbent that one strive to implement the laws of Allah in every generality that is related to interactions. As for what lies beyond that [of specialized knowledge], it must be commensurate with one’s receptivity and not the rank of the one who says it. This is due to the hadith, “Speak to the people according to their level of knowledge; would you like them to belie Allah and His Messenger?”

Junayd (Allah have mercy upon him) was once asked, “Why is it that sometimes two people come to you with the same question, yet you give each of them different answers?” Junayd replied, “The answer is according to the capacity of the questioner. The Prophet  said, ‘We have been ordered to speak to the people according to their level of understanding.’”⁽⁹⁾
Shaykh Muhyi al-Din Ibn ‘Arabi (Allah have mercy upon him) said in section fifty-four of his Futuhat:

Know that the people of Allah did not come up with the allusions used among themselves for their own ends, for they know the explicit truth contained in the allusions already. They only came up with them to prevent the uninitiated novice from entering among them so he would not know what they are speaking of—out of mercy for him lest he hear something he doesn’t understand, leading him to rebuke the people of Allah and subsequently fall into the punishment of being cut off from the spiritual path and never reaching it...

One of the most amazing things of this spiritual path—in fact, it is not found in any other path—is that there is no group besides the Sufis that carries a branch of knowledge, be they the logicians, grammarians, engineers, mathematicians, theologians, or philosophers, except that they have a technical nomenclature that is unknown to the uninitiated novice unless they inform him of the meanings (which is a must), but with regards to the spiritual path, when a truthful spiritual seeker adopts it, he has no knowledge of the technical nomenclature they developed yet after he sits with them he hears the allusions they speak of and understands it all; it is as if he was the one to develop the nomenclature himself! He is able to discuss this science with them. It comes as no surprise to him; rather, he finds it as self-evident knowledge he is unable to do away with. It is as if he always knew it, yet he doesn’t know how he acquired it.

That is the state of the truthful spiritual wayfarer. As for the liar, he doesn’t know what he hears and doesn’t understands what he reads. The scholars of the outward in every era have been continually wary in their understanding of the words of the folk. Suffice with the example of Imam Ahmad b. Surayj. Once, he attended a gathering of Junayd and he was asked, “What have you understood of his words?” He replied, “I don’t know what he is saying; however, I find in his words an obvious force that affects the heart. It indicates inner action and sincerity of self. It is not the words of a heretic.”

Furthermore, the Folk do not speak with symbolic allusion unless outsiders are in attendance or it is in their written works and not elsewhere… It is not hidden that the basis for the enemy’s rebuke and censure stems from nothing but envy. If only those deniers were to abandon their envy and traverse the path of Allah’s Folk, there would be no censure or envy from them. They would have had an increase in knowledge over their present knowledge. At any rate, this is how things are, and there is neither movement nor power except by Allah, the Most Exalted and Tremendous!⁽¹¹⁾
Shaykh ‘Abd al-Karim al-Jili (Allah have mercy upon him) said in his book al-Insan al-Kamil:

Furthermore, I bring to the reader’s attention—after informing him that I have not placed anything in this book except that it is aided by the Book of Allah and the Sunna of His Messenger —that if he notices anything in its contents that contravene the Book and the Sunna, he should know that is (in opposition to the two) only as he understands it and it was not my intent for which I placed these words. Let him refrain from acting in accordance with that while giving concession until Allah opens up for him a sound knowledge of it and he obtains an evidence from the Book of Allah and the Sunna of His Messenger . Know that every knowledge unaided by the Book and the Sunna is misguidance— not that for which you cannot find an aid, as certain knowledge could in fact be aided by the Book and the Sunna but your lack of readiness prevents you from understanding; you are unable to take it with your hand from its proper place, and therefore suppose that it is not aided by the book and the Sunna. The proper course of action in that situation is concession.
From these quotes we have cited from the notable jurists and esteemed Sufis, certain matters become clear.

1. It is inappropriate for one who is not travelling the spiritual path of the Sufis to peruse their books, as it is feared that because such a person is far removed from their technical nomenclature and symbolic allusions, he will misunderstand their real meanings and their authors’ intent.

Having said that, the books of the Sufis are generally divided into three categories. The first category are works that look into correcting the rites of worship and carrying them out with both their outer form and spirit, humility, presence of heart with Allah and observation of their proper outward etiquette. The second category are works that look into purifying the soul, engaging in spiritual struggle, and cleansing the heart and its states, removing its deficient characteristics such as, doubts, misgivings, showing off, malice, rancor, seeking to be heard, seeking prestige, envy and other blameworthy traits, and adorning it with complete characteristics such as repentance, reliance, satisfaction with the Divine, submission, love, sincerity, truthfulness, humility, vigilance and other praiseworthy traits. (These two categories are mentioned in the Ihya’ of Imam al-Ghazali, Qut al-Qulub of Abu Talib al-Makki, and similar works. These sciences are called the sciences of interaction (‘Ulum al-Mu‘amala).) The third category are works that look into the Lordly mystic sciences, Divinely vouchsafed knowledge, ecstatic experiences, and unveiled realities. Most of the works of Shaykh Muhyi al-Din Ibn ‘Arabi fall in this category, such as the al-Futuhat al-Makkiyya and the Fusus, as well as al-Insan al-Kamil of Shaykh ‘Abd al-Karim al-Jili (Allah have mercy upon him). These are the works warned against for those who are not Gnostics treading the spiritual path. These sciences are called the sciences of spiritual unveiling (‘Ulum al-Mukashafa).

2. Sufism is not obtained by merely reading books or by knowing its technical nomenclature. Rather, there must be spiritual wayfaring with its masters and sitting with its people. Shaykh al-Sha‘rani (Allah have mercy upon him) said:

I heard my master ‘Ali al-Khawwas  say, “Beware, dear brother, from believing that you have become a Sufi by merely perusing the books of the Folk and knowing their technical nomenclature. Rather, Sufism is nothing more than adopting their qualities of character and knowing the how they derived their etiquette and character from the Book and the Sunna.”⁽¹²⁾

3. The reason the esteemed Sufis mentioned their allusions and symbolic words was so that no one could take their knowledge except by travelling their path. We have already explained that Sufism is not obtained by reading papers, and that rather it is by keeping the company of the people of ecstatic experience.

4. The passages containing disbelief, deviation, or heresy outside of the folds of the religion are clearly tampering done against the Folk, for you have seen how they were steadfast in holding to the Book and the Sunna.

5. What is affirmed to be from their words and can be interpreted and explained with a sound meaning in accordance with the doctrine of the people of truth, Ahl al-Sunna wal-Jama‘a, then it must be interpreted in that light, for it is their creed which they openly acknowledged and customarily placed in the introductions to their works. For an example of this, see al-Risala al-Qushayriyya, al-Futuhat al-Makkiyya, al-Ta‘arruf li-Madhhab Ahl al-Tasawwuf, Ihya’ ‘Ulum al-Din, and other works.

6. What is correctly attributed to them, but which cannot be given a sound interpretation, is rejected. We do not agree with those who say it nor do we believe it. Rather, we say that anyone who believes such words has fallen into disbelief. Nevertheless, we do not anathematize (takfir) a particular individual, since we know not how he died. First and foremost, we are responsible for having the belief of the people of truth, Ahl al-Sunna wa al-Jama‘a, and we are not responsible for having the belief of a specific individual.
